title: Machine Learning-Assisted THz Technology for Nondestructive Testing in the Tobacco Industry_supp1-3685500.docx
authors: Xiaojun Wu
publish: N/A
abstract: Abnormal moisture content and impurity contamination are core threats to product quality in the tobacco industry. Rapid, accurate, and non-destructive detection is therefore essential for product optimization. Terahertz time-domain spectroscopy (THz-TDS) holds great potential for tobacco detection owing to its non-invasiveness and high penetration capability. However, the effect of laser pulse width on THz radiation characteristics remains to be verified and the challenge of rapid signal analysis remains unsolved. In this work, a free-space THz-TDS system was constructed, which is driven by two compact Ti:sapphire lasers (with pulse widths of 20 fs and 45 fs, respectively) and enables rapid switching of laser sources. Experimental results indicate that the THz wave generated by the 20 fs laser achieves a broader bandwidth (2.5 THz) and a higher center frequency (0.45 THz). Further analysis reveals a strong linear correlation between the THz signal amplitude and the moisture content of tobacco leaves, while distinct waveform differences are observed between tobacco leaves and impurities. By integrating machine learning algorithms, high-precision impurity classification (with an accuracy of 99.9%) and rapid moisture prediction (with a mean absolute error of 0.28%) are realized. The entire signal measurement and analysis process can be completed within 2 seconds. This work provides technical verification for the intelligent upgrading of tobacco detection systems and demonstrates the enormous potential of THz technology in the tobacco industry and even the broader agricultural sector.

title: Dual-timescale Joint Optimization for Dynamic Edge Service Deployment and Task Scheduling in Space-air-ground Integrated Networks_supp1-3685292.pdf
authors: Wenfeng Li
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper investigates dual-timescale joint optimization for dynamic edge service deployment and task scheduling in space-air-ground integrated networks (SAGINs) with mobile edge computing (MEC). We decompose the original problem into a long-term (LT) service deployment subproblem and a short-term (ST) task scheduling subproblem to address the timescale disparity and coupling constraints. For the LT subproblem, an improved whale optimization algorithm (IWOA) is developed with a cosine-based nonlinear convergence factor, vertical-horizontal crossover, and elite opposition-based learning to enhance exploration and convergence. For the ST subproblem, we model task scheduling as a decentralized partially observable Markov decision process (Dec-POMDP) and adopt multi-agent proximal policy optimization (MAPPO) with KL regularization and clipping for stable policy updates. A hierarchical alternating framework (with a self-attention module for traffic-feature extraction) is designed to coordinate the two timescales iteratively. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with the baseline WOA-PPO algorithm, the proposed method increases service deployment benefits by 27.2% and reduces task scheduling costs by 38.5%.

title: Semantic-Aware Behavior Optimization With Safety Reinforcement Feedback for Language-Conditioned Manipulation_supp1-3685452.mp4
authors: Hongpeng Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Language-conditioned manipulation (LCM) couples vision, language, and control to enable natural instruction following, making it a promising direction in embodied AI research. Researchers have combined imitation learning (IL) with reinforcement learning (RL), where IL provides sample-efficient initialization and RL refines the policy through feedback to maximize long-term returns. Despite progress, safe LCM execution faces two challenges: (1) a modality gap that disrupts semantic-spatial correspondence, degrading action quality, and (2) sparse safety costs in high-dimensional states that make constraint estimation and semantic–safety alignment difficult. In this paper, we present Semantic-Aware Behavior Optimization with Safety Reinforcement Feedback, a novel framework that mitigates multimodal misalignment through context-aware initialization and enforces semantic–safety consistency during execution. We introduce spatial–semantic priors to align language goals with spatial observations and embed safety cues. Specifically, a goal prior aligns modalities for context-aware initialization during behavioral cloning, while a constraint prior combined with a feasibility metric provides learnable supervision for safety reinforcement fine-tuning. Evaluations show our framework achieves a 6.7% relative improvement in simulation and effectively reduces safety violations in real-world tasks across diverse scenarios.

title: Attention2Probability: Attention-Driven Terminology Probability Estimation for Robust Speech-to-text System
authors: Yangfan Du, Jun Zhang, Bin Wang, Jin Qiu, Lu Huang, Yuan Ge, Xiaoqian Liu, Tong Xiao, Jingbo Zhu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Recent advances in speech large language models (SLMs) have improved speech recognition and translation in general domains, but accurately generating domain-specific terms or neologisms remains challenging. To address this, we propose Attention2Probability: attention-driven terminology probability estimation for robust speech-to-text system, which is lightweight, flexible, and accurate. Attention2Probability converts cross-attention weights between speech and terminology into presence probabilities, and it further employs curriculum learning to enhance retrieval accuracy. Furthermore, to tackle the lack of data for speech-to-text tasks with terminology intervention, we create and release a new speech dataset with terminology to support future research in this area. Experimental results show that Attention2Probability significantly outperforms the VectorDB method on our test set. Specifically, its maximum recall rates reach 92.57% for Chinese and 86.83% for English. Intervening in SLMs’ recognition and translation tasks using Attention2Probability-retrieved terms improves terminology accuracy by 6–17%, while revealing that the current utilization of terminology by SLMs has limitations.

title: PEAG-Det: A Dual-Stream Phase- and Edge-Aware Framework with Adaptive Gating for Cross-Phase Tumor Detection
authors: Zhiqiang Yang, Qiu Guan, Xueliang Zhang, Chenyan Wu, Xinli Xu, Huafeng Liu, Ying Tang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Early tumor detection in CT is essential for improving patient outcomes, but current detectors struggle with subtle pathological features and poor use of multi-phase imaging, creating a gap with radiological workflows. This challenge is particularly pronounced in pancreatic cystic neoplasms (PCN), where benign and malignant lesions are easily confused and multi-phase analysis is often required. Motivated by this, we propose PEAG-Det, a dual-stream framework that leverages prior knowledge to learn temporal-phase feature importance through primary and auxiliary branches, integrating three core innovations. First, the Re-parameterized Heterogeneous Edge-Aware (RepHEA) module employs multi-scale axial convolutions and large-kernel attention to enhance sensitivity to tumor boundary gradients and calcification structures. Second, the Phase-Aware Cross-Attention Fusion (PCAF) module enables implicit registration and robust cross-phase interaction to enhance complementary feature integration between non-contrast and contrast-enhanced CT. Finally, the Dual-Path Adaptive Gating (DPAG) module adopts a dual-branch gating attention mechanism that adaptively reinforces cross-phase consensus activations while recovering phase-specific cues. Ultimately, PEAG-Det achieves superior performance on both pancreatic CT and brain MRI datasets, surpassing the dual-stream YOLOv12s by 8.7% and 2.2% AP while using less than half the parameters, demonstrating both robust task-level performance and strong cross-organ generalization. The code is available at https://github.com/yang-0201/PEAGDet.

title: Multi-Domain Audio Question Answering Benchmark toward Acoustic Content Reasoning
authors: Chao-Han Huck Yang, Sreyan Ghosh, Qing Wang, Jaeyeon Kim, Hengyi Hong, Sonal Kumar, Guirui Zhong, Zhifeng Kong, S Sakshi, Vaibhavi Lokegaonkar, Oriol Nieto, Ramani Duraiswami, Dinesh Manocha, Gunhee Kim, Jun Du, Rafael Valle, Bryan Catanzaro
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We introduce a multi-domain Audio Question Answering (MD-Audio) benchmark designed to advance research in multi-domain sound understanding. The task is organized into three subsets (Bioacoustics, Temporal Soundscapes, and Complex QA), each targeting distinct aspects of interactive question answering over diverse acoustic environments. The benchmark encompasses a broad range of auditory material, including marine mammal vocalizations, environmental soundscapes, and complex real-world audio recordings. We further detail the evaluation protocol, which employs top-1 accuracy alongside an answer-shuffling robustness criterion, and provide baseline results using state-of-the-art audio-language models (Qwen2-Audio-7B, AudioFlamingo 2, and Gemini-2-Flash). The non-archrival benchmark is released as an open-source resource and used for the DCASE 2025 AQA Challenge. Preliminary analyses on the development set reveal substantial variability in model performance across both tasks and domains. Ultimately, this benchmark is intended to foster progress in the auditory comprehension and reasoning abilities of audio-language models, capabilities that are critical for enabling systems to perceive, interpret, and interact with the acoustic world in a manner approaching human-level acuity.

title: Directed Hypergraph Framelet Neural Network
authors: Zhen Wang, Xiaoyu Tang, Xinyu Qi, Yongheng Wang, Yan Pang, Ji Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Hypergraphs model multi-way directional interactions, yet most Hypergraph Neural Networks ignore direction and collapse higher-order structure. We propose Di-HFNN, which combines a complex-valued incidence encoding yielding a Hermitian Laplacian with multiresolution wavelet frames for spectral filtering. This framework captures directional asymmetries across multiple scales. Di-HFNN achieves state-of-the-art performance on eight benchmarks and demonstrates superior directional modeling on synthetic tasks.

title: Kalman Filter Based Linear Deformable for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Zhihao Zhao, Yinzheng Zhao, Junjie Yang, Quanmin Liang, Daniel Zapp, Kai Huang, M.Ali Nasseri
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In the realm of ophthalmic imaging, accurate vascular segmentation is paramount for diagnosing and managing various eye diseases. Contemporary deep learning-based vascular segmentation models rival human accuracy but still face substantial challenges in accurately segmenting minuscule blood vessels in neural network applications. In this paper, we propose a novel network (KaLDeX) for vascular segmentation leveraging a Kalman filter based linear deformable cross attention (LDCA) module integrated within a UNet++ framework. Our approach is based on two key components: Kalman filter (KF) based linear deformable convolution (LD) and cross-attention (CA) modules. The LD module is designed to adaptively adjust the focus on thin vessels that might be overlooked in standard convolution. The CA module improves the global understanding of vascular structures by aggregating the detailed features from the LD module with the high-level features from the UNet++ architecture. Finally, we adopt a topological loss function based on persistent homology to constrain the topological continuity of the segmentation. The proposed method is evaluated on retinal fundus image datasets (DRIVE, CHASE BD1, and STARE) as well as the 3 mm and 6 mm of the OCTA-500 dataset, achieving an average accuracy (ACC) of 97.25%, 97.77%, 97.85%, 98.89%, and 98.21%, respectively.

title: Aligning Language Models for Lyric-to-Melody Generation with Rule-Based Musical Constraints
authors: Hao Meng, Siyuan Zheng, Shuran Zhou, Qiangqiang Wang, Yang Song
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise in lyric-to-melody generation, but models trained with Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) often produce musically implausible melodies with issues like poor rhythm and unsuitable vocal ranges, a phenomenon we term "constraint violation". To address this, we propose a novel alignment framework that instills musical knowledge without human annotation. We define rule-based musical constraints to automatically generate a preference dataset from an SFT model’s outputs. The model is then aligned through a sequential process, first using Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) on paired preference data, followed by Kahneman-Tversky Optimization (KTO) on unpaired negative samples. Experimental results demonstrate that our aligned model substantially reduces rule violations and outperforms strong baselines in both objective and subjective evaluations, generating melodies with substantially improved musicality and coherence. An interactive demo with audio comparisons is available at https: //arain233.github.io/AligningMelody-demo.

title: A Practical Framework for Simulating the Integration of QKD in TLS Protocol Towards Quantum Secure Communication
authors: Radhika Jamwal, Sarada Prasad Gochhayat, Anshuman Kalla
publish: 2026 National Conference on Communications (NCC)
abstract: With the advent of quantum computing, classical cryptographic techniques—such as those underpinning Transport Layer Security (TLS)—are becoming increasingly vulnerable. Although post-quantum cryptographic algorithms are being developed, their long-term security remains uncertain. Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) offers a fundamentally different approach to key exchange and secure communication, as it bases security on the physics of quantum information rather than computational difficulty. This paper presents a practical simulation framework that integrates QKD-derived keys into the TLS protocol using the Pre-Shared Key (PSK) mode. The system employs a layered QKD architecture, implemented using QuNetSim, to generate and manage quantum keys, which are then distributed through a centralized Key Management Server (KMS). The proposed approach demonstrates how quantum-generated keys can be seamlessly integrated into the TLS protocol to enhance resilience against quantum attacks, providing a viable pathway toward post-quantum-secure communication.

title: Concept Activation Vectors: A Unifying View and Adversarial Attacks
authors: Ekkehard Schnoor, Malik Tiomoko, Jawher Said, Alex Jung, Wojciech Samek
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Concept Activation Vectors (CAVs) are a tool from explainable AI, offering a promising approach for understanding how human-understandable concepts are encoded in a model’s latent spaces. They are computed from hidden-layer activations of inputs belonging either to a concept class or to non-concept examples. Adopting a probabilistic perspective, the distribution of the (non-)concept inputs induces a distribution over the CAV, making it a random vector in the latent space. This enables us to derive mean and covariance for different types of CAVs, leading to a unified theoretical view. This probabilistic perspective also reveals a potential vulnerability: CAVs can strongly depend on the rather arbitrary non-concept distribution, a factor largely overlooked in prior work. We illustrate this with a simple yet effective adversarial attack, underscoring the need for a more systematic study.

title: Unpaired Incremental Hashing for Cross-Modal Retrieval In Non-Stationary Environments
authors: Xing Tian, Zhuojun Song, Yuanzhi Chen, Yile Huang, Wing W. Y. Ng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Cross-modal hashing enables efficient similarity search across different modalities. However, most existing methods rely on stationary and paired data for training, while real world data environments are generally non-stationary with unpaired data dominate. It limits the applicability of existing methods in real world environments. Therefore, an Unpaired Incremental Hashing (UIH) method is proposed for cross-modal retrieval in non-stationary environments. UIH employs kernel mapping and projection optimization to learn modality-independent latent representations, avoiding shared-space rigidity. An individual–global collaborative mechanism dynamically balances sample-level and modality-level semantics. Meanwhile, an adaptive label similarity matrix is employed for rapid incremental updating with new samples and labels. Experiments on two benchmark datasets show that UIH yields significantly better retrieval performance comparing to existing methods in non-stationary and unpaired data environments.

title: 2025 Urgent Speech Enhancement Challenge Multilingual P.808 Listening Tests: Approach and Results
authors: Marvin Sach, Yihui Fu, Kohei Saijo, Wangyou Zhang, Samuele Cornell, Robin Scheibler, Chenda Li, Zhaoheng Ni, Anurag Kumar, Wei Wang, Yanmin Qian, Shinji Watanabe, Tim Fingscheidt
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In speech quality estimation for speech enhancement (SE) systems, subjective listening tests so far are considered as the gold standard. This should be even more true considering the large influx of new generative or hybrid methods into the field, revealing issues of some objective metrics. Efforts such as the Interspeech 2025 URGENT Speech Enhancement Challenge also involving non-English datasets add the aspect of multilinguality to the testing procedure. In this paper, we provide updated challenge results on the full multilingual test set and surprising insights into URGENT Challenge results, questioning the reliability of (P.808) absolute category rating (ACR) subjective testing as gold standard in the age of generative AI. Particularly, it seems that for generative SE methods, subjective (ACR MOS) and objective (DNSMOS, NISQA) reference-free metrics should be accompanied by objective phone fidelity metrics to reliably detect hallucinations. We briefly recap the ITU-T P.808 crowdsourced listening test method and detail our proposed and employed localization protocol for text and audio in subjective ACR tests, that we open-source, enabling reproducible evaluation of multilingual speech processing tasks.

title: 3DifFusionDet: Diffusion Model for 3D Object Detection with Robust Lidar-Camera Fusion
authors: Xinhao Xiang, Simon Dräger, Jiawei Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: LiDAR-camera fusion has proven effective for 3D object detection by leveraging the complementary strengths of each modality, improving robustness and accuracy. Meanwhile, diffusion models—originally designed for image generation—have shown promising properties such as flexibility, dynamic evaluation, and iterative refinement, and have recently been explored in 2D object detection [1]. However, their potential in 3D object detection remains underexplored. In this work, we propose 3DifFusionDet, a novel framework that formulates 3D object detection as a denoising diffusion process. The model learns to iteratively refine a set of randomly initialized 3D bounding boxes into accurate predictions. Built under a LiDAR-camera fusion pipeline, 3DifFusionDet incorporates a two-branch feature alignment module that employs cross-attention to effectively integrate image and point cloud features. Extensive experiments on KITTI and Waymo Open demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches, with the added benefit of adjustable inference steps to trade off accuracy and speed. Our findings highlight the feasibility and advantages of applying diffusion models to 3D object detection, especially in multi-modal settings.

title: CE-GOCD: Central Entity-Guided Graph Optimization for Community Detection to Augment LLM Scientific Question Answering
authors: Jiayin Lan, Jiaqi Li, Baoxin Wang, Ming Liu, Dayong Wu, Shijin Wang, Bing Qin, Guoping Hu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used for question answering over scientific research papers. Existing retrieval augmentation methods often rely on isolated text chunks or concepts, but overlook deeper semantic connections between papers. This impairs the LLM’s comprehension of scientific literature, hindering the comprehensiveness and specificity of its responses. To address this, we propose Central Entity-Guided Graph Optimization for Community Detection (CE-GOCD), a method that augments LLMs’ scientific question answering by explicitly modeling and leveraging semantic substructures within academic knowledge graphs. Our approach operates by: (1) leveraging paper titles as central entities for targeted subgraph retrieval, (2) enhancing implicit semantic discovery via subgraph pruning and completion, and (3) applying community detection to distill coherent paper groups with shared themes. We evaluated the proposed method on three NLP literature-based question-answering datasets, and the results demonstrate its superiority over other retrieval-augmented baseline approaches, confirming the effectiveness of our framework.

title: SAM-Driven Multi-Scale Gated Network for Multimodal Remote Sensing Image Segmentation
authors: Wei Wang, Keqi Hou, Xin Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Low feature fusion efficiency and inadequate modal representations are two major problems with current approaches, despite the fact that multimodal remote sensing data fusion offers abundant information for accurate semantic segmentation. This study proposes an adaptive multi-modal multi-scale gated network based on SAM— SAM-AM3GNet. This network utilizes the Segment Anything Model(SAM) and markedly enhances the efficacy of multimodal remote sensing image segmentation through the incorporation of three pivotal modules: Multi-Modal Multi-Scale Adapter(M3Adapter), Adaptive Gated Modal Fusion Module (AGMFM), Refined Attention Enhancement Block(RAEB). The mean Intersection over Union(mIoU) for the ISPRS Vaihingen and Potsdam datasets were 84.66% and 86.49%, respectively. A substantial body of experimental evidence suggests that our proposed SAM-AM3GNet outperforms currently prevalent methods. The implementation of our method is publicly accessible at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/SAMSegmentation

title: Expanding the Horizon of Provenance Domains for Smart Contract-Based Workflows
authors: Choonhwa Lee, Yibo Zhang, Dani Mertens, Eunsam Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Decentralized workflows supported by provenance data aim to combine into a platform with high trustworthiness, transparency, and accountability. Despite the huge potential of the approach, one limitation of the system architecture is that it lacks access to external data, limiting the functionality and potential use cases of the workflow. To address this limitation, we extend an existing decentralized workflow with a provenance bridge, enabling the workflow to access external provenance-supported data without damaging the trustworthiness of the system. The introduction of this bridge effectively broadens the range of use cases for the decentralized workflow by opening it to various external provenance-supported data sources. This article presents the architectural design of the provenance bridge-enabled workflow system, and discusses our prototype implementation effort along with evaluation results. Specifically, we have designed and implemented the architectural framework of the provenance bridge that expands the provenance domain boundary by incorporating support for provenance model translation and oracle-based external data access. The proposed approach lays a solid foundation for a wider adoption of smart contract-based workflow systems by effectively bridging the gap between different provenance domains.

title: Tass: Task Aligned Subspace Selection for Knowledge Preserving Fine-Tuning
authors: Qi Jing, Peng Qiao, Rongchun Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) has become a prominent approach for supervised fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs). Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) is among the most widely adopted PEFT methods due to its computational and storage efficiency. However, the low-rank update matrices in LoRA often contain task-irrelevant directions, introducing redundant information that can impair downstream performance and reduce parameter efficiency. To this end, we propose Task Aligned Subspace Selection (TASS), a novel method that bridges pre-trained weights and downstream tasks. Specifically, we compute the principal angles between the pre-training weight matrix W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> and the low-rank update matrix ΔW to identify the subspace of W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> that is most relevant to the task. This subspace then guides an adaptive rank reduction of the trained LoRA matrices A and B, effectively removing redundant information while preserving task-relevant knowledge. Experiments demonstrate that TASS consistently outperforms vanilla LoRA and other competitive PEFT methods across diverse tasks, with average gains of +4.76 over LoRA and +1.84 over the best PEFT method.

title: Reason to Retrieve: Structured Chain-of-Thought for Text-Video Retrieval
authors: Yuhao Chen, Shubin Chen, Qiuming Huang, Guangrun Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Text-Video Retrieval (TVR) faces the challenge of aligning sparse textual queries with rich visual-temporal content. Current methods based on large vision-language models often overlook compositional semantics, while standard datasets lack detailed annotations, limiting generalization. To address this, we propose R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> (Reasoning to Retrieve), a framework incorporating structured chain-of-thought reasoning to decompose queries into semantic components (e.g., subject, action, environment) for fine-grained alignment. We also introduce a multi-component scoring mechanism to enhance retrieval accuracy. Furthermore, we construct YouTube CoT 2M (YC2M), the largest reasoning-aware TVR dataset, with 50K videos and 2M structured captions. Experiments demonstrate that R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> consistently improves performance while maintaining efficiency, offering a practical solution for semantic video retrieval.

title: A Comprehensive Ecosystem for Open-Domain Customized Video Generation
authors: Jingxu Zhang, Yuqian Hong, Daneul Kim, Kai Qiu, Qi Dai, Jianmin Bao, Yifan Yang, Xiaoyan Sun, Chong Luo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Recent progress in video generation has shown impressive visual synthesis capabilities. However, open-domain customized video generation remains limited by the lack of large-scale, annotated datasets capturing diverse identity-specific attributes. To address this, we introduce PexelsCustom-1M, the first publicly available million-scale dataset for identity-preserving video generation, containing one million curated ⟨identity, text, video⟩ triplets across 8,000+ categories. Leveraging this, we propose CustoMDiT, a parameter-efficient framework that adapts a pretrained multimodal Diffusion Transformer into a customized video generator with only 8% additional learnable parameters. Our method surpasses prior state-of-the-art. However, benchmarks such as DreamBooth cover only 100 classes, which is insufficient for real-world applications. To overcome this, we construct OpenCustom, a new benchmark with 1,000+ categories, created via cross-dataset knowledge fusion from ImageNet and MS-COCO. Extensive experiments confirm the advantages of both our dataset and model. We will open-source the entire ecosystem—including dataset, pipeline, benchmark, and implementations—to support further research.

title: Beat and Downbeat Detection: A Reformulated Approach
authors: James Bolt, Johan Pauwels, George Fazekas
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We propose a novel beat and downbeat detection model that departs from standard classification-based approaches, addressing their limitations and encouraging exploration of alternatives. Our approach leverages beat and downbeat phase information by computing phase differences between all time frames of a song, forming a Phase Difference Matrix (PDM) that couples frames globally and induces periodicity. We predict the PDM using a Kolmogorov-Arnold Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) with mean absolute error loss. We introduce this model as the Kolmogorov-Arnold Phase TCN with Regression Estimation, KAPTURE. Unlike previous approaches that modify the classification loss to address class imbalance, our method requires no such adaptation, simplifying training and improving generality. Experimental results show that KAPTURE achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art systems whilst reducing model parameters. We highlight the model’s potential through an oracle peak-picking experiment, indicating scope for refinement. This work aims to inspire new directions in beat and downbeat detection beyond classification frameworks.

title: Design and Evaluation of an Immersive VR-Based Basketball Teaching System: Enhancing Student Engagement in College Physical Education Courses
authors: Jinkui Ren
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: The problem of improving student interest in college physical education classes is still a dilemma especially in skill related aspect like basketball. In this paper, the design and analysis of a Virtual Reality (VR)-based teaching system of basketball is provided and combines interactive VR simulations with realtime performance feedback to enhance the learning process and student motivation. The system provides a VR environment that runs on Unity3D with motion tracking sensors to record the movement of the students and give them real time biomechanical and tactical feedback. The instructional model involves adaptive difficulty modification, instructive tutorials, and gamification activities to sustain the interest and promote development of the skills. Mixed-method assessment was undertaken that involved college learners taking basketball classes, and the assessment was done in terms of engagement levels, learning performance, and perceived usability. The evidence of the experiment suggests that the students, who worked with the VR-based system, were more engaged, their shooting accuracy was better, and their knowledge of the tactical movements was better than the students who studied the same matter with the help of regular teaching techniques. The paper also emphasizes the opportunity presented by the system to deliver individualized learning experiences, lower the workload of an instructor and create a safe and immersive atmosphere to acquire certain skills. These results imply that physical education classes at colleges can be improved by immersive VR technologies along with real-time feedback systems that can greatly improve student engagement and learning outcomes.

title: Disentangled Structure Prior Propagation for Guided Depth Super-Resolution
authors: Xin Sun, Haojie Li, Xinchen Ye, Rui Xu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Guided depth super-resolution (GDSR) methods utilize color images to enhance depth map quality by leveraging their strong structural similarity to depth maps. However, the redundant texture details in color images often interfere with the super-resolution process. To address this challenge, we propose the Disentangled Structure Prior Propagation Network (DSPPNet), which includes the Structure-Texture Disentangler (STD) module that isolates and purifies structure-specific features, and the Structure Prior Propagation (SPP) module that propagates purified structural priors across the network to guide depth reconstruction. The proposed method effectively suppresses texture-related artifacts while enhancing the depth reconstruction fidelity. Extensive experiments on various datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method.

title: A Novel Self-Aligning Socket Tool with Torque Feedback for Robotic Maintenance: Design, Calibration, and Experimental Validation
authors: Hamidreza Fahham, Gareth Tucker, Reza Saboori, Soran Parsa
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: Robotic maintenance tasks frequently require reliable engagement with fasteners such as caps, bolts, and locks. However, small angular misalignments between the robot and the fastener often led to unreliable engagement, creating potential safety risks, while also causing increased wear on components and excessive contact forces during operation. This paper presents the design and experimental validation of a flexible self-aligning socket tool capable of compensating for rotational misalignments through a passive mechanical mechanism while providing realtime torque feedback. The tool incorporates a slope-based selfalignment interface, a mechanically compliant socket assembly, and a current-sensor-based torque estimation model calibrated using a custom test rig. The complete system is integrated with both collaborative and industrial robot platforms. Experimental evaluations demonstrate reliable cap engagement, accurate torque estimation, and a significant reduction in normal forces compared with conventional engagement methods. The findings indicate that the proposed tool enhances robustness, safety, and adaptability in robotic maintenance applications.

title: SimToken: A Simple Baseline for Referring Audio-Visual Segmentation
authors: Dian Jin, Yanghao Zhou, Jinxing Zhou, Jiaqi Ma, Ruohao Guo, Dan Guo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Referring Audio-Visual Segmentation (Ref-AVS) aims to segment specific objects in videos based on natural language expressions involving audio, vision, and text information. This task poses significant challenges in cross-modal reasoning and fine-grained object localization. In this paper, we propose a simple framework, SimToken, that integrates a multimodal large language model (MLLM) with the Segment Anything Model (SAM). The MLLM is guided to generate a special semantic token representing the referred object. This compact token, enriched with contextual information from all modalities, acts as a prompt to guide SAM to segment objects across video frames. To further improve semantic learning, we introduce a novel target-consistent semantic alignment loss that aligns token embeddings from different expressions but referring to the same object. Experiments on the Ref-AVS benchmark demonstrate that our approach achieves superior performance compared to existing methods. Code is here.

title: Digital Herd Twin as a Tool to Increase the Efficiency of Cattle Accounting in the Agricultural Sector
authors: Sergei Kurashkin, Yesenia Khrapunova, Kirill Belous, Zakhar Pechenkin, Muhammad Khamraev
publish: 2026 25th International Symposium INFOTEH-JAHORINA (INFOTEH)
abstract: The article focuses on the digital transformation of livestock management through the implementation of a herd digital twin. The main objective of the study is to evaluate the effectiveness of the CIS-Cattle information system, designed to unify accounting processes, improve animal health monitoring, and optimize resource use. The research tasks included analyzing existing paper-based workflows, developing a digital architecture integrating IoT sensors, RFID identification, and machine learning algorithms, and validating the system in real farm conditions. A nine-month field experiment was conducted on a dairy farm with 236 cows. Methods involved real-time data collection, predictive analytics, and comparative analysis of key performance indicators. The results demonstrated an 80 % reduction in manual data entry, a 30 % decrease in disease incidence, 25 % savings in feed costs, and a 17 % increase in calf weight gain, confirming the system's practical efficiency.

title: P-SAM: Parallel Semantic Decoding of SAM for Domain-Driven Prompt Generation in Pore Segmentation
authors: Dongsheng Li, Huijie Zhang, Qiushi Xia
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Precise characterization of pore structures in tight sandstone reservoirs is essential for optimizing unconventional hydrocarbon recovery. However, the microscopic complexity and scarcity of samples pose challenges to the accuracy and generalizability of traditional segmentation methods. While the Segment Anything Model (SAM) demonstrates powerful general segmentation capabilities, its reliance on domain-specific prompts limits its automated application in specialized scenarios. To address these challenges, we propose a Parallel-decoding SAM framework (P-SAM). Unlike existing methods that rely on external models for prompt generation, P-SAM introduces a lightweight prompt decoder that directly produces task-relevant prompts from SAM’s image features while keeping all SAM parameters frozen. Through feature sharing and loss alignment, P-SAM jointly optimizes prompt generation and mask prediction under a unified training goal. Experiments demonstrate that P-SAM surpasses state-of-the-art methods with only 0.16M additional parameters and exhibits strong cross-domain adaptability in tasks such as urban village identification. Our code is available at https://github.com/wudi-ldd/ Parallel-Decoding-SAM.

title: Make a Game: A Novel Paradigm for Interactive Game Rendering
authors: Zijie Meng, Jinming Che, Bingcai Wei, Xixin Cao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Driven by the growing demand for immersive and personalized gaming experiences, existing game video generation models often lack robust multi-modal control and suffer from spatial misalignment or autoregressive drift, thereby necessitating a more advanced real-time interactive solution.To this end, we propose Make-A-Game(MAG), the first DiT-based interactive game video generation model that unifies spatial and non-spatial alignment control signals within the UC-3DMMAttn module and employs Action Prompt Blocks(APBs) for precise, real-time manipulation of in-game entities. MAG follows a three-stage training approach, enabling it to produce high-resolution, drift-free, and temporally coherent content by dynamically modulating character actions while preserving detailed environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate MAG’s superiority in controllability, video fidelity, and stability, providing new avenues for industrial-scale game development and broader interactive media systems. Thus, MAG represents a transformative step in video generation, leveraging the DiT architecture to integrate multi-modal controls seamlessly. The project will be available at https://github.com/YMlinfeng/MAG.

title: EDPOTrans: Enhanced Direct Preference Optimization for Machine Translation Between Low-Resource Language and Chinese with Limited Monolingual Data
authors: Lianxi Wang, Xuming Li, Yuting Su, Songxi Xu, Jiahuan Yang, Nankai Lin, Shengyi Jiang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated superior performance across various machine translation tasks by integrating techniques such as model fine-tuning and reinforcement learning methods like Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), while still facing significant challenges in low-resource languages due to the scarcity of bilingual data. To address this issue, we propose EDPOTrans, a new self-supervised fine-tuning framework for LLMs. Aimed at mitigating the lack of bilingual data, EDPOTrans performs sampling on monolingual data and utilizes Minimum Bayes Risk (MBR) decoding to generate candidate translations and construct preference data. Furthermore, to tackle the potential reward instability in DPO, we introduce Enhanced DPO (EDPO). By incorporating penalty terms into the loss function, EDPO modifies DPO without violating its principles. Experimental results show that EDPOTrans, using extremely limited monolingual data and less computation than MBR decoding, achieves an average improvement of +2.5 BLEU, +1.4 BLEURT, and +4.4 COMET scores over all baselines in machine translation between Chinese and six low-resource languages.

title: Gaussian Splatting with Hybrid Deformation and Multi-Scale Depth Regularization for Dynamic Single-View Video Reconstruction
authors: Ying Ye, Xian Wu, Jintian Li, Junfeng Yao, Shaoqi Wu, Weixing Xie
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: With the advent of 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) and foundation models, high-fidelity dynamic scene reconstruction from single-view videos has become feasible. However, existing 3DGS-based methods primarily focus on fast reconstruction, often neglecting motion interactions between Gaussians and accurate depth generation, leading to suboptimal reconstruction. To address these challenges, we introduce GS-HDM, a novel dynamic reconstruction framework. First, we design a hybrid deformable representation to capture complex motion interactions, combining Fourier functions and shared hash grids to model individual Gaussian motion and spatiotemporal interactions among Gaussians, respectively. In addition, to achieve accurate and sharp depth, we propose a multi-scale depth regularization strategy that guides the network to learn depth priors at different scales, while leveraging confidence weights to mitigate depth noise from foundation models. Finally, we develop an efficient Gaussian initialization strategy to generate dense geometric priors, further enhancing the performance of GS-HDM. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms existing approaches in both photorealistic rendering and accurate depth reconstruction.

title: AHM-Net: An Asymmetric Hierarchical Multi-Modal Fusion Network for Robust UAV Detection Using RGB and Event Data
authors: Daohong Niu, Wenhan Yang, Wen Yang, Dexin Bi, Shuai Ma, Jinjian Wu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Detecting unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) robustly is challenging for single-modality sensors: RGB cameras often fail under poor lighting, while event cameras lack sufficient texture information for reliable detection. To address these limitations, we propose AHM-Net, an Asymmetric Hierarchical Multi-modal Fusion Network that effectively integrates RGB and event data for enhanced UAV detection. The core of our approach is an asymmetric hierarchical fusion encoder that incorporates cross-modal interaction modules, enabling adaptive fusion of complementary features from both modalities at different levels. Additionally, we introduce two novel event-aware enhancements: Stochastic Background Suppression (SBS) for motion-salient feature refinement and Event-Aware Query Selection (EAQS) for texture-compensated query guidance. Experiments on the large-scale FRED benchmark demonstrate that AHM-Net outperforms existing methods, while ablation studies confirm the contribution of each component. These results highlight the promise of event-driven fusion for advancing reliable UAV detection.

title: The Risk Operations Center (RoC): A Unified Framework for Proactive, Business-Aligned Cyber Risk Management
authors: Jatinder Pal Singh, Sukriti Das, Nikhil Verma, Sockey Katheria, Shristi Joshi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: As cybersecurity threats continue to grow in complexity, dynamism and business impact, the demand for advanced intelligence systems that move beyond reactive detection methods grows. Traditional Security Operations Centers (SOCs) are typically not predictive, aligned with the business and agile enough to adapt to changing threats with delayed responses, operational inefficiencies and residual enterprise risk. This study plans to design and assess an AI-driven Risk Operations Center (RoC) based on multi-source data aggregation, cognitive threat modeling, predictive and prescriptive analytics, business context alignment, and explainable reinforcement learning to improve proactive cyber risk management. The framework is based on Deep Representation Learning, Graph Neural Network; Transformer based models, Bayesian Inference and DRL Agents, coupled with Enterprise Knowledge Graphs for Risk Contextual Alignment. Policies and models are continually and federatedly refined over time. Comparative analysis shows the superior performance of the proposed RoC with 96.8 % threat detection accuracy, 2.4 % false positive rate, 94.6 % risk forecasting accuracy, 7.8 s incident response time, and 0.93 business impact alignment and 95 adaptability index outperforming the existing frameworks. The RoC is a business-oriented, self-optimizing and adaptive cybersecurity ecosystem to improve decision transparency, operation efficiency and strategic risk mitigation, which is a huge advancement in the field of AI-powered cyber defense.

title: Robust Deepfake Audio Detection via Multi-Level Intermediate Feature Fusion
authors: Jinpeng Zhao, Jian Zhao, Yufei Zhou, Peijia Zheng, Yusong Du
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Existing deepfake audio detectors, such as the XLSR-Mamba, primarily rely on final-layer features, a paradigm where discriminative details from intermediate layers can be progressively diluted during feature propagation. To alleviate this limitation, we propose a Multi-level Intermediate Feature Fusion (MIFF) module, which explicitly aggregates representations from all Mamba layers. MIFF employs an adaptive recalibration mechanism to dynamically estimate the contribution of each intermediate feature, producing a context-aware weighted fusion. This design preserves discriminative high-level semantics while enriching them with complementary multi-level details, leading to more comprehensive representations. On ASVspoof 2021 DF and In-The-Wild, our system achieves EER of 1.68% and 5.66%, corresponding to relative error reductions of 10.6% and 15.6% over XLSR-Mamba (1.88% and 6.71%), respectively, and demonstrating strong robustness to diverse spoofing attacks.

title: Dual-Domain 3D Mesh Watermarking with Adaptive Vertex Grouping
authors: Shuai Ren, Wenhui Deng, Mo Liu, Tianyu Cong, Shaoming Wu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper proposes a dual-domain 3D mesh watermarking algorithm using adaptive vertex grouping to tackle challenges in topological sensitivity, geometric invariance, and perceptual balance. In contrast to spatial-domain methods vulnerable to vertex reordering and frequency-domain approaches limited by basis mismatch under geometric transformations, our solution introduces a topology-driven grouping strategy based on the cumulative distribution of vertex degree centrality. This method partitions vertices into DCT anchor groups and reordered spherical wavelet groups, facilitating collaborative dual-domain embedding. Quantization modulation in the DCT domain ensures global robustness, while a spherical Daubechies-4 wavelet transform with vertex reordering enables blind extraction and geometric invariance. Experiments demonstrate that the algorithm achieves high imperceptibility and strong robustness against geometric attacks.

title: Parallax-Aware Spatial Transformer: Fusing Physics and Learning for Terahertz Near-Field Localization
authors: Zhi Zeng, Chong Han
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: As a key 6G enabler, Terahertz (THz) ultra-massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) introduces a critical localization challenge. The large aperture expands the near-field range, invalidating far-field models and creating a prohibitive search space. This leads to a dilemma that physics-based near-field codebooks require excessive overhead, while generic deep learning models lack physical consistency or interpretability. Challenging the notion that near-field training is necessary, a Parallax-Aware Spatial Transformer (PAST) is proposed to resolve this dilemma by leveraging near-field parallax effects from the lightweight far-field codebook. This introduces a physics-informed attention bias to focus on the most crucial subarray pairs, while maintaining physics consistency. Simulation results demonstrate PAST achieves millimeter-level and 0.06-degree accuracy, defining a new and highly efficient approach for THz near-field localization.

title: Large Vision Models can Solve Mental Rotation Problems
authors: Sebastian Ray Mason, Anders Gjølbye, Phillip Chavarria Højbjerg, Lenka Tětková, Lars Kai Hansen
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Mental rotation is a key test of spatial reasoning in humans and has been central to understanding how perception supports cognition. Despite the success of modern vision transformers, it is still unclear how well these models develop similar abilities. In this work, we present a systematic evaluation of ViT, CLIP, DINOv2, and DINOv3 across a range of mental-rotation tasks, from simple block structures similar to those used by Shepard and Metzler to study human cognition, to more complex block figures, three types of text, and photo-realistic objects. By probing model representations layer by layer, we examine where and how these networks succeed. We find that i) self-supervised ViTs capture geometric structure better than supervised ViTs; ii) intermediate layers perform better than final layers; iii) task difficulty increases with rotation complexity and occlusion, mirroring human reaction times and suggesting similar constraints in embedding space representations.

title: ViTex: Visual Texture Control for Multi-Track Symbolic Music Generation via Discrete Diffusion Models
authors: Xiaoyu Yi, Qi He, Gus Xia, Ziyu Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In automatic music generation, a central challenge is to design controls that enable meaningful human-machine interaction. Existing systems often rely on extrinsic inputs such as text prompts or metadata, which do not allow humans to directly shape the composition. While prior work has explored intrinsic controls such as chords or hierarchical structure, these approaches mainly address piano or vocal-accompaniment settings, leaving multitrack symbolic music largely underexplored. We identify instrumentation, the choice of instruments and their roles, as a natural dimension of control in multi-track composition, and propose ViTex, a visual representation of instrumental texture. In ViTex, color encodes instrument choice, spatial position represents pitch and time, and stroke properties capture local textures. Building on this representation, we develop a discrete diffusion model conditioned on ViTex and chord progressions to generate 8-measure multi-track symbolic music, enabling explicit texture-level control while maintaining strong unconditional generation quality. The demo page and code are avaliable at https://vitex2025.github.io/.

title: LumiDiff: Luminance-Prior Guided Diffusion for Perceptually Balanced Low-Light Image Signal Recovery
authors: Wenwei Luo, Xiangxu Zhao, Zhikun Zheng, Mengmeng Jing, Lin Zuo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Low-light image recovery can be formulated as a two-dimensional signal restoration problem corrupted by non-stationary degradation factors. To recover the image signal, a critical challenge is the non-uniform illumination caused by non-stationary lighting conditions. Traditional signal processing methods often introduce artifacts during recovery. In this paper, we propose Luminance-Prior Guided Diffusion (LumiDiff) to alleviate this problem. Specifically, we construct a luminance distribution map as luminance prior to capture the light distribution in degraded images, ensuring accurate representation of illumination variances. Subsequently, we utilize the luminance distribution map to guide the global-local information aggregation, effectively restoring areas of extreme darkness and reducing the uneven illumination effects. Finally, we utilize the inherent noise modeling capability of diffusion models to perform overall luminance correction while simultaneously removing the amplified noise from the enhancement process. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework achieves superior balance of quantitative metrics and visual quality compared to existing methods.

title: ReaMap: A Graph-Constrained SPD Change Operator for EEG Reactivity with Cross-Session and Cross-Dataset Transfer
authors: Ekta Srivastava, Siddhant Ujjain, Vaibhav Kagathara, Tapan Kumar Gandhi, Sandeep Kumar
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We study EEG reactivity, systematic changes between eyes-open/eyes-closed (EO/EC) and pre/post cognitive load via a geometry-aware operator on symmetric positive-definite (SPD) covariances. We propose ReaMap, a graph-constrained congruent map that, per band, predicts post-condition covariances from pre-condition inputs in Log–Euclidean space, yielding an interpretable change embedding. An electrode-graph Laplacian enforces spatial plausibility, spectral constraints stabilize the transform, and a pairing loss aligns true pre/post (or EO/EC) observations while separating mismatches. Across two public BIDS EEG corpora, a large adult lifespan study with 5-year follow-up and a high-density 128-channel cohort, ReaMap improves EO/EC and pre/post discrimination, brain-age error, and SPD geodesic error over strong baselines (bandpower, microstates, Riemannian MDM, tangent, re-centering/whitening, LEML, TSA), with the clearest gains under cross-session and cross-dataset shift. Topographies recover canonical patterns (occipital α rebound; frontal-midline θ), and the embedding highlights condition structure, supporting interpretability. Under cross-session and cross-dataset transfer and channel dropout, performance degrades more gracefully than alignment baselines. We frame pre→post effects as reactivity biomarkers rather than diagnostic labels.

title: Discrete-Time Periodic State-Feedback Control via a Variant of Extended LMI with Static Multiplier
authors: Masayuki Sato, Noboru Sebe
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: This paper addresses the design problem of periodic state-feedback controllers for Discrete-Time (DT) linear uncertain systems with static multiplier incorporated. For this problem, we use a variant of Extended LMI formulation, which has been derived through very simple algebraic manipulations with the aid of Elimination lemma, for time-lifted DT linear systems. Although the variant of Extended LMI formulation introduces additional auxiliary matrices and consequently increases the sizes of LMIs in exchange for its simple derivation, their roles have not yet been clarified. We clarify their roles through the design problem of periodic state-feedback controllers for DT linear uncertain systems with static multiplier incorporated, and then examine the conservatism of the proposed method compared with previously proposed methods.

title: Bayesian Uncertainty-Aware MRI Reconstruction
authors: Ahmed Karam Eldaly, Matteo Figini, Daniel C. Alexander
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We propose a novel framework for joint magnetic resonance image reconstruction and uncertainty quantification using under-sampled k-space measurements. The problem is formulated as a Bayesian linear inverse problem, where prior distributions are assigned to the unknown model parameters. Specifically, we assume the target image is sparse in its spatial gradient and impose a total variation prior model. A Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) method, based on a split-and-augmented Gibbs sampler, is then used to sample from the resulting joint posterior distribution of the unknown parameters. Experiments conducted using single- and multi-coil datasets demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed framework over optimisation-based compressed sensing algorithms. Additionally, our framework effectively quantifies uncertainty, showing strong correlation with error maps computed from reconstructed and ground-truth images.

title: PI-GNN: A Physics-Informed Graph Neural Network for Spatio-Temporal Diffusion Prediction
authors: Zimo Wen, Cheng Zeng, Hanwen Hu, Shiyou Qian, Jian Cao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Spatio-temporal diffusion processes (STDPs) are widely observed in natural and social phenomena, such as environmental pollution and biological diffusion. While traditional mechanistic models based on partial differential equations are parameter-heavy and computationally intensive, non-mechanistic methods like neural networks often suffer from limited interpretability and generalization due to their lack of explicit physical constraints. To address these issues, we introduce a physics-informed graph neural network (PI-GNN) modeling for STDP. The basic idea of PI-GNN is to incorporate physical knowledge into the node-update mechanisms of GNNs, embedding constraints from diffusion equations into the neural architecture to effectively capture STDP dynamics. The design of PI-GNN involves three essential aspects. First, based on the limited collected data, we construct a system of linear equations to solve the parameters of the advection-diffusion equation, thereby compensating for the scarcity of the data set. Second, we derive graph-based discretizations of diffusion processes from the diffusion equation to build physically meaningful message-passing modules. Third, we integrate timestamp and spatial-site encodings into PI-GNN to effectively fuse spatio-temporal features, enhancing its generalization capability and prediction accuracy. We conduct experiments on three water and air datasets to evaluate the effectiveness of PI-GNN. Results show that PI-GNN outperforms seven SOTA algorithms. Additionally, the spatiotemporal fusion method also enhances PI-GNN’s scalability, showing potential for real-world environmental applications.

title: Domain-Invariant Mixed-Domain Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation with Clustered Maximum Mean Discrepancy Alignment
authors: Ba-Thinh Lam, Thanh-Huy Nguyen, Hoang-Thien Nguyen, Quang-Khai Bui-Tran, Nguyen Lan Vi Vu, Phat K. Huynh, Ulas Bagci, Min Xu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Deep learning has shown remarkable progress in medical image semantic segmentation, yet its success heavily depends on large-scale expert annotations and consistent data distributions. In practice, annotations are scarce and images are collected from multiple scanners or centers, leading to mixed-domain settings with unknown domain labels and severe domain gaps. Existing semi-supervised or domain adaptation approaches typically assume either a single domain shift or access to explicit domain indices, which rarely hold in real-world deployment. In this paper, we propose a domain-invariant mixed-domain semi-supervised segmentation framework that jointly enhances data diversity and mitigates domain bias. A Copy-Paste Mechanism (CPM) augments the training set by transferring informative regions across domains, while a Cluster Maximum Mean Discrepancy (CMMD) block clusters unlabeled features and aligns them with labeled anchors via an MMD objective, encouraging domain-invariant representations. Integrated within a teacher–student framework, our method achieves robust and precise segmentation even with very few labeled examples and multiple unknown domain discrepancies. Experiments on Fundus and M&Ms benchmarks demonstrate that our approach consistently surpasses semi-supervised and domain adaptation methods, establishing a potential solution for mixed-domain semi-supervised medical image segmentation.

title: SFN-Net: Integrating Spatial-Frequency Feature Fusion into Deep Unfolding Network with NESTA for Compressive Sensing
authors: Tianyi Lu, Hengrui Li, Xiangming Yan, Yanfeng Wu, Lennox Wei, Shaohui Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Deep unfolding networks (DUNs), as an effective approach to combine optimization models and data-driven methods, have gained wide application in the field of compressed sensing (CS) due to their strong performance and interpretability. However, traditional CS methods heavily rely on manual parameter tuning and often focus solely on spatial domain feature extraction, leading to potential information loss. To address these issues, we propose SFN-Net, a deep unfolding network based on NESTA with integrated spatial-frequency features. By unfolding the NESTA into a deep network, SFN-Net iteratively optimizes a general L1 norm CS reconstruction model and enhances feature representation by integrating spatial and frequency domain information, which enables the network to better capture complex image structures. To further enhance performance, we incorporate the Kolmogorov-arnold networks (KAN) module, boosting the network’s ability to model nonlinear features. Experimental results show that SFN-Net achieves superior image reconstruction quality across multiple benchmark datasets.

title: Dynamic Spectrogram Analysis with Local-Aware Graph Networks for Audio Anti-Spoofing
authors: Yingdong Li, Chengxin Chen, Dong Chen, Nanli Zeng, Kun Zeng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of deepfake techniques has introduced diverse forgery artifacts, posing substantial challenges to audio anti-spoofing. This paper proposes a model that adaptively tailors feature extraction to the specific nature of these artifacts. The approach comprises three key contributions: (i) a context-aware dynamic convolution that selects and weights kernels of multiple sizes conditioned on spectral context; (ii) a streamlined front-end/back-end integration that uses a residual-style shortcut to the AASIST back end, thereby obviating a dedicated feature-fusion module; and (iii) an augmented AASIST framework with two new node types designed to capture fine-grained local patterns. Evaluated on the ASVspoof 2019 LA and the Chinese Fake Speech Dataset (CFSD), our proposed model achieves state-of-the-art performance, attaining Equal Error Rates (EERs) of 0.08% and 0.10%, respectively.

title: Understanding Textual Capability Degradation in Speech LLMS via Parameter Importance Analysis
authors: Chao Wang, Rui-Chen Zheng, Yang Ai, Zhen-Hua Ling
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The integration of speech into Large Language Models (LLMs) has substantially expanded their capabilities, but often at the cost of weakening their core textual competence. This degradation limits the ability of speech-enabled LLMs to fully exploit their pre-trained text-based knowledge. In this work, we analyze the underlying mechanisms of this issue through a focused study of the widely used encoder-adaptor paradigm. We propose an analytical framework based on parameter importance estimation, which reveals that fine-tuning for speech introduces a textual importance distribution shift: the layer-wise allocation of parameters critical to textual reasoning is disrupted. Building on this insight, we investigate two mitigation strategies: layer-wise learning rate scheduling and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), both aim to preserve the original parameter distribution. Experimental results show that both approaches better maintain textual competence than full fine-tuning, while also improving downstream spoken question answering performance. Furthermore, our analysis offers a principled explanation for the effectiveness of the proposed mitigation strategies, linking their benefits to the structural properties of textual knowledge in LLMs.

title: Boot-and-Feedback Framework for Generalist-Expert Model Collaboration in Breast Ultrasound Diagnosis
authors: Ming Cheng, Hongyu Sun, Zhaolin Chen, Jun Liu, Hossein Rahmani, Qiuhong Ke
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Breast ultrasound (BUS) is widely used for breast cancer diagnosis yet remains operator-dependent. While deep learning shows promise, ensuring diagnostic reliability and interpretability is challenging. Recent Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) often generate spurious descriptions due to limited domain knowledge, which mislead downstream expert models and compromise clinical validity. To address these challenges, we propose the Boot-and-Feedback (BooF) model collaboration framework for synergistic MLLM-expert interaction. Specifically, in the Boot Stage, the MLLM is guided by the BI-RADS lexicon and preliminary benign-malignant vision-expert predictions, enabling it to transfer general reasoning to BUS analysis while avoiding hallucinations. Subsequently, the Feedback Stage integrates these descriptions with visual features via a lightweight Attention-Gated Cross-Modality Fusion Module. This allows the expert to leverage textual feedback while adaptively filtering noise. Extensive experiments on multiple BUS datasets demonstrate that BooF substantially outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of diagnostic accuracy and interpretability.

title: EDN-Gaussian: Edge-Directed Densification with Covariance Narrowing For Blur-Robust 3d Gaussian Splatting
authors: Tianzhen Dong, Junjie Wang, Wei He, Cong Lin, Youkang Tu, Chenyang Xu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) can achieve efficient novel view synthesis but relies on point cloud initialization using Structure-from-Motion (SfM) or Multi-View Stereo (MVS). Due to motion blur, de-focus, or poor illumination, the reconstructed clouds are often sparse and noisy, leading to limited coverage and unstable convergence. In light of these observations, we propose the EDN-Gaussian method, which enhances visibility via joint covariance–opacity adjustment. It employs edge-directed adaptive densification with anisotropic narrowing and growth-rate control, and introduces a split regularizer to constrain the newborn Gaussians by their parent Gaussians.Vast experiments on the GS-Blur and Deblur-NeRF datasets demonstrate significant improvements in geometric accuracy and rendering quality with degraded inputs.

title: Flow Intelligence: Robust Feature Matching Via Temporal Signature Correlation
authors: Jie Wang, Caoqi Wei, Chen Ye Gan, Jiangtao Wen, Yuxing Han
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Robust video feature matching, particularly across different modalities, remains a significant challenge as existing methods rely on spatial appearance features that are inconsistent in dynamic, real-world conditions. This paper introduces Flow Intelligence, a paradigm-shifting framework that completely abandons spatial appearance in favor of temporal motion patterns. Our training-free approach computes a binary Temporal Motion Signature (TMS) for each video block based on simple frame differencing. Correspondence is then established by efficiently correlating these binary signatures. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method yields a denser set of matches compared to state-of-the-art learning-based approaches, while maintaining competitive regional accuracy. More importantly, it shows exceptional robustness in challenging cross-modal (e.g., RGB-Infrared) scenarios where traditional methods completely fail. By leveraging motion as the sole source of information, Flow Intelligence enables robust, efficient, and adaptable video matching without the need for large datasets or expensive training, opening new possibilities for real-world applications.

title: PMW-Dehaze: Multi-Scale Wavelet Fusion for Image Dehazing via Mamba Framework
authors: Jiayi Chen, Zhuang Zhou, Xin Zhang, Wenyao Qiu, Haijun Xie, Binghua Su, Ran Tao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Single image dehazing remains highly challenging due to the ill-posed nature of the atmospheric scattering model and the limited capability of existing methods to capture long-range dependencies. To address these issues, we propose a novel lightweight framework, termed PMW-Dehaze. Specifically, we design an atmospheric scattering network (ASN) to explicitly model the haze formation process, followed by progressive refinement within the PMW-Dehaze pipeline to achieve stepwise dehazing. Furthermore, we introduce a U-shaped Wavelet-UMamba Block that integrates multi-scale wavelet decomposition with a multi-view Mamba module: the former enhances feature representation through decomposition and multi-scale fusion, while the latter strengthens long-range dependency modeling via an efficient state space mechanism and effectively alleviates the blocking artifact problem that is easily caused by conventional single-patch partitioning through multi-view tokens. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PMW-Dehaze achieves superior generalization across diverse hazy scenarios while significantly reducing computational cost.

title: DiffRIM: A Diffusion-Driven Model for High Efficiency Radar Interference Mitigation
authors: Lulu Liu, Runwei Guan, Kaishen Yuan, Sheng Xu, Fei Ma, Yutao Yue
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Despite its all-weather superiority over optical sensors, the dense deployment of millimeter-wave radar in automotive applications causes severe vehicle-to-vehicle interference, degrading detection integrity and introducing safety risks. However, existing interference suppression methods are often limited by over-smoothing, we propose a Diffusion-based framework for real-time Radar Interference Mitigation (DiffRIM). Its stochastic forward process models the incremental addition of interference, and the learned reverse process conducts iterative removement, thereby significantly enhancing interpretability. We further introduce a Lightweight Autoencoder (LWAE) with Mobile Encoder and Decoder (ME/MD) modules, which extracts multi-scale spatial features through point-wise and hierarchical processing. A dual-residual (DualRes) connection mitigates gradient issues, while depthwise separable convolutions (DSC) and channel attention form an efficient spatio-temporal attention (STA) mechanism to extract sparse patterns. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate the superior mitigation and generalization performance of our approach in both synthetic and real-world datasets. To support the community, our implementation will be made publicly accessible on https://github.com/luluisthebest/DiffRim.

title: Improving Continuous Sign Language Recognition via Lightweight Adaptive Temporal Mixing
authors: Yining Jiang, Sheng Liu, Yuan Feng, Min Xu, Yiheng Yu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Current continuous sign language recognition (CSLR) methods either overlook the dynamics of cross-frame motions or rely on hand-crafted and complex strategies, leading to computational overhead and limited adaptability. To address these issues, we propose DMSign, which integrates two complementary modules: Dynamic Patch Mixing (DPM) and Dynamic Channel Mixing (DCM). DPM reorganizes local spatial patches across frames to flexibly model subtle sign variations, while DCM applies a learnable per-channel temporal filter that dynamically allocates information across frames, thereby promoting complementary feature fusion. Both modules are lightweight and adaptive, balancing efficiency and computational cost. DMSign achieves a new state-of-the-art on three large-scale datasets, i.e., PHOENIX14, PHOENIX14-T, and CSL-Daily.

title: RTNLW: Reversible and Tamper-Aware Natural Language Watermarking Scheme for Multi-Stage Transmission
authors: Tianyi Chen, Bin Liu, Weihai Li, Zikai Xu, Wanying Xu, Xiang Zhang, Nenghai Yu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In multi-stage data transmission scenarios, watermarking plays a crucial role in data traceability. However, highly sensitive applications also impose strict requirements on textual integrity. Existing natural language watermarking schemes often disrupt semantic coherence, resulting in irreversible distortion and preventing effective integrity verification. To address these limitations, we propose a Reversible and Tamper-aware Natural Language Watermarking (RTNLW) scheme that leverages a masked language model and Prediction Error Expansion (PEE). The scheme enables watermark extraction while restoring the original text. In addition, the expansion values of PEE enable tampering detection prior to text integrity verification. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RTNLW achieves a high watermark embedding capacity while preserving semantics effectively. Moreover, the proposed scheme is robust against text tampering attacks, maintaining a bit recognition rate above 85% under a modification rate of 5%.

title: Doppler-Based Pseudo-Reciprocity in FDD for LEO MU-MIMO
authors: Rei Richter, Itsik Bergel, Yair Noam, Ephi Zehavi
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Channel reciprocity is useful in wireless communication since it reduces the overhead of channel-state feedback. However, true reciprocity exists only in time division duplex (TDD) systems. In this paper, we propose a novel tracking method that exploits implicit reciprocity in frequency division duplex (FDD) line-of-sight (LOS) channels, as observed in low-earth-orbit (LEO) satellite communication (SatCom). This novel approach, which we term pseudo-reciprocity, is significant for applying multiple-user multiple-input multiple-output (MU-MIMO) techniques to satellite communication systems that require channel state information at the transmitter (CSIT). We consider a LEO satellite communication system where multiple satellites communicate with a multi-antenna land terminal. In this framework, the terminal tracks downlink channel variations to estimate the corresponding uplink channels, achieving precoding performance comparable to that obtained with full CSIT. The proposed scheme requires only an initial phase of CSIT feedback when the satellite rises.

title: DSFR-Net: Distribution Guided Nighttime Image Scattering Flare Removal
authors: Shaojun Lin, Jie Ma, Jing Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Due to the lack of embedded distribution priors and insufficient modeling capabilities, existing methods often fail to identify the various scattering flares, and may even mistakenly remove the light sources. Our work proposes a unified joint training DSFR-Net, which consists of two pivotal modules. Firstly, when a single nighttime image is input into the Flare Distribution Estimation Module (FDEM), the flare decomposition probability network separates the scattering flares under the guidance of the posterior Gaussian distribution, and the estimator models the flare distribution based on the blur kernel prior. Then, the Attention-Driven Flare Removal Network (AFRN) uses the grayscale flare map to regulate the unpaired training, and introduces a feature preservation loss and a cross-attention mechanism to obtain images with uniform brightness and fewer artifacts. A large number of experimental evaluations show that DSFR-Net outperforms other recent methods in removing scattering flares.

title: Efficient and Global Interaction-Aware Retraining-Free Token Pruning for Vision Transformers
authors: Chenqi Shi, Yi Tian, Hongkun Du, Yuxin Li, Ge Zhang, Guangzhen Yao, Yu Li, Niu Qiang, Wenxin Zhang, Zhenyu Yu, Renda Han
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) and their variants have achieved remarkable performance across a wide range of challenging tasks, yet they typically incur high computational cost and long inference latency. Token pruning mitigates this issue by removing redundant tokens, thereby reducing resource consumption and accelerating inference. However, existing methods, especially reinforcement learning (RL)-based approaches, often rely solely on sparse accuracy rewards despite improving mask quality through global interaction modeling.
