title: Class-Aware Permutation-Invariant Signal-to-Distortion Ratio for Semantic Segmentation of Sound Scene with Same-Class Sources
authors: Binh Thien Nguyen, Masahiro Yasuda, Daiki Takeuchi, Daisuke Niizumi, Noboru Harada
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: To advance immersive communication, the Detection and Classification of Acoustic Scenes and Events (DCASE) 2025 Challenge recently introduced Task 4 on Spatial Semantic Segmentation of Sound Scenes (S5). An S5 system takes a multi-channel audio mixture as input and outputs single-channel dry sources along with their corresponding class labels. Although the DCASE 2025 Challenge simplifies the task by constraining class labels in each mixture to be mutually exclusive, real-world mixtures frequently contain multiple sources from the same class. The presence of duplicated labels can significantly degrade the performance of the label-queried source separation (LQSS) model, which is the key component of many existing S5 systems, and can also limit the validity of the official evaluation metric of DCASE 2025 Task 4. To address these issues, we propose a class-aware permutation-invariant loss function that enables the LQSS model to handle queries involving duplicated labels. In addition, we redesign the S5 evaluation metric to eliminate ambiguities caused by these same-class sources. To evaluate the proposed method within the S5 system, we extend the label prediction model to support same-class labels. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed methods and the robustness of the new metric on mixtures both with and without same-class sources.

title: Vector Quantization-Based Watermarking for Autoregressive Generated Images
authors: Qiran Lai, Adrian G. Bors
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Watermarking is essential for identifying AI generated media, while ensuring their copyright protection and proper usage. Existing image generative AI watermarking methods are designed mainly for Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), or diffusion models, and not for AutoRegressive (AR) models. We propose the Vector Quantization based Watermarking for Autoregressive Generated Images (VQAGI-Mark), a self-attention based image watermarking approach, that selects visually similar token pairs from the codebook and embeds the watermark information through corresponding index replacements, while preserving image quality. Watermarking requires a challenging balancing among its requirements of invisibility, robustness, high bit embedding and security. The proposed watermarking approach embeds multiple bits while enhancing watermark’ robustness. The multi-bit embedded watermark is resilient to a wide range of attacks. Experiments show that the proposed watermark achieves state-of-the-art autoregresive generative watermark fidelity and robustness for watermark identification while maintaining high visual quality. Code is available at https://github.com/TuTAly/VQAGI-Mark

title: VBA-Driven CAD Automation for HFSWR Site Design and Documentation
authors: Darko Marjanović, Dragan Golubović
publish: 2026 25th International Symposium INFOTEH-JAHORINA (INFOTEH)
abstract: High Frequency Surface Wave Radars (HFSWRs) are very successfully used for detecting targets and events on the sea surface. Their effective design and system analysis are crucial for their further application. The most basic part is the HFSWR technical drawing where the precise and detailed arrangement of antennas and other parts of the system can be seen. This paper proposes an efficient and simple software solution for HFSWR technical drawing generation by using VBA-based (Visual Basic for Application) automation in AutoCAD. VBA provides access to the core AutoCAD object model (documents, entities, layouts, viewports, layers, plotting) and limited Map 3D user-interface automation. It is suitable for drafting automation, layout generation, batch plotting, and interaction with external applications (e.g., Microsoft Excel). It offers significant practical advantages when earlier stages of the same project were already implemented using VBA. This software solution can be directly applied for future design of such systems without any modifications, which is its main advantage.

title: Long Chain-Of-Thought Compression via Fine-Grained Group Policy Optimization
authors: Xinchen Han, Hossam Afifi, Michel Marot, Xilu Wang, Lu Yin
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often generate unnecessarily verbose Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning that increases computational costs and latency without proportional performance gains. In this paper, we propose Fine-grained Group policy Optimization (FGO), a Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithm that refines group responses by subdividing them and assigning appropriate weights based on length and entropy, thereby enabling effective CoT compression. Meanwhile, as an enhanced variant of Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), FGO successfully addresses two major limitations of the GRPO: inefficient data utilization and entropy collapse. We evaluate FGO on multiple reasoning LLMs and benchmarks, including MATH500, AIME24, AMC23, and Minerva. Experimental results show that FGO achieves efficient CoT compression without degrading performance, and simultaneously resolves the key limitations of GRPO.

title: Hashing-Baseline: Rethinking Hashing in the Age of Pretrained Models
authors: Ilyass Moummad, Kawtar Zaher, Lukas Rauch, Alexis Joly
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Information retrieval with compact binary codes, also referred to as hashing, is crucial for scalable fast search applications, yet state-of-the-art hashing methods require expensive, scenario-specific training. In this work, we introduce Hashing-Baseline, a strong training-free hashing method leveraging powerful pre-trained encoders that produce rich embeddings. We revisit classical, training-free hashing techniques—principal component analysis, random orthogonal projection, and threshold binarization—to produce a strong baseline for hashing. Our approach combines these techniques with frozen embeddings from state-of-the-art vision and audio encoders to yield competitive retrieval performance without any additional learning or fine-tuning. To demonstrate the generality and effectiveness of this approach, we evaluate it on standard image retrieval benchmarks as well as a newly introduced benchmark for audio hashing.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: Revisiting the Connection between mCCA-Genvar and IVA-G: Role of Orthogonality and Deflation
authors: Isabell Lehmann, Ben Gabrielson, Tülay Adali
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Joint matrix decompositions are widely used to extract useful features from multiple datasets. Multiset Canonical Correlation Analysis (mCCA) is one of the oldest and most established methods among those. Prior work claimed that Independent Vector Analysis (IVA), an extension of Independent Component Analysis (ICA) to multiple datasets, generalizes mCCA and that, under the assumption of orthogonality, the objective function of IVA with a Gaussian model (IVA-G) coincides with that of mCCA using the genvar criterion. We revisit this connection and demonstrate that the main difference between these methods is in fact not orthogonality but deflation, which is inherent to most mCCA objective functions, including genvar. To show this, we introduce orthogonal IVA-G (o-IVA-G) and deflationary orthogonal IVA-G (d-o-IVA-G) and compare them with IVA-G and mCCA-genvar in simulations inspired by the functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI) subgroup identification problem. Our results suggest that the all-at-once methods can successfully perform Joint Blind Source Separation (JBSS) in more difficult scenarios and are more statistically efficient than deflationary methods.

title: VP-GNN: A Unified Graph Framework for Variable-Wise and Patch-Wise Modeling of Irregular Clinical Time Series
authors: Yurong He, Boya Zhang, Longfei Liu, Guosheng Cui, Dan Wu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Electronic Health Records (EHRs) play a vital role in clinical risk prediction and disease state assessment. However, EHR data, being inherently irregular multivariate time series, commonly exhibit sparsity, irregular sampling, and heterogeneous variable dependencies, posing significant challenges for accurate modeling. To address these issues, we propose VP-GNN (Variable-wise and Patch-wise Graph Neural Network). VP-GNN first captures asynchronous inter-variable dependencies via selective message-passing in the variable-wise stage, and then models multi-scale temporal patterns through hierarchical Patch-GNN aggregation. We evaluated VP-GNN on two benchmark EHR datasets, PhysioNet 2012 and Sepsis 2019, across tasks such as in-hospital mortality prediction and early sepsis detection. VP-GNN achieves state-of-the-art performance, with AUROC/AUPRC scores of 2.12% / 10.59% on P12 and 4.26% / 4.96% on P19. These results demonstrate the robustness of VP-GNN in sparse and irregular conditions and offer a promising foundation for personalized medicine and intelligent clinical decision support. Our code will be available at https://github.com/bursonz/VP-GNN.

title: Design of High Available and Fault Tolerant Systems Based on AWS Services
authors: Mladen Zeljić, Emir Mešković
publish: 2026 25th International Symposium INFOTEH-JAHORINA (INFOTEH)
abstract: Achieving high availability and fault tolerance is a fundamental requirement in software system design in a modern digital environment. This paper examines key reliability concepts, as well as strategies to achieve system availability and resilience and explains how Amazon Web Services (AWS) services can be leveraged to implement these principles in distributed applications. We introduce a generalized architectural model for service oriented systems that would emphasize redundancy, failover mechanisms and scalability to ensure resilience under various failure scenarios. The proposed architecture is technology agnostic and applicable to a wide class of distributed applications. Observability mechanisms and controlled chaos experiments are employed to assess system behavior under unexpected conditions and validate its effectiveness. Experimental evaluation on a representative software system summarizes the key findings on providing high availability and resilience and outlines potential directions for further research and system improvements, demonstrating how the proposed approaches can be applied to similar systems to achieve high availability and fault tolerance.

title: On the Role of Extrinsic Value Exchange in Expectation Propagation for Coded MIMO Systems
authors: Fuga Kobayashi, Takumi Takahashi, Hideki Ochiai
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper investigates the statistical properties of log-likelihood ratios (LLRs) at the output of an iterative signal detector based on expectation propagation (EP), highlighting the role of moment matching (MM)-based extrinsic value exchange in coded systems. Prior studies have mainly examined convergence behavior in asymptotic regimes; however, EP’s advantage is not always evident in practical finite-size uncoded multi-input multi-output (MIMO) detection, where probabilistic data association (PDA), which lacks extrinsic value exchange, can perform comparably to or even better than EP. In contrast, when channel coding is applied, EP consistently outperforms PDA. To explain this, we analyze the statistical properties of demodulator output LLRs and show that extrinsic value exchange not only ensures inter-iteration independence but also enhances the Gaussianity of LLRs. We quantify this effect using negentropy and demonstrate its benefit in improving the decoder’s error-correcting capability, thereby establishing the superiority of EP in coded systems.

title: From Pretraining to Robustness: Benchmarking SSL Models for Noise-Robust Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Sofia Eleftheriou, Theodoros Giannakopoulos, Themos Stafylakis, Ion Androutsopoulos
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) models are now the dominant architectures for many speech tasks, including speech emotion recognition (SER), achieving state-of-the-art performance on clean benchmarks. Yet their robustness to noise and corruption in realistic environments remains underexplored and often overestimated. This work presents a detailed evaluation of four SSL models on SER with IEMOCAP and MSP-Podcast under diverse corruption types and severity levels, using a standardized benchmark. Our analysis shows substantial performance degradation under corruption, even for the "robust" variant. We also examine data augmentation and leave-one-corruption-out training, demonstrating that while augmentations can recover much of the lost accuracy, their effectiveness varies across corruption families, and generalization to unseen conditions is often limited. The results highlight the need to measure robustness not only in absolute terms but also as the fraction of degradation repaired, and to report augmentation gains separately for seen and unseen corruptions. This study establishes the first comprehensive benchmarking protocol for SER robustness, and further proposes a set of good practices for reproducible evaluation and for guiding the development of robustness-enhancing methods.

title: Scaling Multi-Talker ASR with Speaker-Agnostic Activity Streams
authors: Xiluo He, Alexander Polok, Jesús Villalba, Thomas Thebaud, Matthew Maciejewski
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: An increasingly common training paradigm for multi-talker automatic speech recognition (ASR) is to use speaker activity signals to adapt single-speaker ASR models for overlapping speech. Although effective, these systems require running the ASR model once per speaker, resulting in inference costs that scale with the number of speakers and limiting their practicality. In this work, we propose a method that decouples the inference cost of activity-conditioned ASR systems from the number of speakers by converting speaker-specific activity outputs into two speaker-agnostic streams. A central challenge is that naïvely merging speaker activities into streams significantly degrades recognition, since pretrained ASR models assume contiguous, single-speaker inputs. To address this, we design new heuristics aimed at preserving conversational continuity and maintaining compatibility with existing systems. We show that our approach is compatible with Diarization-Conditioned Whisper (DiCoW) to greatly reduce runtimes on the AMI and ICSI meeting datasets while retaining competitive performance.

title: LDINET: A Lightweight Dual Domain Interaction Network for Human Pose Estimation
authors: Xuping Liang, Yanxia Wang, Jing Luo, Guoyu Xia, Xinliang Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Human pose estimation is one of the key tasks in the field of computer vision. However, achieving a balance between model parameter volume, computational complexity, and human pose estimation accuracy remains a challenge for current pose estimation models. To address the challenge, we propose a novel lightweight dual domain interaction network for human pose estimation (LDINet), which contains three innovative modules. First, a High Frequency Filtering Module (HFFM) is designed, which high-frequency responses generated by a high-pass filter are used as mask weights to enhance the small keypoint features in the original feature map along the spatial dimension. Second, a Spatial Perception Module (SPM) is proposed to capture spatial dependencies that are lacking in the backbone network’s output stage. Finally, a Frequency Domain Decoder (FDD) is introduced to enhance frequency domain feature extraction capabilities. Extensive experiments on the MSCOCO 2017 and MPII datasets validate the performance of LDINet, and ablation studies on the MPII dataset confirm the effectiveness of HFFM, SPM, and FDD.

title: Unlocking Hidden Potential in Point Cloud Networks with Attention-Guided Grouping-Feature Coordination
authors: Shangzhuo Xie, Qianqian Yang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Point cloud analysis has evolved with diverse network architectures, while existing works predominantly focus on introducing novel structural designs. However, conventional point-based architectures - processing raw points through sequential sampling, grouping, and feature extraction layers - demonstrate underutilized potential. We notice that substantial performance gains can be unlocked through strategic module integration rather than structural modifications. In this paper, we propose the Grouping-Feature Coordination Module (GF-Core), a lightweight separable component that simultaneously regulates both grouping layer and feature extraction layer to enable more nuanced feature aggregation. Besides, we introduce a self-supervised pretraining strategy specifically tailored for point-based inputs to enhance model robustness in complex point cloud analysis scenarios. On ModelNet40 dataset, our method elevates base-line networks to 94.0% accuracy, matching advanced frameworks’ performance while preserving architectural simplicity. On three variants of the ScanObjectNN dataset, we obtain improvements of 2.96%, 6.34%, and 6.32% respectively.

title: Diffface-Edit: a Diffusion-Based Facial Dataset for Forgery-Semantic Driven Deepfake Detection Analysis
authors: Feng Ding, Wenhui Yi, Xinan He, Mengyao Xiao, Jianfeng Xu, Jianqiang Du
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Generative models can now produce imperceptible, fine-grained facial edits, raising serious privacy risks. Yet existing AI-generated face datasets seldom cover subtle, region-level manipulations, and the effect of splice attacks between real and edited samples on detectors remains underexplored; we term these detector-evasive samples. To fill these gaps, we introduce DiffFace-Edit, a dataset with over two million fake images and edits across eight facial regions with diverse single- and multi-region combinations. We further analyze the impact of detector-evasive samples, provide a comprehensive dataset study, and propose a cross-domain IMDL-based evaluation. Dataset will be available at https://github.com/ywh1093/DiffFace-Edit.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Multi-Scale Feature Fusion with Transformer for Time Series Prediction
authors: Jianhong Lin, Zongxia Xie, Yun Wang, Haoyu Xing, Feng Xiong
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Real-world time series data typically exhibit multi-scale characteristics, such as short-term local fluctuations and long-term global trends, which are crucial for accurate prediction. However, existing methods often employ simple concatenation, weighted summation, or attention mechanisms to fuse multi-scale feature. They tend to overlook the differences in noise across scales within the input data, as well as the variations in uncertainty within prediction results. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel time series prediction framework—UMFF (Uncertainty-Aware Multi-Scale Feature Fusion with Transformer). UMFF characterizes uncertainty by leveraging input data noise and prediction confidence, and dynamically adjusts fusion weights across multiple scales based on the probability density functions associated with each scale, thereby enhancing the accuracy and stability of predictions. Experimental results demonstrate that UMFF consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance in both point prediction and probabilistic prediction tasks, achieving superior results across multiple benchmark datasets. The code is available at https://github.com/ljhjxt/umff.

title: A Graph-Driven Hybrid CNN-GNN Approach for Vessel Classification in Adaptive Optics Images
authors: Abir Aissa, Guillaume Lachaud, Florence Rossant, Hélène Urien, Michel Paques
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Adaptive Optics Ophthalmoscopy (AOO) enables high-resolution retinal imaging for clinical biomarker analysis. While existing methods automatically segment retinal bifurcations and extract vascular biomarkers, they require manual selection of high-quality arterial regions, limiting scalability and efficiency. We propose a fully automated patch-based CNN-GNN framework for vessel classification in AOO images. Our method extracts deep features from image patches, constructs graphs with cost-weighted edges capturing vessel connectivity, then applies Graph Attention Networks (GATs) for classification. This hybrid approach integrates local features with global topological information through graph processing, eliminating manual region selection requirements. We evaluate our pipeline on a dataset of 4,258 vessel patches across four CNN/Transformer backbones (ResNet-18, EfficientNet-B2, DenseNet-121, TinyViT). The backbone+GAT combination consistently outperforms backbone-only baselines, achieving up to 86.5% accuracy with improvements ranging from 1.9% to 4.4% across architectures. Our approach provides a robust, scalable foundation for automated vascular biomarker analysis in AOO imaging, supporting more efficient clinical workflows.

title: Specular-Aware Lambert Reconstruction for Monocular Endoscopic Depth Estimation
authors: Jingyang Shan, Shaolin Zhang, Tianyi Wang, Jun Wu, Xiaolin Qin
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Monocular endoscopic depth estimation benefits from the near-field lighting prior but remains fragile under specular highlights that violate photometric assumptions. We propose an optical-flow-guided Lambert surface reconstruction for robust highlight suppression and depth recovery. First, we introduce Frequency–Intensity Joint Prompting (FIJP) to generate reliable specular prompts by fusing high-frequency responses, brightness percentiles, and low-saturation cues, which guide SAM for fine masks. Second, we propose an optical-flow-guided Lambert surface reconstruction, where valid pixels are propagated across frames before inpainting, and LaMa is adapted to the endoscopic domain to suppress dark artifacts and preserve texture. Finally, we develop a temporal near-field light fusion framework that refines depth by combining per-pixel shading with inter-frame geometric consistency. Our method achieves superior highlight removal on C3VD, CoLON10K, and a clinical dataset, and obtains reliable depth results on C3VD, demonstrating robustness to specular reflections. The source code is available at https://github.com/sjyzy/LRED.

title: KBNet: A Knowledge Bridging Network for Generalizable Deepfake Detection
authors: Chao Liu, Liejun Wang, Zhiqing Guo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Generalizable deepfake detection is challenging due to the domain gap between training and real-world data. While CNN-based detectors capture local textures well, they often overfit and generalize poorly. Methods using large pre-trained models, such as Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP), improve cross-domain detection but lack sensitivity to fine-grained artifacts. To overcome these limitations, we propose KBNet, a novel knowledge-bridging framework that dynamically integrates local artifact-sensitive features from a CNN backbone with semantic prior knowledge from CLIP. KBNet consists of two key components: the Bridge Feature Generator (BFG), which uses cross-attention to inject semantic information from CLIP into CNN features at multiple stages. The Cross-Representation Fusion Block (CRFB) aligns and fuses multi-source features into a discriminative representation. Extensive experiments show that KBNet achieves state-of-the-art performance in both intra-dataset and cross-dataset settings, demonstrating strong generalization for real-world deepfake detection.

title: Polarization Fingerprint Identification via Cluster-Driven Sub-Classifiers Routing
authors: Xian Li, Jinlong Xu, Dong Wei, Weiqing Huang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Polarization Fingerprint (PF) enables cost-effective device identification, yet its discriminative capability with a monolithic classifier struggles when facing large-scale device sets. Therefore, we propose a Cluster-driven Sub-classifiers Routing scheme for Polarization Fingerprint Identification (CSR-PFI) to enhance identification performance. CSR-PFI partitions the registered device set into smaller, mutually exclusive, and variable-scale subsets using a clustering model based on PF features. It then routes each incoming PF sample to its dedicated sub-classifier, whose architecture is tailored for the corresponding subset size. On a 5,000 device dataset, CSR-PFI improves identification accuracy from 89.98% to 94.17% at 15 dB SNR, while requiring only 4.37 M additional parameters and reducing computational cost by 22.44M FLOPs. Furthermore, CSR-PFI achieves a substantial reduction in FAR while maintaining the same FRR, thereby significantly enhancing identification security.

title: SentientSec Corporate Management System with AI NLP Avatars & IoT Cyber Defense
authors: Sujanthi S, Sri Harivarshan R, Neya Charlote S A, Visnupriyan P M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: This article focuses on the improvement of a corporate management system named SentientSec that facilitates the exchange of information, enhances strong privacy, and anticipatory user interaction. This includes artificial intelligence and natural language processing that are used to introduce a virtual assistant to handle cyber threats and solve security concerns in a real-time environment. This also includes the perspective of augmented and virtual reality that enables the users to visualize a 3D situational awareness model of cyber security breaches. Through this the users gain knowledge about the attacks and the way to defend and negate them. To track the physical security breaches, IoT devices are used. This system, on the whole, offers AI feedback in real time, physical monitoring through IoT devices, and AR/VR responses to the users.

title: Enhancing Speaker Verification with w2v-BERT 2.0 and Knowledge Distillation Guided Structured Pruning
authors: Ze Li, Ming Cheng, Ming Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large-scale self-supervised Pre-Trained Models (PTMs) have shown significant improvements in the speaker verification (SV) task by providing rich feature representations. In this paper, we utilize w2v-BERT 2.0, a model with approximately 600 million parameters trained on 4.5 million hours of unlabeled data across 143 languages, for the SV task. The MFA structure with Layer Adapter is employed to process the multi-layer feature outputs from the PTM and extract speaker embeddings. Additionally, we incorporate LoRA for efficient fine-tuning. Our model achieves state-of-the-art results with 0.12% and 0.55% EER on the Vox1-O and Vox1-H test sets, respectively. Furthermore, we apply knowledge distillation guided structured pruning, reducing the model size by 80% while achieving only a 0.04% EER degradation. Source code and models are released at https://github.com/ZXHY-82/w2v-BERT-2.0_SV.

title: Online Time-Series Contrastive Learning for Soft Sensing of Biopharmaceutical Processes
authors: Yinlong Li, Licheng Pan, Hu Xu, Xinggao Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Biopharmaceutical processes typically generate abundant high-frequency online sensor data but suffer from sparse and delayed offline quality measurements, creating a “data-rich but label-poor” dilemma that hinders effective process monitoring. Furthermore, these processes are subject to significant distribution shifts due to batch-to-batch variability and time-varying metabolic states. To address these challenges, this article proposes a novel online time-series contrastive learning framework for soft sensing, validated on an industrial erythromycin fermentation process. We introduce a mechanism-informed soft contrastive learning strategy that utilizes information derived from domain knowledge to construct instance-level and temporal similarity matrices, guiding the model to learn physically meaningful representations from unlabeled data. In addition, we develop a dual-timescale online learning architecture comprising a slow branch for robust representation learning and a fast branch for real-time adaptation. Experimental results on a large-scale industrial dataset demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms state-of-the-art time-series contrastive learning baselines, particularly in predicting complex rheological indicators like broth viscosity under temporal distribution shifts.

title: TransSeg: 3-D Semantic Segmentation Based on 2D–3D Trans-Modal Fusion for Autonomous Driving
authors: Mingxi Zhuang, Guangming Wang, Hesheng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: With the rapid development of multimodal data fusion based on deep learning, semantic segmentation using the complementary information from the camera and LiDAR sensor can enhance the environmental perception of autonomous driving. However, most fusion methods overlook critical issues arising during the fusion process, including: first, insufficient fusion effectiveness in one-to-one scenarios due to a lack of precise feature alignment; second, low efficiency in the fusion process and a large computational burden on the model. To address these issues, we propose a trans-modal fusion method based on state space model and deformable transformer module. The proposed trans-modal fusion method makes use of the 2-D prior information of the image, such as texture and appearance information. By distilling knowledge from fused 2D–3D representations, it significantly enhances the feature extraction capability of the 3-D point cloud. First, we design a lightweight global gap adjustment attention module based on a linear state-space model, named global gap attention, to better bridge the information gap between point clouds and images. Then, to enhance the efficiency of trans-modal fusion, we adopt a local cross fusion module, which utilizes a many-to-many fusion approach to achieve more accurate feature alignment. Finally, to strengthen the channel information of point cloud features, we introduce an efficient channel attention during the feature extraction process. The experimental results show that the proposed method achieves a better segmentation accuracy compared with baseline. We show superiority of our method on two popular urban datasets SemanticKITTI and nuScenes (achieving mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) scores of 70.9% and 80.4%, respectively).

title: Multisource Multimodal Calibration for Precise Disconnector Recognition in Power Grids
authors: Yucen Fan, Jingsong Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Accurate extrinsic calibration between light detection and ranging (LiDAR) and cameras is essential for multisensor fusion in industrial perception. Most existing approaches rely on rigid sensor rigs and synchronized capture, which limits their applicability to real-world industrial scenarios where sensors are often deployed independently or operate asynchronously. In contrast, this article presents a structure-aware, targetless calibration framework that enables reliable LiDAR–camera alignment across heterogeneous and unsynchronized data sources. The method establishes robust 3D–2D correspondence through a dual-line strategy that anchors the calibration on structural priors—using device-specific main lines and surrounding auxiliary lines—to ensure stability under large viewpoint offsets. Unlike conventional autonomous-driving setups, the proposed framework addresses the geometric misalignment challenges inherent to cross-platform sensing, such as uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV)–ground robot cooperation or multirobot inspection fleets, where rigid coupling is infeasible. Experiments on real-world power-system inspection data further validate the method’s accuracy and robustness, achieving subdegree alignment precision and enabling fine-grained disconnector state verification as a representative industrial use case. The results demonstrate that the proposed asynchronous calibration paradigm offers a general and practical solution for multisource fusion in complex industrial environments.

title: History-of-Error Neural Network Disturbance Rejection With Application to Motion Control of a Cylindrical Robot
authors: Samira Asadi, Amin Farjah, Mehrdad Moallem
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Precision motion control is essential for the reliable and efficient operation of industrial and autonomous systems. This article presents an adaptive neural network (NN) controller that achieves high-accuracy trajectory tracking and disturbance rejection in the presence of nonlinear effects, such as cogging and friction forces. The key contribution lies in incorporating a history-of-error learning mechanism into the NN objective function, enabling the controller to adapt based on both instantaneous and past tracking dynamics. This temporal formulation enhances learning efficiency and disturbance suppression compared to conventional instantaneous-error methods. The controller employs a single-layer feedforward NN to estimate unknown dynamics, with a modified backpropagation algorithm and e-modification ensuring stable weight adaptation. A Lyapunov-based stability analysis is presented that guarantees bounded tracking errors and weight convergence. The resulting control law achieves robust real-time implementation without compromising computational efficiency. Experimental results on a cylindrical robot subjected to friction and cogging torque disturbances show over 60% improvement in tracking accuracy and up to 40% enhancement in disturbance rejection relative to NN and proportional integral derivative (PID) baselines. These results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed history-enhanced adaptive neural control framework for precision motion systems.

title: Joint Information-Theoretic Threshold Optimization and Error Bounds for MAP Decoding in Parallel Stochastic Quantizer Architectures
authors: Hassan Tavakoli, Thinh Nguyen, Bella Bose
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This paper studies a Parallel Stochastic Quantizer architecture, in which multiple low-resolution comparators process an input signal in parallel, each corrupted by additive noise. We model the system as a discrete memoryless channel and analyze the mutual information (MI) between the input and the aggregated quantized output. To optimize information transfer, we propose an iterative algorithm that alternates between maximizing MI via momentum-accelerated gradient ascent and minimizing an upper bound on the MAP error probability using gradient descent. Theoretical analysis establishes that the MAP error probability decays exponentially with the number of parallel comparators, and the MI approaches the input entropy in the large-branch regime. Simulation results demonstrate the convergence of the algorithm, the dynamic adaptation of thresholds and noise parameters, and the tightness of the Bhattacharyya bound in predicting the system’s error probability. While minimizing the MAP error probability targets task-specific performance, maximizing mutual information provides a general-purpose objective that preserves task-relevant structure, ensuring robust performance across a wide range of downstream inference tasks. This joint optimization enables the system to achieve both low error rates and broad utility without being tailored to a single application. Our approach provides an efficient framework for designing low-power, high-speed quantization systems suitable for edge computing and sensing applications.

title: Enhancing Auditory Brainstem Response Extraction From Noised EEG With Adaptive Kalman Denoising Technique_supp1-3681602.pdf
authors: Xin Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Auditory brainstem response (ABR) is a weak evoked EEG signal that provides an objective measure for assessing auditory function. However, the traditional extraction method, namely, averaging is noise-sensitive and needs thousands of trials, which places high demands on the subjects and the experimental environment. Kalman weighted (KW) technique has the potential to extract ABR with high quality but relies heavily on expert experience for precise parameter tuning. In this article, an adaptive Kalman denoising technique, which can adaptively adjust the parameter, was developed. A comprehensive investigation was carried out on different noise types (pink noise/Gaussian white noise/uniform noise), proportions (20%/40%/60%/80%/100%), amplitudes (20/40/60/80 μV), and integrated manners (early-noised/intermittent-noised/late-noised). Multiple metrics, such as Pearson correlation coefficient, root mean square error, latency and amplitude of characteristics wave, and the wave recognition rate were calculated for evaluation. The simulation results showed that the proposed method outperformed the averaging and KW techniques over these evaluation metrics. Also, these evaluation metrics of the proposed method were much more stable than those of averaging the KW. Finally, we verified the proposed method in the real scenario. It is believed that the proposed method opens a window for daily ABR-based auditory health condition screening, which can benefit the early detection and diagnosis of auditory diseases.

title: WildfireKR: An LLM-Agent Decision Support Framework for Autonomous Wildfire Spread Simulation in South Korea
authors: Eungyu Park, Jangwon Park, Taeyu Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Wildfire management in South Korea requires rapid scenario exploration tools that enable responders to evaluate potential fire spread under varying weather and fuel conditions within operationally relevant timeframes. This paper presents WildfireKR, an open-source decision support system (DSS) that leverages large language model (LLM) agent orchestration to automate end-to-end wildfire spread simulation. The system chains ten functional modules, seven domain-specific and three supporting, via structured LLM tool-calling, enabling users to initiate multi-scenario analyses via natural language or structured YAML configurations. Korea-specific calibration comprising approximately 4,700 Monte Carlo simulations across four historical fires established a broadleaf spread probability of 0.70, reducing mean area error by 47% relative to the Mediterranean-calibrated baseline. Evaluation on three historical wildfires (Samcheok 2022, Goseong 2019, Uljin 2022) yielded area ratios of 0.65–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.58\times $ </tex-math></inline-formula> relative to observed burned areas. The single underestimation (Goseong, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.65\times $ </tex-math></inline-formula>) is attributed to extreme wind-driven spotting beyond the simplified spotting model’s capability, while the overestimations are consistent with the expected behavior of suppression-free simulations. Supplementary spatial assessment using Sentinel-2 differenced Normalized Burn Ratio (dNBR) derived burn scars with perimeter-based reference delineation (morphological closing) produced Recall values of 0.89–0.99 and IoU of approximately 0.24, indicating that the simulated fire envelope captured the majority of the actual burned area, a property relevant for decision support where false negatives carry disproportionate consequences. For comparison, pixel-based reference delineation yields lower IoU (0.13–0.16), demonstrating that much of the apparent spatial disagreement arises from interior unburned enclaves within fire boundaries rather than fundamental model failure. Systems evaluation confirmed high repeatability (burned-area CV = 3.3–7.1%), operational efficiency (2.7 min vs. 2–4 h manual workflow), and robustness (8/8 adversarial input tests detected). The framework will be released under the MIT license upon acceptance and comprises approximately 1,600 logical lines of Python.

title: Beyond Single-Framework Architectures: A Systematic Evaluation and Hybrid Design for Scalable Multi-Agent Coordination
authors: Muhammad Saleem Khan, Muhammad Janas Khan, Muhammad Sharif, Sadaf Yasmin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent frameworks have emerged as powerful tools for complex coordination tasks, yet systematic comparisons across realistic, large-scale scenarios remain limited. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of three prominent frameworks, i.e., CrewAI, AutoGen, and LangChain, alongside a novel hybrid architecture that strategically combines their complementary strengths. Using CREW-WILDFIRE, a realistic wildfire response benchmark featuring heterogeneous agents and partial observability, we evaluated the framework’s performance across 17 task levels and 7 behavioral competencies. Our results reveal distinct architectural trade-offs: CrewAI achieves 34% higher task designation scores through hierarchical coordination but lacks adaptability; AutoGen demonstrates 28% superior plan adaptation via conversational feedback, yet incurs high token costs; LangChain offers maximum flexibility at the expense of a 42% increase in computational overhead. Based on these insights, we propose and validate a hybrid LangGraph-CrewAI architecture that achieves a 96.1% success rate while reducing token consumption by 76.2% and decision latency by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$14.5\times $ </tex-math></inline-formula> compared to pure CrewAI implementations. Our comprehensive validation across 51 episodes (17 scenarios <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\times 3$ </tex-math></inline-formula> seeds) demonstrates perfect scores in task designation and agent capitalization competencies, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. This work provides evidence-based guidance for framework selection, highlights fundamental scalability challenges in current systems, and demonstrates that intelligent hybrid designs can overcome individual framework limitations while maintaining cost-efficiency.

title: Intrusion Detection Model Using Fused Quantum NN–LeNet With Improved Elliptic Curve Cryptography Based Data Security
authors: V Kalyanasundaram, Sivakami Raja, S Kanthimathi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: An intrusion detection system (IDS) is a network security tool originally designed to identify vulnerability exploits against a target program or computer. Once the attacker has access to the network, they can rapidly collect information. Hence, there is a need of intrusion detection system, which also extends to secure the data as well. This work focusses on a new DL-based IDS model along with data security assurance by deploying enhanced ECC model. Initially, the input data is pre-processed using min-max normalization process. Subsequently, Quantum based Hybrid model is introduced that combines the models like flatten layer proposed feature map with EVD-based QNN (FLPF with EVD-QNN) and LeNet models. Here, the final fused detection results are determined by Double Sigmoid Median min-max normalization-based Score Level Fusion (DSMMN-SLF). Subsequently to the intrusion detection, this work concentrates on secured data transmission, for which Enhanced Elliptic Curve Cryptography (EECC) is proposed that encrypts the data to assure its privacy. From analysis, the precision of detecting the intrusions using FLPF with EVD-QNN+ LNt was 97% which is high when comparing to existing methods.

title: A Probabilistic REM Framework With Dynamic Genetic Beam Selection for Robust Indoor mmWave Communication
authors: Takumi Bushi, Kenji Ohira, Hideyuki Shimonishi, Toshiro Nakahira, Shoko Shinohara, Yusuke Asai
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In mmWave-based cyber-physical systems (CPS), reliable connectivity for robot control requires real-time beamforming. We propose a hybrid radio environment map (REM) estimation framework that decomposes the radio environment into a deterministic linear component and a stochastic Gaussian process regression (GPR) component. This approach enables real-time optimization of beamforming patterns for multiple mobile robots, even when the number of robots exceeds the available beam resources. The accuracy of REM estimation is validated using measurement data obtained from an indoor mmWave private 5G system, while the performance of dynamic beam selection is evaluated through simulations to enable controlled and repeatable analysis. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed REM-guided beam selection reduces the probability of communication outages and improves signal reliability compared to the baseline beam selection strategies. These results indicate that probabilistic REMs can serve as an effective decision-support tool for beam control in indoor mmWave cyber–physical systems.

title: Integrated High-Order Mode Filtering using Cascaded Asymmetric Directional Couplers
authors: Mengyuan Ye, Guanyu Chen, Duowei Zeng, Siyuan Zhou, Tao Zhu
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: We propose and experimentally demonstrate a silicon-integrated high-order mode-pass filter based on cascaded asymmetric directional couplers (ADCs). The proposed design enables flexible selection of arbitrary higher-order modes while effectively filtering out all lower-order modes. As a proof of concept, we fabricated a second-order (TE₂) mode-pass filter. Experimental results show that the device achieves an insertion loss of less than 4 dB across the wavelength range of 1520–1580 nm, with extinction ratios for both TE₀ and TE₁ modes exceeding 15 dB. This compact and complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS)-compatible architecture offers a scalable solution for mode-selective filtering in silicon photonic MDM systems.

title: Black-Box Adaptation for Deepfake Detection via Local Relation Guided AUC Optimization
authors: Xiaotian Si, Linghui Li, Bingyu Li, Liwei Zhang, Ziduo Guo, Kaiguo Yuan, Qi Tian
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Deepfake technologies pose a growing threat to the Internet of Things (IoT), enabling identity spoofing and the spread of misinformation. Although numerous face forgery detectors have been developed to counter these risks, their realworld deployment is often limited by inherent dataset biases. Existing domain adaptation techniques offer potential remedies, but typically rely on access to raw source data and employ data-dependent alignment strategies under a transductive learning paradigm, raising substantial privacy concerns for source domain individuals. This study revisits the problem from the perspective of black-box domain adaptation and introduces a detection framework that leverages only the predictions from the source model. The method is grounded in a local relation-guided AUC optimization strategy, which leverages the robustness of AUC-based objectives in noisy environments while addressing the limitations of conventional AUC optimization, particularly its vulnerability to confirmation bias and reliance on a fixed decision threshold. To this end, two key components are introduced. First, a nearest-neighbor calibration mechanism is presented, where the local relation feature (LRF), a parameter-free representation, captures differences between real and fake images without favoring specific forgery types, thereby reducing bias inherited from the source model. Second, a GMM-based adaptive thresholding scheme is employed to address asymmetric predictions by dynamically determining the optimal decision boundary for real and fake images. Experiments across multiple datasets show that our approach achieves superior generalization compared to state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, the framework is compatible with a broad range of source and target model configurations to enhance detection performance.

title: FuseRes: Robust In-Bed Respiration Monitoring System via Multi-modal Fusion of Millimeter Wave Radar and Wi-Fi Signals
authors: Chengjian Xing, Xiaolu Zeng, Xiaopeng Yang, Yu Liu, Huimin Hao, Miao Liu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Non-contact vital sign sensing has attracted increasing attention from both academia and industry. Millimeter-wave radar-based respiration sensing provides high accuracy but suffers from a limited field of view and strong dependence on target position and orientation, which restricts its practical deployment. In contrast, Wi-Fi-based sensing provides wide coverage and great resilience over device setup, yet its respiration estimation accuracy under ideal conditions is generally inferior to that of millimeter-wave radar. Consequently, the robustness of single-modal approaches in practical scenarios remains limited due to the inherent drawbacks of each system. This paper presents a fusion-based respiration monitoring system that integrates millimeter-wave radar and Wi-Fi signals. By jointly exploiting the high precision of millimeter-wave radar and the wide-area sensing capability of Wi-Fi, robust respiration monitoring is achieved in practical bedroom environments. To effectively utilize heterogeneous signals, a fusion decision scheme is designed to adaptively determine the necessity of signal fusion. Furthermore, a multi-modal signal fusion method based on multivariate signal processing is proposed to jointly extract the common respiration components shared across different modalities. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system, FuseRes, can robustly and accurately estimate respiration in complex real-world scenarios, supporting stable non-contact vital sign monitoring and facilitating practical deployment.

title: TensorTouch: Calibration of Tactile Sensors for High Resolution Stress Tensor and Deformation for Dexterous Manipulation
authors: Won Kyung Do, Matthew Strong, Aiden Swann, Boshu Lei, Monroe Kennedy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Robotics
abstract: Advanced dexterous manipulation requires identifying and controlling multiple simultaneous contacts, including interactions with compliant objects where deformations are large. Raw optical tactile images are information rich but lack calibrated physical meaning, limiting cross-sensor use and real-world deployment. We present TensorTouch, a calibration framework that combines finite element analysis and learning to infer dense deformation and stress/force fields from a single tactile image. Across real sensors, TensorTouch achieves contact localization errors under 1.29 mm and mean force errors under 0.139 N per axis. In a multi-object selective grasp task with two simultaneously contacted objects (including identical cables), the system achieves up to 90.0% success. We further demonstrate robustness under repeated loading, yielding 38.295 dB PSNR between the initial tactile image and the image after 20,000 contacts. The learned model runs in real time at 95 Hz on an RTX 5090, proving to be suitable for contact-rich, dexterous manipulation.

title: Blockchain-Assisted Time-Bound Encrypted Matching for Trustworthy Agentic-AI Edge Networking
authors: Zhicheng Li, Jian Xu, Zexin Wang, Huiyang He, Jinyi Zhu, Fucai Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Agentic edge networking relies on autonomous agents running closed-loop perception-reasoning-planning pipelines to coordinate in real time. A central coordination primitive is to decide whether two exchanged risk descriptors refer to the same event, while preventing privacy leakage and ensuring trustworthy execution under zero-trust, multistakeholder participation. Public-key encryption with equality test (PKEET) enables matching over encrypted values, yet existing designs are poorly suited to agentic edge deployments because they often assume a designated tester or trusted authority, provide coarse delegation that amplifies token-leakage damage, and lack decentralized accountability against dishonest or selective execution. We propose Blockchain-assisted Time- Bound PKEET (BT-PKEET) that offers secure multi-agent coordination with decentralized trust and provenance. BTPKEET binds encryption and authorization to short time windows via PRF-derived window keys, and supports three least-privilege token types (ciphertext-bound, peer-bound, and event-bound) to constrain inference abuse and collusion surfaces in low-entropy descriptor spaces. We further develop a contract-friendly, unified on-chain verification equation that supports arbitrary token combinations and records auditready provenance for accountability. To enable continuous tracking without costly re-encryption or re-authorization, we introduce bridge tokens that incrementally refresh cross-window capability, with fast revocation by halting bridge publication. Experiments on a Hyperledger Fabric prototype show stable on-/off-chain Test overhead (7<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$times$</tex-math></inline-formula>-11<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$times$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and reduced window migration cost (1.7<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$times$</tex-math></inline-formula>-2.0<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$times$</tex-math></inline-formula>), achieving order-of-magnitude gains over pairing-heavy baselines while providing consortium-wide auditable coordination outcomes.

title: Classifier-Agnostic Ambiguity Detection Framework for Targeted Oversampling in Imbalanced Learning
authors: Takorn Prexawanprasut, Worapat Paireekreng
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper introduces a classifier-agnostic framework for ambiguity detection in imbalanced datasets, designed to improve classification performance by isolating regions of high local uncertainty. Unlike traditional resampling methods that treat the entire dataset uniformly, our framework identifies ambiguous regions based on misclassified minority instances and quantifies local uncertainty using entropy estimated over k-nearest neighborhoods. A distribution-driven threshold selection mechanism dynamically selects high-entropy zones that indicate structurally complex and failure-prone areas in the feature space. These regions are further aggregated using clustering techniques to ensure a coherent spatial representation. The resulting ambiguous zones can guide targeted oversampling strategies that enhance model performance while avoiding overfitting in well-separated regions. The framework is modular and classifier-independent, enabling systematic comparison across different models. Extensive experiments on 34 benchmark datasets demonstrate that ambiguity-guided interventions significantly improve recall, F1-score, and G-mean compared to global SMOTE approaches. The proposed method offers a statistically grounded, scalable, and interpretable approach to localized resampling, making it suitable for deployment in high-stakes domains such as medical diagnosis and anomaly detection.

title: Run-to-run control of chemical mechanical polishing head with adjustable partition pressure based on deep reinforcement learning
authors: Yuxiang Li, Wentao Liu, Guoshan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Semiconductor Manufacturing
abstract: The polishing head is one of the most important components in the chemical mechanical polishing (CMP) process, used to achieve an ultra-smooth surface on the wafer. Due to the effect of the retaining ring and the replacement of the polishing pad, the polishing process is subjected to drift and shift interference. The CMP head with partition pressure is designed to exert pressure separately according to the situation of the wafer edge being affected, and compensate for the disturbance caused by slight changes in different areas inside the wafer. A run-to-run (R2R) control method based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is proposed to adjust the process parameters between runs. A dual experience replay mechanism is adopted to effectively utilize the interactive information between the agent and the CMP environment designed. Simulation experiments are conducted under the conditions of pad drift and pad replacement. Results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance in MRR optimization. The WIWNU is optimized to a minimum of 0.3%, and the maximum difference of the polished wafer thickness under the two experimental conditions is below 25 nm. Thus, the proposed method can effectively compensate for the drift caused by equipment aging, and can sense environmental changes and adjust process parameters.

title: Bulletin Board: NEMA Announces 2026 Golden Omega Award Winner
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Electrical Insulation Magazine
abstract: NEMA has nominated Solomon Chiang, Senior Technical Fellow at The Gund Company, for the 2026 Golden Omega Award, to be presented during the 44th Electrical Insulation Conference (EIC), July 12 to 16, 2026, in Las Vegas, Nevada.

title: SSFNet: Spatial-Spectral Frequency-Aware Network for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Guanliang Wan, Danqing Liu, Yunxin Liu, Tengyue Yang, Yanhui Guo
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Spatial–spectral modeling methods have achieved remarkable progress in hyperspectral image (HSI) classification. However, most existing approaches primarily emphasize spatial–spectral feature fusion or multi-scale representation learning, while the intrinsic frequency-discriminative characteristics of spatial and spectral signals remain underexplored for coordinated local–global modeling. To address these issues, we propose a novel Spatial–Spectral Frequency-Aware Network (SSFNet) for hyperspectral image classification. SSFNet adopts a dual-branch architecture, consisting of a Frequency-Aware Spatial Modeling (FASM) branch and a Frequency-Based Spectral Modeling (FBSM) branch. The spatial branch (FASM) enhances boundary sensitivity and global contextual consistency through explicit high–low frequency modulation, enabling improved spatial representation learning. The FBSM branch performs spectral frequency decomposition, where the low-frequency component captures smooth, large-scale spectral trends, and the high-frequency component models fine-grained variations and discriminative fluctuations. A gated fusion mechanism is further designed to adaptively integrate complementary frequency features across different scales. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate that SSFNet consistently outperforms eight state-of-the-art methods in terms of classification accuracy and generalization capability. These results verify the effectiveness of explicit frequency-aware spatial–spectral modeling for hyperspectral image classification.

title: High-Resolution Strain Demodulation via Spectrogram Reassignment-Assisted OFDR for Esophageal Manometry
authors: Zihang Ji, Lei Tu, Tianle Chen, Zhou Xu, Liang Wang, Ming Tang
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: High-resolution manometry (HRM), as a critical diagnostic method for gastrointestinal diseases, is widely employed for esophageal manometry. However, existing water-perfused catheters and expensive solid-state catheters to realize HRM suffer from either limited frequency response or insufficient spatial resolution. On the other hand, fiber-optic manometry sensor based on optical frequency domain reflectometry (OFDR) can realize distributed measurement at high spatial resolution. Nevertheless, there is a tradeoff between strain resolution and sensing resolution in conventional OFDR, which prevents it from achieving both high sensing resolution and pressure resolution in esophageal manometry, making it difficult to satisfy the HRM requirement. To address this, we propose a spectrogram reassignment (SR) method for strain demodulation, which simultaneously enhances the sensing resolution and strain resolution in OFDR but without much deterioration of the demodulation speed. The experiment results demonstrate a sensing resolution of 1.4 mm and strain resolution of 1.9 μϵ, showing over 4 times improvement when compared with the conventional OFDR. Using the fabricated fiber-optic manometry sensor with 80 μm diameter bend insensitive fiber, our SR-assisted OFDR demonstrates a pressure sensitivity of 1.97 μϵ/mmHg and pressure resolution of 0.96 mmHg within traditional esophageal pressure range of 0∼300 mmHg, satisfying the HRM requirement. This is the first work to present an OFDR fiber-optic manometry sensor that meets the HRM requirement, and it provides a competitive method for esophageal manometry.

title: Joint Optimization of Hybrid Beamforming and Decoding Order for Rate-Splitting Multiple Access-Based Uplink mmWave Systems
authors: Yangyang Cai, Liqun Fu, Dehua Zhao, John M. Cioffi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: To enhance the spectral efficiency and access capacity of communication networks, this work proposes an innovative architecture that integrates rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) with hybrid beamforming (HBF) for uplink millimeter wave (mmWave) communications. In the RSMA transmission, each user’s message is split into two sub-messages, contributing to the overall transmission rate, and the base station (BS) decodes them using successive interference cancellation (SIC) technique. To maximize the system’s sum-rate, users optimize HBF, while the BS determines the decoding order for all sub-messages. The joint optimization is formulated as a non-convex mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem, subject to power, minimum rate, decoding order, and constant modulus constraints. To meet this challenge efficiently, this work introduces a two-stage optimization framework. The first stage optimizes the analog beamformer, while the second stage utilizes alternating optimization (AO) to optimize the decoding order and digital beamformer. Specifically, for given hybrid beamformers, a low-complexity strategy is derived to obtain the optimal SIC decoding order by ranking the sub-messages according to equivalent channel gains and minimum rate constraints. Furthermore, a fast online-learning-based algorithm for digital beamforming, named Learning-aided differentiable explicit Projection Gradient Descent (LPGD), accelerates the optimization process. Simulation results show that the LPGD-based digital beamforming algorithm converges over 100 times faster than traditional fractional programming (FP)-based methods, while maintaining comparable performance. Moreover, the proposed RSMA scheme achieves up to 26.4% and 34.4% improvement in sum-rate over NOMA and SDMA schemes, respectively.

title: CFO-Robust Detection for 5G PRACH under Fading Channels: Analytical Modeling and Performance Evaluation
authors: Daniel Alarcón-Martín, Mari Carmen Aguayo-Torres, Francisco J. Martín-Vega, Gerardo Gómez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: The Physical Random Access Channel (PRACH) is essential for initial access and synchronization in both 5G and future 6G networks; however, its detection is highly sensitive to impairments such as high user density, large carrier frequency offset (CFO), and fast fading. Although prior studies have examined PRACH detection, they are often restricted to specific scenarios or lack a comprehensive analytical characterization of performance. We introduce a unified analytical framework that characterizes the statistical distribution of the received power delay profile (PDP) under flat Rayleigh fading and supports both coherent combining (CC) and power combining (PC) repetition strategies. For each strategy, we derive optimal threshold expressions and closed-form detection probabilities. Furthermore, we analyze two key cases depending on the coherence time: identical and independent channel realizations per repetition. Secondly, we exploit the correlation induced by CFO across cyclic shifts to design a novel low-complexity detector that exploits PDP dependencies. Numerical results indicate that PC outperforms CC when repetitions experience independent channels, while CC can be preferable under identical realizations in limited settings. On the other hand, the proposed CFO-aware detector delivers improved robustness under severe CFO conditions.

title: Lithium-Ion Battery SOH Estimation via Mixture Correntropy Loss-Based PCA and Kolmogorov-Arnold-Enhanced Liquid Neural Networks
authors: Wentao Ma, Zhuo Li, Peng Guo, Yang Li, Badong Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Accurate state of health (SOH) estimation of lithium-ion batteries is essential for reliable battery management but is particularly challenging under mixed non-Gaussian measurement noise and data scarcity. This article proposes a robust SOH estimation framework that combines noise-resistant feature extraction with data-efficient, interpretable continuous-time dynamic modeling. A mixture correntropy loss-based principal component analysis (MCL-PCA) method is first developed, which employs a hybrid Gaussian–Laplacian correntropy criterion to adaptively suppress mixed non-Gaussian noise and extract stable low-dimensional health features (HFs). These features serve as inputs to a newly designed Kolmogorov-Arnold-enhanced liquid neural network (KLNN), which augments continuous-time liquid dynamics with structured nonlinear mappings to improve stability and nonlinear generalization under limited small-sample conditions. These two components are integrated into a unified framework that yields coherent and physically consistent SOH degradation trajectories. Experimental results on laboratory and public datasets demonstrate that MCL-PCA significantly improves feature robustness, while KLNN achieves superior SOH estimation accuracy in noisy and small-sample scenarios, resulting in notably lower prediction errors than conventional PCA-based methods and consistent advantages over baseline models.

title: Four Quadrants Operations of Single-Stage CSI With Discharge Path in Motor Drives Applications
authors: Giovanni Migliazza, Emilio Carfagna, Fabio Immovilli, Emilio Lorenzani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Classical single-stage current source inverters (CSIs), i.e., without a prestage converter, powered by a DC voltage source can only operate when the output line-to-line voltages are greater than the input DC voltage, thanks to their inherent boost capability. The recently proposed single-stage CSI with discharge path is able to control the drive from zero speed to the maximum speed only in the first quadrant without the insertion of any additional prestage converter to control the input DC current. Thanks to a deeper analysis on the topology and on the control strategy, this work shows a new control scheme and, consequently, a space vector modulation able to cover the four quadrant operations of the machine without any use of bidirectional or additional switches or other modifications of the topology. The single-stage CSI with discharge path state-space equations are presented with the average model and verified through simulations. Stability analysis is considered through Nyquist criterion. Simulations and experimental results confirm the validity of the proposed solution for single-stage CSI with discharge path topology, paving the way for a broader adoption of this power converter in motor drive applications.

title: End-to-End Autonomous Navigation: A Deep Unfolding and Reinforcement Learning-Based Approach
authors: Xudong Wang, Guanghui Wen, Han Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Autonomous navigation demands the ability to operate in unknown and unstructured environments using only onboard, limited-range sensor data, without reliance on prior maps or global information. To address this challenge, this article introduces a novel end-to-end navigation framework composed of perception and planning modules. By adopting a modular end-to-end paradigm, the framework avoids the compounding errors inherent in traditional, decoupled pipelines. The perception component, Perception Net, is an interpretable deep unfolding network that efficiently processes raw point clouds into a compact feature representation of arbitrary obstacles, exhibiting significant advantages in both efficiency and accuracy. The planning component is a reinforcement learning planner featuring a velocity-adaptive Bézier action space, which learns a smooth and kinematically feasible navigation policy endowed with a forward-looking capability to handle nonconvex environments. The agent’s decisions are based solely on its own state, goal coordinates, and real-time perception features. Extensive simulations demonstrate the framework’s high success rate and robust generalization to complex scenarios. The effectiveness of this end-to-end framework is further validated through real-world field tests on an autonomous surface vehicle platform, demonstrating its capability to operate as a map-less and forward-looking system for autonomous navigation in complex environments.

title: Enhancing Physical Layer Security in Modern Transmission Systems via Semantic Similarity–Aware Signal Processing
authors: Haythem Hayouni
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Semantic similarity is explored as a novel dimension for enhancing physical layer security in wireless transmission systems. A new semantic-aware security model is proposed, in which transmitted signals are adaptively shaped according to their semantic alignment with the legitimate receiver. By embedding semantic similarity into the signal generation process, the proposed approach inherently limits information leakage to unintended receivers without relying on artificial noise, secret keys, or additional power. Analytical insights and numerical results demonstrate improved secrecy performance compared with conventional physical layer security schemes.

title: Information Rates of Approximate Message Passing for Bandlimited Direct-Detection Channels
authors: Daniel Plabst, Mohamed Akrout, Tobias Prinz, Amine Mezghani, Gerhard Kramer
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: The capacity of bandlimited direct-detection channels is challenging to compute or approach due to the receiver non-linearity. A generalized vector approximate message passing (GVAMP) detector is designed to achieve high rates at a reasonable level of complexity. The rates increase by using multi-level coding and successive interference cancellation. The methods are applied to fiber-optic channels with intersymbol interference caused by spectrally efficient pulse shapes, chromatic dispersion, and receiver sampling at twice the baud rate. Bipolar modulation operates within 0.26 bits per channel use (bpcu) of the real-alphabet coherent capacity for optically amplified links, reducing the best-known theoretical gap of 1 bpcu. Remarkably, bipolar modulation achieves 6 dB and 3 dB of power gain over unipolar modulation with and without optical amplification, respectively. Simulations with polar-coded modulation confirm the gains. The GVAMP complexity, measured in multiplications per information bit (mpib), is proportional to the number of iterations and to the logarithm of the block length, and is substantially lower than that of other equalizers. For example, a system with 64-ary bipolar modulation and a root-raised cosine pulse with a 1% roll-off factor was simulated over 4 km of optically amplified standard single-mode fiber in the C-band. The GVAMP receiver requires 93 mpib to achieve 5 bpcu at 300 gigabaud.

title: Integrated Multimodal Sensing, Communication, and Computation With Adaptive Splitting and Dynamic Routing in Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Jin Yang, Danpu Liu, Zhilong Zhang, Xinxin He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In real-time remote sensing scenarios, satellites typically acquire environmental data and relay it to ground data centers for inference, which incurs substantial latency and backhaul consumption. On-satellite inference has been explored to alleviate this overhead. However, the limited computation and energy budgets of a single satellite lead to processing latency on large-scale workloads. Accordingly, a collective inference of satellites and ground servers is utilized to realize compute-while-forwarding inference in an integrated multimodal sensing, communication, and computation (ISCC) framework. A central challenge is that sensing modality selection, deep neural network (DNN) splitting, and dynamic routing are tightly coupled in this scenario. Optimizing these dimensions separately is suboptimal under resource constraints. To manage the coupled multi-domain resources, a joint optimization problem is formulated to maximize the overall task inference accuracy while minimizing end-to-end latency and energy consumption. This problem jointly optimizes sensing modality selection, DNN layer splitting, dynamic routing, and resource allocation. To address this non-convex problem, a hybrid proximal policy optimization (HPPO)-based reinforcement learning algorithm is developed with a dual-actor structure handling discrete and continuous actions. The features related to network topology, transmission capacity, computational loads, and task requirements are extracted for a phase-aware sequential decision design aligning sensing, routing, and computation adaptive decisions with constraint-integrated reward shaping that stabilizes learning and preserves feasibility under time-varying network connectivity. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme can achieve 23.79% <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula> 51.04% improvements in system utility compared to baseline methods, demonstrating its effectiveness for resource management in satellite-terrestrial ISCC systems.

title: Coverage Optimization Strategy for UAV Ad Hoc Network Based on Multi-Objective Improved Cooperative Search Algorithm
authors: Qiaqia Yin, Li Yang, Liu He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: To address the issues of insufficient coverage quality and high secondary deployment energy consumption in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) ad hoc network during the coverage process in indoor obstacle environments, this paper proposes a coverage optimization strategy based on a multi-objective improved cooperative search algorithm (MOICSA). The proposed strategy constructs a multi-objective optimization model that integrates the coverage quality index and secondary deployment energy consumption. Building upon this, three key improvements are introduced to the classical cooperative search algorithm (CSA): (1) the incorporation of fast non-dominated sorting to mitigate local optima trapping, (2) the integration of a crowding distance mechanism to enhance solution set diversity, and (3) the embedding of a dynamic Lévy flight strategy to balance global exploration and local exploitation capabilities. Simulation experiments demonstrate that, under equivalent computational cost, the multi-objective deep Q-network algorithm (MODQN) exhibits the lowest secondary deployment energy consumption, while its coverage quality is far lower than that of other comparative algorithms, eventually leading to its suboptimal comprehensive performance in multi-objective optimization. In contrast, the proposed MOICSA outperforms multi-objective particle swarm optimization (MOPSO), non-dominated sorting genetic algorithm II (NSGA-II), multi-objective virtual force-guided whale optimization algorithm (MOVFWOA), and multi-objective cooperative search algorithm (MOCSA), achieving improvements of 10.719%, 6.667%, 4.348%, and 7.368% in coverage quality index, respectively, while simultaneously reducing secondary deployment energy consumption by 17.697%, 15.784%, 28.206%, and 12.667%. These results confirm that the proposed strategy provides a reliable solution for deploying UAV swarms in obstacle environments.

title: NOMA-Enabled Covert and Fair Data Collection for Multi-UAV Wireless Network Under Imperfect CSI
authors: Menglong Cheng, Juan Li, Chaoxiong Ye, Byungjin Cho, Zheng Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: This paper studies NOMA-enabled covert and fair short-packet data collection in multi-UAV networks under imperfect channel state information (CSI), assisted by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) to serve Internet of Things (IoT) nodes (INs). Motivated by sensitive IoT missions, we consider a scenario in which suppressing detectability can be more restrictive than maximizing throughput, and where finite-blocklength transmission and link heterogeneity necessitate a joint treatment of covertness, reliability, and fairness. Accordingly, this paper first characterizes the feasible boundary of covert communication under imperfect CSI and constructs an optimization-compatible covert constraint formulation. Then, a joint optimization framework is proposed to maximize the minimum amount of data collected from all INs, by jointly optimizing INs’ transmit power, RIS phase shifts, and the 3D trajectories of UAVs.
