To address the strong coupling and high non-convexity in the short-packet reliable rate expression with respect to the power variables under imperfect CSI, a two-layer nested alternating optimization algorithm for 3D covert and fair data collection (TLNA-3DCF) is developed. In the inner layer, robust NOMA power allocation is performed under imperfect CSI; in the outer layer, the RIS phase shifts and UAV 3D trajectories are alternately optimized based on the inner-layer results, with feasibility enforcement and convex approximations used to ensure convergence and manageable complexity. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme, while strictly satisfying covert communication constraints, significantly improves the service level of edge INs, thereby enhancing fairness in data collection.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 27th International Symposium on Symbolic and Numeric Algorithms for Scientific Computing (SYNASC)

title: Single-DMRS Based CFO Estimation for Low Latency Cellular Communications
authors: Ahmed Abdelkader, Waqar Anwar, Zhibin Yu, Xiaofeng Wu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The emerging cellular communication systems require both high throughput and low latency, which can be realized by high-order modulation schemes and reliable carrier-frequency offset (CFO) estimation with a low pilot overhead. In the fifth generation (5G) new radio (NR) system, single front-loaded demodulation reference symbols (DMRS) have been introduced for low-latency channel estimation in ultra reliable low latency communications (URLLC) scenarios. However, due to the multi-access interference (MAI) within a single DMRS, the CFO estimation still has to rely on other paired reference symbols, such as the tracking reference symbols (TRS). This paper proposes a novel method which enables CFO estimation by using a single DMRS. The method leverages the structure of the block interleaved frequency division multiple access (B-IFDMA) to perform joint MAI separation and CFO estimation. Simulations show that the proposed method can approach the Cramer-Rao lower bound (CRLB) and can sufficiently avoid (DL) error vector magnitude (EVM) degradation for high-order modulation based low-latency communications.

title: UDT: Unsupervised Dual-Path Target Feature Refinement for Robust SAR Automatic Target Recognition
authors: Oh-Tae Jang, Min-Gon Cho, Kyung-Tae Kim
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar automatic target recognition (SAR ATR) aims to classify targets such as vehicles, ships, and aircraft in complex remote sensing environments. A major challenge in SAR ATR is clutter, which biases recognition toward spurious background signals and significantly limits robustness under extended operating conditions. To address this issue, we propose UDT, a novel unsupervised feature-level clutter reduction module that requires no target mask annotations. UDT adopts a dual-path structure: a gating path that reduces clutter and localizes targets, and a magnitude path that amplifies target informations to enhance discriminability. Furthermore, a residual compensation mechanism progressively strengthens target representations across layers, ensuring stable feature learning. Extensive experiments demonstrate that UDT effectively reduces clutter while preserving essential features, improving ATR performance under diverse operating conditions.

title: PocketDVDNet: Realtime Video Denoising For Real Camera Noise
authors: Crispian Morris, Imogen Dexter, Fan Zhang, David R. Bull, Nantheera Anantrasirichai
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Live video denoising under realistic, multi-component sensor noise remains challenging for applications such as autofocus, autonomous driving, and surveillance. We propose PocketDVDNet, a lightweight video denoiser developed using our model compression framework that combines sparsity-guided structured pruning, a physics-informed noise model, and knowledge distillation to achieve high-quality restoration with reduced resource demands. Starting from a reference model, we induce sparsity, apply targeted channel pruning, and retrain a teacher on realistic multi-component noise. The student network learns implicit noise handling, eliminating the need for explicit noise-map inputs. PocketDVDNet reduces the original model size by 74% while improving denoising quality and processing 5-frame patches in real-time. These results demonstrate that aggressive compression, combined with domain-adapted distillation, can reconcile performance and efficiency for practical, real-time video denoising.

title: Not Just Detection: Aligned-Driven Purification of Indirect Prompt Injection for Reliable Agent Interaction
authors: Qingxin Xiao, Boyang Zhang, Ziqian Zeng, Zhengle Ding, Xin Cheng, Lingwei Dang, Jinhui Wu, Yangyang Zhao, Qingyao Wu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper proposes an innovative defense framework against indirect prompt injection attacks in agent systems, integrating structure-aware attention-based detection with preference-aligned purification. Our method effectively identifies and removes malicious instructions embedded within structured interaction data, while preserving task utility and ensuring model security. Specifically, we design an end-to-end structured defense pipeline that combines supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement-based policy optimization to accurately filter adversarial content without compromising structural integrity. To support the training of the purification module, we construct the first adversarial dataset tailored to structured indirect injection scenarios. Furthermore, we introduce a novel attack variant that manipulates response data fields to simulate more deceptive and realistic threats targeting agent behavior. Experimental results on the AgentDojo benchmark demonstrate that, compared to existing detection-based defenses, our method not only significantly reduces attack success rates but also substantially improves the agent’s task completion performance in interactive settings. Project page: https://ilianzby.github.io/Agent-IPI-SID-Defense/.

title: CASP: Confidence-Aware Structural Preservation for Continual Test-Time Adaptation Object Detection
authors: Yao Xia, Liuyu Xiang, Dehua Ma, Zhenbo Xu, Huijia Wu, Zhaofeng He
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Continual Test-Time Adaptation for Object Detection (CTTA-OD) aims to adapt models to evolving, unlabeled data streams. Prevailing methods predominantly update each class representation independently, a strategy that neglects the crucial category relationship geometry underpinning a model’s semantic understanding. We empirically find this negligence leads to structural distortion and undermines long-term performance. To counteract this, we propose Confidence-Aware Structural Preservation (CASP), a regularization framework that makes preserving this category relationship geometry a core component of adaptation. CASP operates through two key components: a Graph-based Structural Constraint (GSC) to model the geometry, and a Dynamic Confidence Weighting (DCW) mechanism to modulate the constraint’s strength based on predictive reliability. Extensive experiments on standard benchmarks like COCO-C and SHIFT demonstrate that CASP sets a new state-of-the-art while preserving a better category geometric structure.

title: SQP-Based Passive Coherent Localization via Joint DTD and AOA Measurements
authors: Sining Liu, Yaxing Yue, Boyang Jia, Guisheng Liao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In passive coherent localization, differential time delay and angle of arrival can significantly reduce the required number of illuminators and receivers. However, traditional methods like weighted and iterative constrained weighted least squares often suffer from threshold effects and convergence failures due to the highly nonlinear measurement model. To address these issues, we propose a sequential quadratic programming approach that decomposes the non-convex problem into quadratic programming subproblems and iteratively seeks the local optimum. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method approaches the Cramér-Rao lower bound and achieves higher localization accuracy than existing methods.

title: FedAMF: Federated Alignment with Multi-Part Feature for Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification
authors: Bhairi Neelima, Nirmala Murali, Deepak Mishra
publish: 2026 National Conference on Communications (NCC)
abstract: Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification (VI-ReID) faces challenges due to the large domain gap between RGB - IR images of numerous sensors and the privacy issues associated with centralizing surveillance data. To handle these two issues, we propose FedAMF (Federated Alignment with Multi-part Feature) that integrates a part-based feature learning module to enhance local representations with detailed body-region features, hence improving resistance to inter-class similarity, pose changes, and illumination changes. Key to our model is a collaborative alignment mechanism through which clients obtain globally aggregated class prototypes that represent feature vectors for each identity which are later used to direct local feature learning. By matching local features with shared prototypes, FedAMF effectively mitigates cross-modality inconsistencies and inter-client feature variances without needing the raw data exchange. This prototype-based, multi-objective training approach guarantees that the acquired representations are both discriminative and modality-invariant. Furthermore, we incorporate Differential Privacy (DP) to formally measure the security of the model, and our privacy leakage analysis shows a low bounded privacy loss of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\epsilon=1.23$</tex>. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that FedAMF attains competitive VI-ReID performance while maintaining stringent privacy standards, providing a viable and scalable solution for real-world applications.

title: TF-MAMBANET: A Temporal and Frequency Fused Bidirectional Mamba Architecture for PPG Foundation Model
authors: Zhanpeng Bao, Yannick Benezeth, Fan Yang, Yonglin Zhang, Haibin Wang, Chao Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Photoplethysmography (PPG) is the most widely used non-invasive physiological signal monitoring technology, which has been extensively applied in clinical health management and wearable devices. However, most existing deep learning models based on PPG are designed for specific tasks and lack cross-scenario generalization capability. This paper proposes TF-MambaNet, a foundation model for PPG signals. The model is pre-trained on 50,000 hours of real-world PPG data collected from 100 users in real-world scenarios. It adopts a lightweight bidirectional Mamba architecture that integrates temporal and frequency domain information, combined with a novel multi-scale hybrid loss function. This design enables the model to capture global trends, local patterns, and amplitude dynamics of blood volume pulse (BVP) signals, and can be easily used for multiple downstream tasks. TF-MambaNet outperforms existing models in 9 of 15 tasks across 8 public datasets, while maintaining an extremely small parameter size, making it directly applicable as a feature extractor for personalized health monitoring.

title: RISC-V Microarchitecture Information Leakage Attack via Transient Execution
authors: Jiashun Wang, Rundi Zhai, Yishuo Wang, Chaoyue Liang, Baojiang Cui
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The increasing threat of microarchitectural transient attacks in recent years has presented a significant challenge to processor security. Immediate measures are essential to address the microarchitectural security vulnerabilities in RISC-V processors. This paper systematically evaluates seven commercially available RISC-V processors. The proposed attack framework comprehensively analyzes cache security and timing side-channel attacks across various microarchitectures. Additionally, it introduces three transient execution attack primitives targeting the RISC-V microarchitecture, including a previously undiscovered sub-variant. We implement proof-of-concept attacks on seven distinct microarchitectures and validate their feasibility and effectiveness. Experimental results show that unprivileged attackers can illicitly access sensitive data on real-world RISC-V processors using timing side-channel attacks and transient execution attack primitives. Furthermore, the attack demonstrates a high degree of accuracy, successfully compromising seven microarchitectures with a success rate of approximately 96% and a 30% improvement in efficiency. We reported two CVE vulnerabilities, both of which were confirmed by the developers, highlighting the practicality of the security analysis.

title: Early Prediction Method for Learners at Risk Based on Multi-Source Feature Fusion
authors: Haoxin Xu, Changyong Qi, Tong Liu, Bohao Zhang, Bingqian Jiang, Longwei Zheng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Early identification of at-risk learners is crucial for improving learning outcomes in Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs). This paper presents an early prediction method for identifying such learners using multi-source feature fusion, aiming to enhance the accuracy of risk detection in MOOCs. The proposed method incorporates a TF-IDF algorithm to effectively evaluate the significance of learning behavior data and integrates learners’ profile information with behavioral features, which are then processed by a sequential model for risk prediction. Furthermore, explainable AI techniques are employed to reveal the key factors influencing the predictions and diagnose the underlying causes of academic risk. Experimental results on real-world datasets demonstrate that the method significantly outperforms traditional machine learning models in early prediction, particularly during the one to ten week period. This research provides a robust approach to predicting at-risk learners, offering valuable insights for developing personalized educational interventions and contributing to improved completion rates in MOOCs.

title: Interpretable Modeling of Articulatory Temporal Dynamics from Real-Time MRI for Phoneme Recognition
authors: Jay Park, Hong Nguyen, Sean Foley, Jihwan Lee, Yoonjeong Lee, Dani Byrd, Shrikanth Narayanan
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Real-time Magnetic Resonance Imaging (rtMRI) visualizes vocal tract action, offering a comprehensive window into speech articulation. However, its signals are high dimensional and noisy, hindering interpretation. We investigate compact representations of spatiotemporal articulatory dynamics for phoneme recognition from midsagittal vocal tract rtMRI videos. We compare three feature types: (1) raw video, (2) optical flow, and (3) six linguistically-relevant regions of interest (ROIs) for articulator movements. We evaluate models trained independently on each representation, as well as multi-feature combinations. Results show that multi-feature models consistently outperform single-feature baselines, with the lowest phoneme error rate (PER) of 0.34 obtained by combining ROI and raw video. Temporal fidelity experiments demonstrate a reliance on fine-grained articulatory dynamics, while ROI ablation studies reveal strong contributions from tongue and lips. Our findings highlight how rtMRI-derived features provide accuracy and interpretability, and establish strategies for leveraging articulatory data in speech processing. The source code is publicly available. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: EntroLLM: Entropy Encoded Weight Compression for Efficient Large Language Model Inference on Edge Devices
authors: Arnab Sanyal, Gourav Datta, Prithwish Mukherjee, Sandeep P. Chinchali, Michael Orshansky
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong performance across tasks, but face storage and compute challenges on edge devices. We propose EntroLLM, a compression framework combining mixed quantization and entropy coding to reduce storage while preserving accuracy. We use a combination of unsigned and asymmetric quantization. Tensor-level quantization produces an entropy-reducing effect, increasing weight compressibility, and improving downstream Huffman encoding by 7× (8-bit) and 11.3× (4-bit) over state-of-the-art methods. Huffman coding further reduces memory bandwidth demands, while a parallel decoding strategy enables efficient weight retrieval with minimal latency. Experiments on edge-scale LLMs (smolLM-1.7B, phi3-mini-4k, mistral- 7B) show up to 30% storage savings over uint8 and 65% over uint4 models, with 31.9–146.6% faster inference on memory-limited devices like the NVIDIA JETSON P3450. EntroLLM requires no retraining and is compatible with existing post-training quantization pipelines, making it practical for edge LLM deployment.

title: SWAN: Boosting Image Super-Resolution with Stochastic Wavelet Attention
authors: Shengyi Xiong
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Convolutional neural networks have significantly advanced single image super-resolution (SR), where Non-Local (NL) attention is crucial for accurate restoration. However, NL attention is computationally expensive and often over-emphasizes global context while underutilizing local correlations essential for SR. We propose a Stochastic Wavelet Attention (SWA) mechanism that efficiently models global-local dependencies in both spatial and frequency domains. SWA consists of two sparse NL attentions: Intra-Wavelet Attention (IWA) for global context within each wavelet sub-band, and Cross-Wavelet Attention (CWA) for local correlations across sub-bands. By operating on compact wavelet representations, SWA drastically reduces computational complexity. Moreover, we introduce stochastic aggregation to capture the one-to-many nature of SR, enabling diverse predictions and uncertainty estimation. A subsequent uncertainty reduction algorithm improves generalization to out-of-distribution images. Experiments demonstrate that SWA achieves state-of-the-art performance with higher efficiency.

title: Comparative Benchmarking of 2D LiDAR SLAM Algorithms with ROS 2 on Raspberry Pi 5
authors: Ulas Sahin, Gokturk Can, Ezgi Altiok, Ibrahim Cavdar, Necip Gozuacik
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: Mobile robotics increasingly relies on SLAM for robust autonomous navigation. While many algorithms exist, systematic comparisons within the ROS 2 framework under real-world conditions remain limited. This study addresses this gap by benchmarking three widely used 2D LiDAR-based methods-GMapping, Hector SLAM, and Cartographer-on a wheeled mobile robot. Using both simulation and on-device experiments, we evaluate mapping accuracy, localization quality, and computational efficiency. Results show that Cartographer achieves the highest accuracy in structured environments, Hector SLAM demonstrates robustness without odometry, and GMapping performs reliably only in small-scale settings. These findings highlight trade-offs relevant to embedded deployment. The main contributions are: (i) a reproducible evaluation pipeline on ROS 2, (ii) quantitative analysis of accuracy versus resource usage on Raspberry Pi 5, and (iii) practical guidelines for algorithm selection in autonomous systems. This work advances the understanding of ROS 2-based SLAM and supports informed deployment in robotics applications.

title: $\mathbb{F}_{q}$-Linear Qudit Data Syndrome Codes
authors: Goli Nagamani, Pradeep Kiran Sarvepalli
publish: 2026 National Conference on Communications (NCC)
abstract: Quantum codes which can correct both errors in the data as well as in the syndromes are called quantum data syndrome codes. An important framework for constructing quantum data syndrome codes is due to Ashikhmin et al. Their approach can be used to construct a quantum data syndrome code for any stabilizer code. While some constructions are known there are not many families of quantum data syndrome codes known. Furthermore, there has not been much work in the construction of qudit data syndrome codes. In this paper, we extend the framework of Ashikhmin et al. to the qudit setting focusing on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbb{F}_{q}$</tex>-linear codes. Then we propose a systematic construction for CSS type data syndrome codes. We then construct a few families of data syndrome codes based on cyclic codes including the ReedSolomon codes, and BCH codes.

title: MIDI-LLaMA: An Instruction-Following Multimodal LLM for Symbolic Music Understanding
authors: Meng Yang, Jon McCormack, Maria Teresa Llano, Wanchao Su, Chao Lei
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Recent advances in multimodal large language models (MLLM) for audio music have demonstrated strong capabilities in music understanding, yet symbolic music, a fundamental representation of musical structure, remains unexplored. In this work, we introduce MIDI-LLaMA, the first instruction-following MLLM for symbolic music understanding. Our approach aligns the MIDI encoder MusicBERT and Llama-3-8B via a two-stage pipeline comprising feature alignment and instruction tuning. To support training, we design a scalable annotation pipeline that annotates GiantMIDI-Piano with fine-grained metadata, resulting in a MIDI–text dataset. Compared with the baseline trained on converting MIDI into ABC notation under the same instruction-tuning procedure, MIDI-LLaMA substantially outperforms in captioning and semantic alignment in question answering. Human evaluation further confirms the advantages of MIDI-LLaMA in music understanding, emotion recognition, creativity, and overall preference. These findings demonstrate that incorporating symbolic music into large language models enhances their capacity for musical understanding.

title: Parametric Analysis of Feature Specific Neural Coding During Music Imagery and Perception
authors: Aditi Jha, Derek Lomas, Krishna Miyapuram
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Music cognition transforms auditory signals into perceptual experiences through complex brain processing, yet neuroimaging studies have underrepresented mid-level features like timbre and tonality. This work introduces a parametric General Linear Model (GLM) framework integrating Mel- Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) and Chroma features—established Music Information Retrieval (MIR) tools for spectral envelope and tonal content— directly into fMRI analysis. Analyzing datasets evaluating perception vs. imagery in trained musicians, we reveal: (1) a motor-executive network dominant in imagery, with Chroma driving subcortical sequencing hubs; (2) functional dissociation in auditory cortex, with primary regions exclusive to perception and higher- order areas persistent in imagery; and (3) feature- specific codes during perception, linking MFCC to spectral analysis and Chroma to tonal prediction, with overlapping auditory hubs but distinct spatial extents. This MIR-neuroimaging fusion demonstrates state- dependent neural dynamics, enabling precise decomposition of auditory processing hierarchies and advancing models for music-based neurotherapeutics and brain-computer interfaces.

title: Compressive Spatial Channel Estimation Under IQ Imbalance
authors: Hamed Masoumi, Nitin Jonathan Myers
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In-phase and quadrature-phase (IQ) imbalance in high-frequency systems distorts received measurements, causing standard channel estimation algorithms that ignore this mismatch to fail. In this paper, we develop an augmented compressed sensing (CS) model to jointly estimate the sparse channel and the IQ imbalance (IQI) parameter in the form of an augmented CS vector. This vector exhibits group sparsity, which is exploited using our tailored paired-support orthogonal matching pursuit (PSOMP) algorithm. Finally, the estimate from PSOMP is decomposed to determine the channel and the IQI parameter. Numerical results show that our method achieves better support recovery and a lower error in the estimated channel than the baselines.

title: SAR-Caption Ranker: Optimizing Automatic SAR Image Descriptions via RLAIF
authors: Xinjun Cheng, Qiwei Ma, Junjie Zhu, Xichuan Zhang, Chunping Qiu, Jun Wang, Qiangjuan Huang, Ke Yang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Automatic captioning of Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images is a challenging task due to speckle noise, complex textures, and imaging distortions. Existing vision-language models (VLMs), such as BLIP2, InstructBLIP, LLaVA, and Qwen-VL, demonstrate strong captioning ability in optical imagery but perform suboptimally when directly applied to SAR data. In this paper, we propose a novel framework for SAR image captioning enhanced with Reinforcement Learning with AI Feedback (RLAIF). Our method first generates multiple candidate captions using several pretrained VLMs, then evaluates them with a SAR-specific reward function that measures object relevance, spatial relations, semantic completeness, and linguistic fluency. Based on these reward scores, captions are re-ranked or sampled to produce the final output. Experiments on benchmark SAR datasets show that our approach significantly improves BLEU, CIDEr, and SAR-specific consistency scores compared to direct VLM baselines. The proposed framework is model-agnostic, annotation-free, and provides an efficient pathway to adapt large-scale captioning models to SAR imagery.

title: SURE: Synergistic Uncertainty-Aware Reasoning for Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Conversations
authors: Yiqiang Cai, Chengyan Wu, Bolei Ma, Bo Chen, Yun Xue, Julia Hirschberg, Ziwei Gong
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition in conversations (MERC) requires integrating multimodal signals while being robust to noise and modeling contextual reasoning. Existing approaches often emphasize fusion but overlook uncertainty in noisy features and fine-grained reasoning. We propose SURE (Synergistic Uncertainty-aware REasoning) for MERC, a framework that improves robustness and contextual modeling. SURE consists of three components: an Uncertainty-Aware Mixture-of-Experts module to handle modality-specific noise, an Iterative Reasoning module for multi-turn reasoning over context, and a Transformer Gate module to capture intra- and inter-modal interactions. Experiments on benchmark MERC datasets show that SURE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, demonstrating its effectiveness in robust multimodal reasoning. These results highlight the importance of uncertainty modeling and iterative reasoning in advancing emotion recognition in conversational settings. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: TsqLoRA: Towards Sensitivity and Quality Low-Rank Adaptation for Efficient Fine-Tuning
authors: Yu Chen, Yifei Han, Long Zhang, Yue Du, Bin Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Fine-tuning large pre-trained models for downstream tasks has become a fundamental approach in natural language processing. Fully fine-tuning all model parameters is computationally expensive and memory-intensive, especially in resource-constrained environments. Existing parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods reduce the number of trainable parameters but typically overlook the varying sensitivity of different model layers and the importance of training data. In this work, we propose TsqLoRA, a novel method that integrates data-quality-driven selection with sensitivity-aware low-rank adaptation, consisted of two main components: a quality-aware sampling mechanism for selecting the most informative training data, and a dynamic rank allocation module that adjusts the rank of each layer based on its sensitivity to parameter updates. The experimental results demonstrate that TsqLoRA improves fine-tuning efficiency while maintaining or even improving performance on a variety of NLP tasks. Our code will be available at https://github.com/Benjamin-Ricky/TsqLoRA.

title: Flowiid: Single-Step Intrinsic Image Decomposition Via Latent Flow Matching
authors: Mithlesh Singla, Seema Kumari, Shanmuganathan Raman
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Intrinsic Image Decomposition (IID) separates an image into albedo and shading components. It is a core step in many real-world applications, such as relighting and material editing. Existing IID models achieve good results, but often use a large number of parameters. This makes them costly to combine with other models in real-world settings. To address this problem, we propose a flow matching-based solution. For this, we design a novel architecture, FlowIID, based on latent flow matching. FlowIID combines a VAE-guided latent space with a flow matching module, enabling a stable decomposition of albedo and shading. FlowIID is not only parameter-efficient, but also produces results in a single inference step. Despite its compact design, FlowIID delivers competitive and superior results compared to existing models across various benchmarks. This makes it well-suited for deployment in resource-constrained and real-time vision applications.

title: AGI-CLIP: Multi-Modal LLM Knowledge Transfer For Ai-Generated Image Quality Assessment
authors: Chi Zhang, Xiangfei Sheng, Zhichao Yang, Pengfei Chen, Leida Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of generative models, AI-generated image quality assessment (AGIQA) has become increasingly critical. Existing AGIQA methods primarily rely on CLIP, which suffers from significant domain gaps between CLIP’s natural image training data and AI-generated images, along with insufficient task-specific knowledge. To address these limitations, we propose AGI-CLIP, which incorporates quality-aware knowledge from multi-modal large language models (MLLMs). Specifically, we first design a multi-MLLM judge strategy that employs ensemble and verification mechanisms to generate reliable multi-attribute quality comments. Then we develop Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) multi-attribute adapters to map the initial CLIP into feature spaces that capture representations of quality attributes. Extensive experiments on multiple AGIQA benchmarks demonstrate that AGI-CLIP significantly outperforms existing methods. Data and code will be publicly available https://github.com/1carrot1/AGI-CLIP.

title: Non-Uniform Haze Removal for Remote Sensing Image Based on Wavelet-Domain Haze-Aware Complementary Learning
authors: Yanmeng Liu, Libao Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Non-uniform haze removal in remote sensing image (RSI) remains highly challenging due to the difficulty of modeling spatially varying haze distributions and preserving fine-grained textures. To address these issues, we propose a wavelet-domain haze-aware complementary learning (WHCL) pipeline, which operates primarily in the wavelet domain for more effective, locally adaptive restoration. First, we design a novel haze density analyzer that fuses dark channel, contrast, and color shift cues into haze density maps, providing reliable local conditioning for subsequent restoration. Based on this, we propose a haze-aware complementary modulation framework composed of two cooperating modulators: a global structure modulator (GSM) that adaptively recalibrates low-frequency representations to suppress large-scale atmospheric scattering, and a texture detail modulator (TDM) that selectively amplifies structurally salient high-frequency responses to recover fine-grained textures. Extensive experiments demonstrate that WHCL achieves superior performance in both quantitative metrics and visual quality.

title: Comparison of ROS 2 QoS Policies for Real-Time Quadruped Robot Control
authors: Gokhan Erdemir
publish: 2026 25th International Symposium INFOTEH-JAHORINA (INFOTEH)
abstract: Real-time legged robot control requires communication mechanisms that balance reliability with strict timing determinism. In high-frequency closed-loop systems, even moderate latency variance can degrade tracking performance and compromise locomotion stability. ROS 2, built on the Data Distribution Service (DDS) standard, provides configurable Quality of Service (QoS) policies that allow fine-grained control over communication behavior. However, selecting appropriate QoS profiles for control-critical data streams remains a practical challenge in wireless robotic deployments. This study presents an experimental evaluation of ROS 2 QoS configurations for real-time quadruped robot control using a Unitree Go2 EDU platform operating at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ H z}$</tex>. Rather than evaluating communication streams in isolation, the analysis focuses specifically on control-critical feedback and command streams within the closed-loop pipeline. Two representative scenarios are considered: high computational load under stable wireless conditions and degraded wireless communication with induced packet loss and jitter. System performance is quantified by end-to-end latency, jitter, packet loss, deadline miss ratio, and control-level metrics such as RMSE, overshoot, and settling time. Experimental results demonstrate that latency dispersion, particularly tail latency and jitter, has a stronger impact on control stability than packet delivery guarantees alone. Best-Effort configurations with limited buffering achieved more consistent timing behavior and improved transient response than strictly Reliable modes under stressed conditions. The findings highlight the importance of timing-aware and stream-specific QoS tuning to preserve stability margins in high-frequency legged robotic systems.

title: Rethinking Change Detection: Benchmarking Multi-Agent Remote Sensing Image Change Understanding
authors: Yuxuan He, Wanlin Liu, Fangling Leng, Yubin Bao, Tiancheng Zhang, Ge Yu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The existing methods for remote sensing image change detection (RS-CD) mostly rely on bi-temporal differencing and mask generation, which localize changes but do not explain them. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) and vision–language models (VLMs) enable multi-modal captioning and visual question answering, yet existing methods remain confined to single-image analysis or isolated modules, lacking a unified framework for multi-temporal understanding. We present a training-free multi-agent system that rethinks the task as multi-modal change understanding rather than mask prediction. A main-Agent coordinates sub-agents: RS-CC-Agent for informative change captions and RS-VQA-Agent for semantic, open-ended answers. A knowledge bridge delivers contextual priors to support VQA reasoning. Experiments show that model outputs closely align with expert judgments; an ablation confirms the knowledge bridge improves VQA accuracy. We also benchmark diverse LLMs/VLMs under a standardized protocol combining automatic and human evaluation, establishing strong and reproducible baselines for multimodal change understanding.

title: Rail-Level Power Profiling of Embedded Video Playback
authors: Sayeh Janani, Muhammad Amin Qureshi, Christian Herglotz
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This work presents an experimental study of power consumption during video playback on an embedded platform with ARM Cortex-A53 processors. We analyze how workloads based on the H.264 and H.265 video coding standards affect power usage across different internal power rails. We perform detailed rail-level monitoring during decoding. The results show that the power of the platform is primarily driven by resolution, with only a marginal difference between H.264 and H.265 decoding. At the subsystem level, the SoC and platform domains dominate overall consumption, while ARM activity grows with resolution, and CPU/HDMI rails remain negligible.

title: TURTLEBENCH: Evaluating Top Language Models via Real-World Yes/No Puzzles
authors: Qingchen Yu, Shichao Song, Ke Fang, Yunfeng Shi, Zifan Zheng, Hanyu Wang, Simin Niu, Zhiyu Li, Yanfang Chen, Xiaoqing Zheng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Existing benchmarks for Large Language Models (LLMs) are often static, knowledge-dependent, or costly, hindering the reliable evaluation of reasoning in dynamic user interactions. To address this, we propose TurtleBench, a novel benchmark derived from real user guesses in an online "Turtle Soup Puzzle" game. This approach yields a dynamic dataset that isolates logical reasoning from background knowledge, mitigating risks of data contamination and model cheating. TurtleBench contains 1,532 annotated user guesses, on which we evaluated nine leading LLMs. Our results show that OpenAI's o1 series models lag behind other top competitors. We hypothesize this stems from limitations in their latent reasoning, such as simplistic Chain-of-Thought (CoT) processes and noise from longer reasoning paths. The dataset and code are publicly available at https://github.com/mazzzystar/TurtleBench.

title: G2LST: Global to Local Stackelberg Decision Model for Computation Offloading Under Mobile Internet of Things
authors: Jiaxuan Yu, Yifu Li, Yijie Wang, Zhigang Chen
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The exponential growth of mobile Internet of Things (IoT) and the surging demand for low-latency streaming applications pose significant challenges for resource-limited edge devices. Edge task offloading has been proposed as a promising paradigm to distribute computationally intensive tasks to nearby edge servers in urban Internet of Things scenarios. Previous studies on edge task offloading have primarily focused on multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL). However, the limited computational power of edge devices constrains their ability to capture global dependencies in complex multi-agent environments. The Global to Local Stackelberg decision model (G2LST) is designed to enable edge terminals to adaptively extract key global dependencies while leveraging local observations. In the MARL driven edge task offloading environment, G2LST operates through a hierarchical infrastructure to facilitate superior task offloading strategies. Extensive experiments on real world datasets demonstrate that G2LST achieves the highest task completion ratio while maximizing computational resource utilization in complex IoT environments.

title: Brain-Score Meets Representational Similarity Analysis: a Methodological Convergence in Model-Brain Alignment
authors: Zhejun Zhang, Wenqing Zhou, Chengyi Zhao, Liang Dong, Lin Zhang, Lei Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Brain-Score (BS) and Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) are two mainstream approaches for evaluating model-brain alignment, based on predictive mapping and geometric similarity paradigms, respectively. Despite widespread adoption, their intrinsic consistency remains unvalidated. Using the DERCo EEG dataset, we systematically examined correspondence between BS and RSA across 47 Large Language Models (LLMs) spanning 0.5B–14B parameters. Our results reveal remarkable convergence: both metrics exhibit consistent inverted U-shaped distributions peaking at intermediate layers, with strong positive correlations within individual models (46/47 significant, mean r = 0.725) and robust aggregate correlation across 1,496 observations (r = 0.403, p < 0.001). These findings were further validated on the Podcast ECoG dataset. Our architecture-agnostic, cross-modality findings demonstrate that predictive mapping and geometric similarity capture highly overlapping aspects of model-brain alignment, establishing empirical foundations for unified NeuroAI frameworks. Code is available at https: //github.com/Mochizuki-BUPT/Brain-Score-Meets-RSA.

title: InstructAudio: Unified Speech and Music Generation with Natural Language Instruction
authors: Chunyu Qiang, Kang Yin, Xiaopeng Wang, Yuzhe Liang, Jiahui Zhao, Ruibo Fu, Tianrui Wang, Cheng Gong, Chen Zhang, Longbiao Wang, Jianwu Dang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Text-to-speech (TTS) and text-to-music (TTM) models face significant limitations in instruction-based control. TTS systems usually depend on reference audio for timbre, offer only limited text-level attribute control, and rarely support dialogue generation. TTM systems are constrained by input conditioning requirements that depend on expert knowledge annotations. The high heterogeneity of these input control conditions makes them difficult to joint modeling with speech synthesis. Despite sharing common acoustic modeling characteristics, these two tasks have long been developed independently, leaving open the challenge of achieving unified modeling through natural language instructions. We introduce InstructAudio, a unified framework that enables instruction-based (natural language descriptions) control of acoustic attributes including timbre (gender, age), paralinguistic (emotion, style, accent), and musical (genre, instrument, rhythm, atmosphere). It supports expressive speech, music, and dialogue generation in English and Chinese. The model employs joint and single diffusion transformer layers with a standardized instruction-phoneme input format, trained on 50K hours of speech and 20K hours of music data, enabling multi-task learning and crossmodal alignment. Fig. 1 visualizes performance comparisons with mainstream TTS and TTM models, demonstrating that InstructAudio achieves optimal results on most metrics. To our best knowledge, InstructAudio represents the first instruction-controlled framework unifying speech and music generation. Audio samples are available at: https://qiangchunyu.github.io/InstructAudio/

title: Camera-Trajectory-Driven Signed Distance Fields for Automated Object Isolation
authors: Yusuf Şevki Günaydın, Emre Demirbaş, Ömer Faruk Özüyağlı, Fatih Nar
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: Accurate isolated object reconstruction from drone footage is often challenging because of background clutter, sparse camera coverage, and viewpoint limitations. We introduce a geometric method in this study that leverages the drone's camera positions and viewing directions, extracted from Structure-fromMotion, to construct a kernel-based Signed Distance Field. This field explicitly encodes whether points lie inside, on, or outside the drone's flight route, providing a precise spatial representation of the trajectory. By evaluating the SDF at candidate points, the method can identify regions corresponding to the object of interest versus the surrounding environment. In typical SfM pipelines such as COLMAP, unbounded scenes or spiral drone captures often lead to sparse reconstructions that unintentionally include distant background structures, which in turn causes Gaussian Splats to represent unwanted terrain or sky regions. Our trajectory-aligned SDF mitigates this issue by enabling posttraining filtering of Gaussian Splat reconstructions, removing background splats (e.g., terrain/sky/background buildings) without changing the original splatting pipeline. We validate the approach on synthetic spiral trajectories that include wind and sensor disturbances, as well as on real drone flights. Results demonstrate that the field generates smooth, consistent spatial representations, offering a foundation for trajectory-informed 3-Dimensional reconstruction, automated object isolation, and targeted scene editing. This method provides a practical and flexible framework for future drone-based data capture and geometric analysis.

title: Negative-Aware Routing Network with Adversarial Knowledge Injection for Efficient LLM Adaptation
authors: Xingchen Li, Qibo Qiu, Chao Sun, Yilin Zhang, Yuchao Yan, Li Yu, Jian Jiang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) has been widely used in Large Language Model (LLM) adaptation for its ability to scale model capacity with limited computation. However, conventional routing strategies assign only non-negative weights to experts, which may overlook the adversarial effects of conflicting expert knowledge. We argue that negative routing scores can indicate such conflicts and should be preserved to model complex token–expert interactions. Our empirical results further show that retaining negative signs of routing weights can improve model performance. On this basis, we propose a novel framework, NOVA, consisting of two key components: a Negative-Aware Routing Network (NARN), which embeds tokens and experts into a shared knowledge space and leverages cosine similarity to capture both positive and negative influences; and an Adversarial Knowledge Injection (AKI) module, which synthesizes adversarial token signals to explicitly guide the model in distinguishing expert contributions. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks verify the effectiveness of our method.

title: Deep Image Prior with L0 Gradient Regularizer for Image Smoothing
authors: Nhat Thanh Tran, Kevin Bui, Jack Xin
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Image smoothing is a fundamental image processing operation that preserves the underlying structure, such as strong edges and contours, and removes minor details and textures in an image. Many image smoothing algorithms rely on computing local window statistics or solving an optimization problem. Recent state-of-the-art methods leverage deep learning, but they require a carefully curated training dataset. Because constructing a proper training dataset for image smoothing is challenging, we propose DIP-ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>, a deep image prior framework that incorporates the ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> gradient regularizer. This framework can perform high-quality image smoothing without any training data. To properly minimize the associated loss function that has the nonconvex, nonsmooth ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> "norm", we develop an alternating direction method of multipliers algorithm that utilizes an off-the-shelf ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> gradient minimization solver. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed DIP-ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> outperforms many image smoothing algorithms in edge-preserving image smoothing and JPEG artifact removal.

title: Expectation Propagation Detector Exploiting Overlapping Block Structures for Highly Correlated MIMO Systems
authors: Kabuto Arai, Takumi Yoshida, Takumi Takahashi, Koji Ishibashi
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper proposes an expectation propagation (EP)-based detector, termed overlapping block partitioning EP (OvEP), for highly correlated multiple-input-multiple-output (MIMO) systems. In this method, large-scale measurements are partitioned into partially overlapping blocks, for which low-complexity linear minimum mean square error (LMMSE) filters are designed. The filter outputs are then combined to generate tentative estimates. By subtracting the outputs of overlapping parts from those of the block parts in the combining process, inter-block correlation induced by high spatial correlation can be effectively suppressed, leading to an improved detection performance. Numerical results demonstrate that OvEP attains performance comparable to LMMSE-EP without requiring full-dimensional matrix inversion.

title: Linear Cross-Attention Guided Feature Pyramid Networks for Crowd Counting
authors: Hao-Yuan Ma, Li Zhang, Shuai Shi
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Drastic scale variations and the semantic gap between features from different network depths pose significant challenges for crowd counting. To address this issue, we conceptualize these features as distinct pseudo-modalities and propose a Linear Cross-Attention Module (LCAM) to effectively alleviate the semantic gap between them. Build upon LCAM, we develop a Linear Cross-Attention guided Feature Pyramid Network (LCA-FPN), which achieves superior multi-scale feature fusion. We further develop Visual Mamba for Crowd Counting (VMambaCC), a novel architecture leveraging LCA-FPN for feature alignment and Mamba’s selective state space model to efficiently capture long-range dependencies. Extensive experiments on four challenging datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. VMambaCC achieves competitive performance, demonstrating substantial improvements in counting and localization, particularly in highly congested scenes. Code is available at: https://github.com/Jason-Mar1/VMambaCC.

title: Toward Faithful Explanations in Acoustic Anomaly Detection
authors: Maab Elrashid, Anthony Deschênes, Cem Subakan, Mirco Ravanelli, Rémi Georges, Michael Morin
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Interpretability is essential for user trust in real-world anomaly detection applications. However, deep learning models, despite their strong performance, often lack transparency. In this work, we study the interpretability of autoencoder-based models for audio anomaly detection, by comparing a standard autoencoder (AE) with a mask autoencoder (MAE) in terms of detection performance and interpretability. We applied several attribution methods, including error maps, saliency maps, SmoothGrad, Integrated Gradients, GradSHAP, and Grad-CAM. Although MAE shows a slightly lower detection, it consistently provides more faithful and temporally precise explanations, suggesting a better alignment with true anomalies. To assess the relevance of the regions highlighted by the explanation method, we propose a perturbation-based faithfulness metric that replaces them with their reconstructions to simulate normal input. Our findings, based on experiments in a real industrial scenario, highlight the importance of incorporating interpretability into anomaly detection pipelines and show that masked training improves explanation quality without compromising performance.

title: Trustworthy and Privacy-Preserving Perceptual Hashing with Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Client-Side Content Scanning
authors: Dehong Meng, Zhangshuang Guan, Peng Zhao, Hongbing Cheng, Zhiguo Wan
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In client-server applications such as copyright protection and content moderation, learning-based perceptual hashing compresses images into compact binary codes whose Hamming distances approximate perceptual similarity. Clients then transmit these codes to servers for comparison. However, this approach faces dual challenges: algorithmically, how to effectively balance robustness and discriminability while mitigating bit imbalance issues; protocol-wise, transmitting these hashes compromises client privacy through content inference and cross-platform user tracking. To address these challenges, we propose a trustworthy privacy-preserving framework that integrates deep hashing with zero-knowledge proofs. The framework comprises: (1) A robust deep hashing module that generates discriminative binary codes by optimizing a composite objective function composed of the Angular Triplet and quantization losses, while using a multi-scale strategy to correct bit imbalance. (2) A privacy-preserving similarity comparison protocol based on Sumcheck and Logarithmic Lookup, which enables clients to locally prove batch Hamming distance relationships against public dataset entries without disclosing their hash values. We conducted comprehensive evaluations to demonstrate the practicality and efficiency of our design compared to existing schemes. Source code is available at https://github.com/mengdehong/zkph.

title: AISHELL6-Whisper: A Chinese Mandarin Audio-Visual Whisper Speech Dataset with Speech Recognition Baselines
authors: Cancan Li, Fei Su, Juan Liu, Hui Bu, Yulong Wan, Hongbin Suo, Ming Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Whisper speech recognition is crucial not only for ensuring privacy in sensitive communications but also for providing a critical communication bridge for patients under vocal restraint and enabling discrete interaction in noise-sensitive environments. The development of Chinese mandarin audio-visual whisper speech recognition is hindered by the lack of large-scale datasets. We present AISHELL6-Whisper, a large-scale open-source audio-visual whisper speech dataset, featuring 30 hours each of whisper speech and parallel normal speech, with synchronized frontal facial videos. Moreover, we propose an audio-visual speech recognition (AVSR) baseline based on the Whisper-Flamingo framework, which integrates a parallel training strategy to align embeddings across speech types, and employs a projection layer to adapt to whisper speech’s spectral properties. The model achieves a Character Error Rate (CER) of 4.13% for whisper speech and 1.11% for normal speech in the test set of our dataset, and establishes new state-of-the-art results on the wTIMIT benchmark. The dataset and the AVSR baseline codes are open-sourced at https://zutm.github.io/AISHELL6-Whisper.

title: A Unitary Quantum Process Tomography Method Based on a Density Matrix Diagonalization
authors: Yannick Deville, Alain Deville
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Quantum Process Tomography (QPT) methods aim at identifying, i.e. estimating, a quantum process. QPT is a major quantum information processing tool, since it especially allows one to experimentally characterize the actual behavior of quantum gates, that may be used as the building blocks of quantum computers. We here consider unitary (possibly dense, i.e. without sparsity constraints) processes, which corresponds to isolated systems. Moreover, we develop a QPT method applicable to a significant number of qubits and hence to a high state space dimension, which allows one to tackle more complex problems. Using the unitarity of the process allows us to propose a method that first achieves part of QPT by performing an eigenanalysis of the estimated density matrix of a process output. We then complete the QPT by using another state differently. The relevance of our method is validated by means of simulations.

title: Establishing Stochastic Object Models from Noisy Data via Ambient Measurement-Integrated Diffusion
authors: Xiaoning Lei, Jianwei Sun, Wenhao Cai, Xichen Xu, Yanshu Wang, Hu Gao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Task-based assessments of image quality (IQ) are critical for evaluating medical imaging systems, which must account for randomness, including anatomical variability. Stochastic object models (SOMs) provide a statistical description of such variability. However, conventional mathematical SOMs fail to capture realistic anatomy, while most data-driven approaches typically require clean data, which are rarely available in clinical tasks. To address this challenge, we propose an unsupervised method called Ambient Measurement-Integrated Diffusion (AMID) with noise decoupling, which establishes clean SOMs directly from noisy measurements. AMID introduces a measurement-integrated strategy aligning measurement noise with the diffusion trajectory, and explicitly models coupling between measurement and diffusion noise across steps, and thus an ambient-consistency loss is thus designed based on it to learn clean SOMs. Experiments on real CT and mammography datasets show that AMID outperforms existing methods in generation fidelity and yields more reliable task-based IQ evaluation, demonstrating its potential for unsupervised medical imaging analysis.

title: MAPEX: A Multi-Agent Pipeline for Keyphrase Extraction Using LLMs
authors: Liting Zhang, Shiwan Zhao, Aobo Kong, Qicheng Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Keyphrase extraction is a fundamental task in natural language processing. However, existing unsupervised prompt-based methods with Large Language Models (LLMs) typically rely on simplistic prompt designs, which limit their robustness across document lengths and hinder the full exploitation of LLMs’ reasoning and generation capabilities. To address these challenges, we propose MAPEX, the first framework that introduces multi-agent collaboration into keyphrase extraction. MAPEX coordinates an LLM-based Extractor and an Enhancer, leveraging modules for role-based prompting, topic guidance, knowledge augmentation, and post-processing. A dual-path strategy enables dynamic adaptation to document length: short texts are processed via knowledge-driven extraction, while long texts benefit from topic-guided extraction. Extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets across three different LLMs demonstrate its strong generalization and universality, outperforming the state-of-the-art (SOTA) unsupervised method by 2.44% and standard LLM baselines by 4.01% in F1@5 on average. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/NKU-LITI/MAPEX.

title: Towards Language-Independent Face-Voice Association with Multimodal Foundation Models
authors: Aref Farhadipour, Teodora Vukovic, Volker Dellwo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper describes the UZH-CL system submitted to the FAME2026 Challenge. The challenge focuses on cross-modal verification under unique multilingual conditions, specifically unseen and unheard languages. Our approach investigates two distinct architectures, consisting of a baseline dual-encoder system trained from scratch using contrastive and orthogonal projection losses, and a foundation model approach leveraging ImageBind with LoRA. To address the data scarcity and language constraints of the challenge, we curated an external Arabic dataset from VoxBlink. Our best-performing system, ImageBind-LoRA, demonstrates remarkable cross-lingual generalization: despite being fine-tuned exclusively on Arabic audio, it achieved an EER of 24.73% on the evaluation set (English and German), securing 2nd place in the competition.

title: Semantic Mining and Cross-Center Synergy for Cross-Modal Person Re-Identification
authors: Yanyan Lv, Zhihao Chen
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification (VI-ReID) involves matching pedestrian images across visible and infrared modalities, which faces a substantial modality discrepancy due to their distinct imaging principles. While existing methods attempt to reduce this discrepancy through image translation or feature alignment, they often overlook high-level semantic consistency and insufficiently explore intra-class and inter-class relationships for cross-modal metric learning. To address these issues, we propose a novel Semantic Mining and Cross-Center Synergy (SMCCS) network. Our framework introduces three key components: a Semantic-Aware Interactive (SAI) module that adaptively focuses on identity-relevant high-level semantic regions across modalities; a Fine-Grained Cross-Alignment (FGCA) loss that encourages a compact intra-class distribution in the shared feature space; and an Enhanced Cross-Triplet (ECT) loss that improves inter-class discriminability of cross-modal representations. Extensive experiments on two standard datasets, SYSU-MM01 and RegDB, demonstrate that our method achieves the state-of-the-art performance. Our code is available at https://github.com/GuYan66/ICASSP2026-SMCCS.

title: Resolution-Progressive Diffusion Model for Pansharpening
authors: Shaowu Wu, Keyan Jin, Lihui Chen, Lihua Jian, Xingyu Liu, Liwei Jing, Xiaoguang Niu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Diffusion models have gained widespread adoption in pansharpening tasks due to their superior generative capabilities and robust performance. However, existing diffusion model-based pansharpening methods primarily focus on conditional denoising. These methods overlook the fundamental objective of pansharpening: the explicit enhancement of spatial resolution, which results in excessive sampling steps and reduced efficiency. To address this limitation, we propose a Resolution-Progressive Diffusion Model (RPDiff) that reduces the sampling steps of diffusion processes and inherently aligns with the pansharpening task by progressively reconstructing images at increasing resolutions. Our model employs Implicit Neural Representation (INR) to enable continuous resolution progression and incorporates a multi-branch fusion architecture. The model first employs three encoders to extract spectral information, spatial information, and noise observation features from source images, respectively. Subsequently, the extracted features are processed through implicit neural representations to construct feature maps at the next timestep resolution. Finally, a decoding fusion module integrates spectral and spatial information to produce the fused high-resolution multispectral image. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed model achieves superior performance in both visual quality and quantitative metrics compared to state-of-the-art approaches. Project page at: https://github.com/ShaowuCh/RPDiff

title: RIR-Former: Coordinate-Guided Transformer for Continuous Reconstruction of Room Impulse Responses
authors: Shaoheng Xu, Chunyi Sun, Jihui Aimee Zhang, Prasanga Samarasinghe, Thushara Abhayapala
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Room impulse responses (RIRs) are essential for many acoustic signal processing tasks, yet measuring them densely across space is often impractical. In this work, we propose RIR-Former, a grid-free, one-step feed-forward model for RIR reconstruction. By introducing a sinusoidal encoding module into a transformer backbone, our method effectively incorporates microphone position information, enabling interpolation at arbitrary array locations. Furthermore, a segmented multi-branch decoder is designed to separately handle early reflections and late reverberation, improving reconstruction across the entire RIR. Experiments on diverse simulated acoustic environments demonstrate that RIR-Former consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in terms of normalized mean square error (NMSE) and cosine distance (CD), under varying missing rates and array configurations. These results highlight the potential of our approach for practical deployment and motivate future work on scaling from randomly spaced linear arrays to complex array geometries, dynamic acoustic scenes, and real-world environments.

title: FCLIP: Clip-Driven Multi-Level Fusion for Training-Free Zero-Shot Semantic Segmentation in Remote Sensing Images
authors: Dengji Zhang, Lulin Li, Shijie Wang, Yue Li, Pin Tao, Shiying Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Open-vocabulary zero-shot semantic segmentation in remote sensing images is critical for natural resource management and ecological monitoring. Existing CLIP-based methods often suffer from reduced accuracy for two reasons: first, a trade-off between global and local features; second, noise from low-level features. We propose Conditional Neighborhood Attention (CNA) to address these issues. It applies Q-Q, K-K, and V-V self-relations in the last attention layer of the CLIP image encoder, using a local Gaussian bias to focus on nearby regions without disrupting the original distribution. Furthermore, we propose a Multi-level Static Adaptive Fusion framework (MSAF) which integrates global, local, and low-level features in a complementary manner. Without training, the framework leverages simple and efficient static weights to preserve global semantic consistency and enhance local discriminability. It also suppresses noise from low-level features. Comprehensive evaluations demonstrate that our method achieves an average of 1.0% improvement in mIoU over existing state-of-the-art approaches across six benchmark datasets for remote sensing semantic segmentation, and exhibits strong cross-domain generalization capability. Source code and model weight are available at https://github.com/305840574/FCLIP.

title: Metatoolagent: Towards Generalizable Tool Usage in LLMs Through Meta-Learning
authors: Zheng Fang, Wolfgang Mayer, Zeyu Zhang, Jian Wang, Hong-Yu Zhang, Wanli Li, Zaiwen Feng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Tool learning is increasingly important for large language models (LLMs) to effectively coordinate and utilize a diverse set of tools in order to solve complex real-world tasks. By selecting and integrating appropriate tools, LLMs extend their capabilities beyond pure language understanding to perform specialized functions. However, existing methods for tool selection often focus on limited tool sets and struggle to generalize to novel tools encountered in practical deployments. To address these challenges, we introduce a comprehensive dataset spanning 7 domains, containing 155 tools and 9,377 question-answer pairs, which simulates realistic integration scenarios. Additionally, we propose MetaToolAgent (MTA), a meta-learning approach designed to improve cross-tool generalization. Experimental results show that MTA significantly outperforms baseline methods on unseen tools, demonstrating its promise for building flexible and scalable systems that require dynamic tool coordination.

title: DCR-MUCL:Dual Granularity Consistency Routing Multimodal Unified Contrastive Learning For Rumor Detection Network
authors: Xinyu Zheng, Yicun Liu, Zhengnan Qiao, Xianguo Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Social network rumors pose a threat to social stability, and existing methods have issues such as redundant noise in comments, insufficient diversity in multimodal features, and inadequate mining of sample similarities and differences. To address these problems, we propose the Dual Granularity Consistency Routing Multimodal Unified Contrastive Learning for Rumor Detection Network (DCR-MUCL). This network first enhances modality dependencies through a text-visual interactive feature integration module. Then, it compresses redundancy and filters noise using a dual-granularity consistency routing hypergraph framework, encoding social associations. Next, it strengthens interactions with a cross-modal co-attention mechanism. Finally, it designs a multimodal unified virtual augmented contrastive learning network to explicitly model the collaborative anomalies of the three modalities, combining real labels and virtual features to enhance contrastive learning, which is then input into a classifier for detection. Experiments show that this method significantly surpasses existing best methods.

title: Butterfly Transformer for Lightweight Image Restoration
authors: Mingyang Yu, Zhijian Wu, Dingjiang Huang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Image restoration (IR) is a classic vision task that aims to recover clear images from degraded inputs. Deep learning methods have achieved remarkable progress, but often at the cost of excessive model complexity, which hinders deployment in real-world applications. We propose the Butterfly Transformer (ButterFormer), a novel lightweight Transformer designed for efficient image restoration. It contains two main components: (1) Butterfly Self-Attention (BSA), which enables comprehensive information representation through mutual propagation and interaction between spatial and channel operators; and (2) Multi-order Gated Network (MGN), which enhances local perception and supports coarse-to-fine context aggregation. Extensive experiments on image super-resolution, deraining, and low-light enhancement demonstrate that ButterFormer achieves competitive performance with significantly fewer parameters, making it practical for resource-constrained scenarios.

title: Extended Phase Shift Modulation for Dual Active Bridge Converters in Battery-Based DC Microgrids
authors: Kecheng Zhao, Priya Singh Bhakar, Shamsodin Taheri, Edris Pouresmaeil
publish: 2026 25th International Symposium INFOTEH-JAHORINA (INFOTEH)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of power electronic devices and high penetration of renewable energy sources and battery energy storage systems, the dual active bridge converter is widely adopted in DC microgrids. It provides high voltage gain, soft switching capability, and galvanic isolation between the primary and secondary sides. Among different modulation techniques, extended phase shift modulation has been shown to effectively control power losses and improve energy transfer performance in dual active bridge converters. In this paper, an extended phase shift modulation method presented that continuously adjusts the switching pulses of a converter enable power transfer from a battery to a DC bus. The proposed approach provides the optimal internal and external phase shifts corresponding to the reduced inductor peak current through peak current control. A feedforward control mechanism maps the phase shifts to their associated current references. These references are processed through an inductor current comparator and logic circuitry to generate switching pulses. This modulation improves power transfer efficiency and reduces inductor peak current when compared with conventional single phase shift control. The advantage of the proposed approach is verified by simulations results conducted in the MATLAB/Simulink environment.

title: Microservices Optimization with Container Orchestration and Auto-Scaling Strategies in Cloud Environments
authors: Arun Kumar Rajamandrapu, Aparna Krishna Penumatcha, Surya Teja Meesala, Ajay Krishna Borra, Nagireddy Vasipally, Sathish Kuppan Pandurangan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Microservices have become the dominant framework for developing scalable, resilient cloud-native applications, albeit optimizing such systems under workloads that are inherently dynamic is surprisingly difficult. Current orchestration and auto-scaling approaches are typically static threshold-based, resulting in inefficient uses of cost, latency, or energy. This paper addresses the problem with an adaptive approach that combines container orchestration, workload forecasting, and reinforcement-learning defined auto-scaling. A heterogeneous Kubernetes cluster was used to evaluate the approach against 5 state-of-the-art methods for an e-commerce application workload. The framework delivered a 20% latency reduction, a 15% throughput increase, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times$</tex> faster provisioning signal (or scale) load response, a 12% lower cost, and SLA compliance of 99.5%. The results demonstrate how intelligent orchestration and predictive workload scaling could address the challenges that cloud-native resilience, efficiency, and sustainability pose. Building from the conclusions, proactive, AI-based frameworks can deliver improved performance for cloud-native systems and help motivate further research in the fields of multi-cloud, edge-cloud, and energy-aware orchestration.

title: Mind the Shift: Using Delta SSL Embeddings to Enhance Child ASR
authors: Zilai Wang, Natarajan Balaji Shankar, Kaiyuan Zhang, Zihan Wang, Abeer Alwan
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) models have achieved impressive results across many speech tasks, yet child automatic speech recognition (ASR) remains challenging due to limited data and pretraining domain mismatch. Fine-tuning SSL models on child speech induces shifts in the representation space. We hypothesize that delta SSL embeddings, the differences between embeddings from a fine-tuned model and those from its pre-trained counterpart, encode task-specific information that complements fine-tuned features from another SSL model. We evaluate multiple fusion strategies on the MyST children’s corpus with different models. Results show that delta embedding fusion with WavLM yields up to a 10% relative WER reduction for HuBERT and a 4.4% reduction for W2V2, compared to fine-tuned embedding fusion. Notably, fusing WavLM with delta W2V2 embeddings achieves a WER of 9.64, setting a new state-of-the-art among SSL models on the MyST corpus. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of delta embeddings and highlight feature fusion as a promising direction for advancing child ASR.

title: Navigating Modality Uncertainty: Modality-Interaction Enhanced Mixture-of-Experts for Multi-Modal Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Hanting Shen, Bin Chen, Zhangtao Cheng, Kaiju Li, Jia Chen, Ting Zhong, Fan Zhou
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multi-modal knowledge graph completion (MMKGC) aims to infer missing factual triples by integrating structural information with rich multi-modal data such as text and images. While existing methods design various multi-modal fusion mechanisms, they largely overlook the inherent disparities in modality quality, as entity modalities across triples differ in informativeness, uncertainty, and noise, and they fail to address sample-specific modality uncertainty, ultimately resulting in suboptimal performance. To address this limitation, we propose MIMoE, a Modality-Interaction Enhanced Mixture-of-Experts framework with an uncertainty-aware router that adaptively integrates heterogeneous modalities. Specifically, MIMoE adopts a two-stage training paradigm: intra-modal pre-training to capture modality-specific semantics, followed by inter-modal post-training to model fine-grained cross-modal interactions with uncertainty-aware adaptive routing. Extensive experiments on three benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of MIMoE.

title: Construction of Binary Sequence Pairs with Equal Periodic Autocorrelation
authors: Soo-Chang Pei, Kuo-Wei Chang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We study the construction of sequence pairs with equal autocorrelation in the periodic setting. Existing approaches are primarily suited for the aperiodic case, but they are limited to certain sequence lengths and often yield only trivial pairs from a periodic perspective. To address these limitations, we propose two new constructions of nontrivial equal periodic autocorrelation pairs: one applicable to sequences of even length and another based on difference sets. These results broaden the framework for understanding equal autocorrelation sequences and suggest new directions for future research.

title: DAAGNet: Depth-Adaptive Anchor Graph for Weakly-Supervised Crowd Counting
authors: Yu Lei, Xiaomin Wang, Lei Wang, Yuxiao Wang, Wolin Liang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Crowd counting technology provides critical data support for a smart city. However, existing weakly-supervised methods are still challenged by perspective effects. Distant people appear small and blend into the background, while large, nearby people are sparse, both of which contribute to a decrease in count accuracy. We observe that the cause of this problem lies in the widespread neglect of depth, a critical geometric prior. To address this issue, we introduce Depth-Adaptive Anchor Graph for weakly-supervised Crowd Counting (DAAGNet), which explicitly injects metric depth into the pipeline. Specifically, with the help of Metric3D, we derive a compact horizontal/vertical-averaged descriptor that captures horizontal and vertical depth gradients as stable, low-resolution geometry. These cues guide the encoder–decoder to produce cell-wise node embeddings.
