title: AI-Augmented OBE Framework for French Listening-Speaking Instruction: A Data-Driven Adaptive Learning System with Real-Time Feedback
authors: Yunhan Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Individual and adaptive approaches to teaching a foreign language should be employed to develop effective listening and speaking skills in the foreign language. The suggested paper presents an outcome-oriented education (OBE) model of learning on how to teach French listening and speaking, complemented by the use of AI and a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based model of Speech Recognition and Natural Language Processing (NLP) as a real-time assessment and feedback mechanism. The system documents oral learner feedback, evaluates oral pronunciation, oral fluency, and oral comprehension, and provides corrective feedback to learners on an individual basis in the form of feedbacks that are aligned with the established predetermined OBE objectives. An adaptive learning engine is a reinforcement-based engine, which modifies the difficulty of the task, suggests particular exercises, and adjusts dynamically the learning paths according to the performance of the learner. The teachers will have the opportunity of viewing detailed visualizations of the learner progress and they have the ability to initiate appropriate interventions and make instructional decisions on the basis of the data.

title: Adapting HFMCA to Graph Data: Self-Supervised Learning for Generalizable fMRI Representations
authors: Jakub Frąc, Alexander Schmatz, Qiang Li, Guido Van Wingen, Shujian Yu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) analysis faces significant challenges due to limited dataset sizes and domain variability between studies. Traditional self-supervised learning methods inspired by computer vision often rely on positive and negative sample pairs, which can be problematic for neuroimaging data where defining appropriate contrasts is non-trivial. We propose adapting a recently developed Hierarchical Functional Maximal Correlation Algorithm (HFMCA) to graph-structured fMRI data, providing a theoretically grounded approach that measures statistical dependence via density ratio decomposition in a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS), and applies HFMCA-based pretraining to learn robust and generalizable representations. Evaluations across five neuroimaging datasets demonstrate that our adapted method produces competitive embeddings for various classification tasks and enables effective knowledge transfer to unseen datasets. Codebase and supplementary material can be found here: https://github.com/fr30/mri-eigenencoder

title: Digital Transformation of Audit Practices Through AI and Information Technology: A Case Study of Automated Compliance Checking in Cloud-Based ERP Systems
authors: Zhang Jinyi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: It has become a reality that a significant amount of cloud-based Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems is causing the digitization of the audit procedures. The traditional auditing processes are extremely reliant on a manual audit and set rules that fail to govern the massive and dynamic ERP data. The paper provides a very simple hybrid audit automation system, which entails Rule-Based Compliance Checking and Random Forest (RB-RF) in carrying out automated compliance audit in ERP systems in the cloud. The proposed strategy will employ preset audit guidelines founded on regulatory and organizational policy that will be utilized to filter blatant cases of meeting policy. Subsequently, the residual transactional and access log data is subsequently processed with the assistance of a Random Forest machine learning model that will be used to detect undisclosed non-compliant patterns and anomalies. The rule-based layer, which can be interpreted and regulated, and the object detecting accuracy of the Random Forest model are optimized to acquire complex relations between past audit data. Key aspects such as frequency of transaction, authorization and authorization mismatch and access irregularities are used to model training. To ensure continued audit and scalable compliance auditing, the hybrid framework will be implemented in a cloud ERP background. The results of the case study demonstrate that the proposed RB-RF approach helps to reduce false positives, improve the precision of the compliance detection, and reduce the number of efforts, which must be employed in the manual auditing by far compared to the application of standalone rules-based methods. The experiment confirms the fact that simple hybrid approach can be applied to make audits more efficient and reliable in cloud ERP systems in the recent times.

title: EBAD-GS: Deblurring Gaussian Splatting with Event-Driven Bundle Adjustment
authors: Yufei Deng, Yuanjian Wang, Chenwei Tang, Jiahao Fan, Rong Xiao, Jiancheng Lv
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We propose Deblurring Gaussian Splatting with Event-driven Bundle Adjustment (EBAD-GS), a method for high-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from dual-modal data comprising event streams and severely blurred images. Unlike conventional RGB-only approaches, EBAD-GS leverages the high temporal resolution of events to accurately recover camera trajectories and capture radiance dynamics during exposure. Based on a physical motion blur model, a blur loss enforces consistency between the real blurred image and the motion-blurred image synthesized by integrating latent sharp frames over the exposure interval, while event-based supervision constrains temporal brightness changes. Consistency is further reinforced through an Event-based Double Integral (EDI) prior, enhancing fine details and textures in the reconstructed 3D scene. Experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that EBAD-GS achieves superior cam-era pose estimation and high-quality multi-view 3D reconstruction under severe motion blur and low-light conditions, highlighting the effectiveness of event-guided optimization for motion-deblurred 3D reconstruction.

title: A Novel Arbitrary Recoverable Multi-Image Hiding Algorithm Based On Polarization Theory
authors: Yuan Guo, Chenghao Xie
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We propose a multi-image hiding method based on polarization theory, addressing the structural issue of being unable to individually recover hidden images in multi-image hiding algorithms. By phase encoding multiple hidden images with the carrier image, hidden phases and carrier phases are obtained. These multiple hidden images are embedded into the same carrier image by utilizing different polarization states. Each hidden image can be individually recovered through vector decomposition using its corresponding key. Experimental results show that with 16 hidden images, the PSNR value exceeds 41 dB, while the PSNR of the recovered images exceeds 57 dB. The hiding time is less than 0.4 seconds, and the recovery time is less than 0.2 seconds. This method features low complexity, high capacity, short processing time, excellent recovery performance, strong robustness, and high security, making it suitable for high-quality, flexible extraction of large-capacity images.For experimental simplification, the present study focuses exclusively on grayscale imagery. Nevertheless, the proposed algorithm demonstrates excellent extensibility to color images through RGB three-channel integration, yielding equally satisfactory results.

title: UniSTFormer: Unified Spatio-Temporal Lightweight Transformer for Efficient Skeleton-based Action Recognition
authors: Wenhan Wu, Zhishuai Guo, Chen Chen, Aidong Lu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Skeleton-based action recognition (SAR) has achieved impressive progress with transformer architectures. However, existing methods often rely on complex module compositions and heavy designs, leading to increased parameter counts, high computational costs, and limited scalability. In this paper, we propose a unified spatio-temporal lightweight transformer framework that integrates spatial and temporal modeling within a single attention module, eliminating the need for separate temporal modeling blocks. This approach reduces redundant computations while preserving temporal awareness within the spatial modeling process. Furthermore, we introduce a simplified multi-scale pooling fusion module that combines local and global pooling pathways to enhance the model’s ability to capture fine-grained local movements and overarching global motion patterns. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our lightweight model achieves a superior balance between accuracy and efficiency, reducing parameter complexity by over 58% and lowering computational cost by over 60% compared to state-of-the-art transformer-based baselines, while maintaining competitive recognition performance.

title: Topological Signal Processing for 3D Point Cloud Data
authors: Tiziana Cattai, Stefania Sardellitti, Stefania Colonnese, Sergio Barbarossa
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Our goal in this paper is to apply the topological signal processing (TSP) framework to the analysis of 3D Point Clouds (PCs) represented on simplicial complexes. Building on Discrete Exterior Calculus (DEC) theory for vector fields, we introduce higher-order Laplacian operators that enable the processing of signals over triangular meshes. Unlike traditional approaches, the proposed approach allows us to characterize both color attributes, modeled as 3D vectors on nodes, and geometry, modeled as 3D vectors on the barycenter of each triangle. Then, we show as TSP tools may efficiently be used to sample, recover and filter PCs attributes treating them as edge signals. Numerical results on synthetic PCs demonstrate accurate color reconstruction with robustness to sparse data and geometry refinement in the case of noisy PC coordinates. The proposed approach provides a topology-based representation to characterize the geometry and attributes of PCs.

title: Tackling Data Heterogeneity in Parameter-Efficient Federated Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
authors: Jun Liu, Yunming Liao, Hongli Xu, Yang Xu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs). To reduce costs, FedLoRA integrates low-rank adaptation (LoRA) into FL, exchanging only a small subset of parameters. However, FedLoRA still suffers from heavy local computation and slow convergence under data heterogeneity. To this end, we propose HierFedLoRA, a hierarchical FedLoRA framework that jointly addresses these challenges. HierFedLoRA partitions devices into near-IID groups and adapts their intra-group aggregation frequency to mitigate non-IID effects. Meanwhile, it dynamically assigns diverse fine-tuning depths, i.e., the number of continuous layers tuned from the output, to reduce resource consumption. By jointly optimizing aggregation frequency and fine-tuning depth, HierFedLoRA balances efficiency and convergence. Experiments on a physical testbed with 80 commercial devices show that HierFedLoRA accelerates training by at least 1.97× and improves accuracy by 1.6%–4.1% compared to strong baselines.

title: Physics-Informed Anomaly Detection of Terrain Material Change in Radar Imagery
authors: Abdel Hakiem Mohamed Abbas Mohamed Ahmed, Beth Jelfs, Airlie Chapman, Eric Schoof, Christopher Gilliam
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In this paper we consider physics-informed detection of terrain material change in radar imagery (e.g., shifts in permittivity, roughness or moisture). We propose a lightweight electromagnetic (EM) forward model to simulate bi-temporal single-look complex (SLC) images from labelled material maps. On these data, we derive physics-aware feature stacks that include interferometric coherence, and evaluate unsupervised detectors: Reed-Xiaoli (RX)/Local-RX with robust scatter (Tyler’s M-estimator), Coherent Change Detection (CCD), and a compact convolutional auto-encoder. Monte Carlo experiments sweep dielectric/roughness/moisture changes, number of looks and clutter regimes (gamma vs K-family) at fixed probability of false alarm. Results on synthetic but physically grounded scenes show that coherence and robust covariance markedly improve anomaly detection of material changes; a simple score-level fusion achieves the best F1 in heavy-tailed clutter.

title: Optimized DeepLabV3+ Architecture for Semantic Segmentation
authors: Cristina Cărunta, Călin-Adrian Popa
publish: 2025 27th International Symposium on Symbolic and Numeric Algorithms for Scientific Computing (SYNASC)
abstract: Designing highly accurate lightweight semantic segmentation models is an essential task in the computer vision field due to the necessity to deploy these segmentation models in real-world scenarios. Developing optimized architectures is a challenging research direction since it needs to maintain a right balance between segmentation accuracy and inference speed. Therefore, we consider to rethink an advanced semantic segmentation model, called DeepLabV3+, into a more lightweight approach, enhancing its real-time performance. Through our method, we achieve better optimization across three important performance metrics, i.e., inference speed, GFLOPs (Giga Floating Point Operations), and the number of learnable parameters, while maintaining the accuracy at the same level. Our method is validated through extensive experiments conducted on two urban street scene datasets, namely Cityscapes and CamVid. In particular, on the Cityscapes dataset, our optimized model demonstrates a significant speed-up improvement from 276 FPS to 289 FPS on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$512 \times 1024$</tex> input resolution, a 75% reduction in learnable parameters, and a lower computational load of the model, 10.8 GFLOPs compared to 18.8 GFLOPs, and all these results occur while preserving the 74% accuracy level.

title: When Large Vision-Language Models Meet Person Re-Identification
authors: Qizao Wang, Bin Li, Xiangyang Xue
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) that incorporate visual models and large language models have achieved impressive results across cross-modal understanding and reasoning tasks. In recent years, person re-identification (ReID) has also started to explore cross-modal semantics to improve the accuracy of identity recognition. However, effectively utilizing LVLMs for ReID remains an open challenge. While LVLMs operate under a generative paradigm by predicting the next output word, ReID requires the extraction of discriminative identity features to match pedestrians across cameras. In this paper, we propose LVLM-ReID, a novel framework that harnesses the strengths of LVLMs to promote ReID. Specifically, we employ instructions to guide the LVLM in generating one semantic token that encapsulates key appearance semantics from the person image. This token is further refined through our Semantic-Guided Interaction (SGI) module, establishing a reciprocal interaction between the semantic token and visual tokens. Ultimately, the reinforced semantic token serves as the representation of pedestrian identity. Our framework integrates the semantic understanding and generation capabilities of LVLM into end-to-end ReID training, allowing LVLM to capture rich semantic cues during both training and inference. LVLM-ReID achieves competitive results on multiple benchmarks without additional image-text annotations, demonstrating the potential of LVLM-generated semantics to advance person ReID.

title: Resonate-and-Fire Neurons Meet EMG: Enhancing Gesture Classification with Spiking Neural Networks
authors: Davide Manna, Trevor Bihl, Gaetano Di Caterina
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Surface Electromyography (EMG) is widely used in rehabilitation, healthcare, and robotics, where low-power solutions are essential. However, EMG signal classification often demands significant computational resources. This paper introduces a novel, low-power approach using Resonate-and-Fire (RF) neurons combined with Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs). SNNs offer efficient, low-latency classification via neuromorphic (NM) hardware, and RF neurons serve as an encoding layer, enabling end-to-end NM processing. This encoding transforms EMG data into spike-frequency representations, emphasising signal relevance in the frequency domain. The proposed method is evaluated on the Ninapro DB5 dataset and demonstrates superior performance compared to both conventional and other NM-based approaches, while maintaining low latency. These results highlight the potential of fully NM systems to outperform traditional methods, offering asynchronous, sparse, and energy-efficient computation for EMG classification.

title: SAM-ALE: Enhancing SAM for Low-Shot Digital Pathology Semantic Segmentation Via an Auxiliary Lightweight Encoder
authors: Yuli Qian, Mingjie Li, Minghui Wu, Kun Yan, Ping Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The Segment Anything Model (SAM) has been introduced for universal segmentation in natural images but struggles with medical image segmentation due to a significant domain gap. While various medical SAM variants have been developed to adapt SAM to medical datasets, we observe that they still require a large amount of labeled data to perform effectively in digital pathology semantic segmentation, where annotations are particularly costly and time-consuming. To address this challenge, we propose SAM-ALE, a novel approach that enhances SAM for Low-shot digital pathology semantic segmentation via an Auxiliary Lightweight Encoder. Our approach leverages a small amount of labeled data and a large pool of unlabeled data to train a task-specific lightweight encoder, which captures pathology-specific visual features. The encoded features are then integrated with SAM’s embeddings to improve segmentation performance with minimal labeled supervision. Experiments on the CRAG and MoNuSeg datasets validate our approach. For example, with just 2% labeled data, our method enables SAM to achieve performance (86.52% Dice) comparable to its fully fine-tuned counterpart (87.37% Dice) on CRAG, utilizing a lightweight encoder with only 7.7M parameters. Additionally, our approach allows seamless integration with various medical SAM variants. The code and data are released at https://github.com/qianyuli123/SAM-ALE.

title: A Noval Monte Carlo Gradient Method Based on Meta-Learning for Effective Step-Size Selection in Active Noise Control
authors: Luyuan Li, Jisheng Bai, Xiruo Su, Xiaoyi Shen, Dongyuan Shi, Woon-seng Gan
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Active noise control (ANC) is an effective approach to noise suppression, and the filtered-reference least mean square (FxLMS) algorithm is a widely adopted method in ANC systems, owing to its computational efficiency and stable performance. However, its convergence speed and noise reduction performance are highly dependent on the step size parameter. Common step-size algorithms—such as normalized and variable step-size variants—require additional computational resources and exhibit limited adaptability under varying environmental conditions. To address this challenge, a novel Monte Carlo gradient meta-learning (MCGM) approach is proposed herein to determine an appropriate step size, into which a forgetting factor is incorporated to mitigate the impact of initial zero effect. Compared to other algorithms, the proposed method imposes no additional computational burden on FxLMS operations. Numerical simulations involving real-world acoustic paths and noise signals further confirm its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Integrating Machine Learning AI with Green Supply Chain Practices for Enhanced Sustainable Performance
authors: S. Aneesha Bhanu, M. Krithika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Implementing artificial intelligence based on machine learning in the context of supply chain management has become one of the key factors in the development of sustainable performance. In this study, the authors investigate the connection between AIenabled practices in the supply chain and sustainability performance with a specific focus on the mediating role of green supply chain initiatives. Using a quantitative research approach, the authors collected the data on 251 professionals who represented manufacturing and service sectors. The main machine learning applications that have been discussed are predictive analytics, smart decisionmaking systems, demand forecasting, and process optimization. Findings of the rho correlation analysis of Spearman show that supply chain performance has a strong positive correlation with digital transformation and the coefficient of the correlation is 0.642 at statistically significant level. The results confirm that the artificial intelligence has a significant effect on sustainable performance during both direct and indirect influence, based on the environmentally conscious supply chain practices. On the whole, the investigation emphasizes the fact that the implementation of the machine learning technologies and the application of the eco-friendly supply chain strategies enables to improve the functioning of the organizations, reduce the number of waste, optimize the use of resources, and reinforce organizational sustainability.

title: Fine-Grained Gesture Recognition via Narrow-Kernel CNN and Attention-based SEMG-ACC Fusion
authors: Honglei Zhang, Sofiane Boudaoud, Imad Rida
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Surface electromyography (sEMG) is widely used for hand gesture recognition in rehabilitation and exoskeleton control. However, in this context, its sparse and variable nature often limits the recognition of fine-grained gestures. To address this, we propose a dual-stream narrow-kernel multimodal fusion framework that integrates raw sEMG and accelerometer (ACC) signals. Each modality is processed by a dedicated convolutional stream, and cross-modal dependencies are captured through multi-head self-attention and gated attention to enhance gesture discriminability. Extensive experiments on the public NinaPro DB2 and DB7 datasets, covering up to 49 gesture classes across multiple subjects, demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance, particularly in classifying subtle and ambiguous gestures.

title: Hybrid Algorithm of Finite State Machine State Assignment for Power Minimization
authors: Tomasz Grzes
publish: 2025 27th International Symposium on Symbolic and Numeric Algorithms for Scientific Computing (SYNASC)
abstract: The presented paper discusses a hybrid algorithm for minimizing the power consumed by the finite state machine (FSM). The proposed algorithm is based on the modification of three algorithms: the sequential algorithm, where code assignment is based on the previous encoding, state splitting, where a state is divided into several states, and using a special model of an FSM, which lead to the reduction of the length of the internal state's code. Modifications to the algorithms include using the second or third best element in the sequential algorithm, splitting the state into more than two new states, and using different lengths for state code. Every modification is tested to see if it leads to a decrease in the power in the FSM. The proposed algorithm executes methods starting from the state splitting, followed by the implementation of the common architectural model, and finally, the sequential method of state encoding. The experimental results show that the proposed method reduces the power consumption compared to NOVA, JEDI, column-based based and original sequential algorithms by at least 16%.

title: Adaptive Deterministic Flow Matching for Target Speaker Extraction
authors: Tsun-An Hsieh, Minje Kim
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Generative target speaker extraction (TSE) methods often pro-duce more natural outputs than predictive models. Recent work based on diffusion or flow matching (FM) typically relies on a small, fixed number of reverse steps with a fixed step size. We introduce Adaptive Deterministic Flow Matching TSE (AD-FlowTSE), which extracts the target speech using an adaptive step size. We formulate TSE within the FM paradigm but, unlike prior FM-based speech enhancement and TSE approaches that transport between the mixture (or a normal prior) and the clean-speech distribution, we define the flow between the background and the source, governed by the mixing ratio (MR) of the source and background that creates the mixture. This design enables MR-aware initialization, so that the model starts at an adaptive point along the background–source trajectory rather than applying the same reverse schedule across all noise levels. Experiments show that AD-FlowTSE achieves strong TSE with as few as a single step, and that incorporating auxiliary MR estimation further improves target speech accuracy. Together, these results highlight that aligning the transport path with the mixture composition and adapting the step size to noise conditions yields efficient and accurate TSE. Sound examples and source codes can be found at https://minjekim.com/research-projects/AD-FlowTSE#icassp2026.

title: Universal 3D Point Cloud Attack Using Gaussian Distribution Modeling
authors: Yuqi Bu, Daizong Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Deep learning models for 3D point clouds have shown to be vulnerable to adversarial attacks, which have received increasing attention in various safety-critical applications such as autonomous driving, robotics, and surveillance. However, existing attacks typically focus on optimizing adversarial perturbations for a certain 3D object, which often leads to overfitting and hardly remain malicious once they are transferred to attack a different 3D object. To this end, in this paper, we propose a novel Universal 3D Point Cloud Attack method to generate cross-object adversarial samples from a new distribution approximation perspective, which models the potential 3D object input distribution and adds the same distribution-fitting adversarial perturbation on different 3D objects for improving the universality. Specifically, we exploit the Laplace approximation to model the Gaussian distribution of the point cloud inputs. By sampling from this approximated distribution with Monte Carlo mechanism, we efficiently optimize and fit a single input-agnostic perturbation over diverse 3D object pairs, yielding strong universality. Extensive experiments on four 3D models and two widely used datasets demonstrate the superior performance of our proposed method.

title: Evaluating Cybersecurity Risk via Psychometric Analysis and Machine Learning
authors: Adrian Diac, Alin Stefanescu
publish: 2025 27th International Symposium on Symbolic and Numeric Algorithms for Scientific Computing (SYNASC)
abstract: In today's landscape, where cyber threats evolve on all fronts, the human factor remains a key enabler of their success. This paper presents a solution for psychological assessment and cybersecurity risk management by profiling individuals within an organization and estimating the likelihood that a phishing, ransomware, or social-engineering attack against them will succeed and propagate through the structure. The system's architecture is built as a set of microservices with distinct roles. The first component processes incoming text and extracts five relevant psychometric dimensions: awareness, conscientiousness, stress, neuroticism, and risk tolerance, using a BERT-based fine-tuned model and heuristic relabeling rules. The second component functions like an HR department, storing and connecting users and organizational entities in a relational database. The third one calculates each person's composite risk via various techniques, uncovers vulnerabilities in a simulated cyberattack scenario, and parses BPMN diagrams for interoperability. All services are exposed through a graphical interface designed to deliver high-performance support for organizational cybersecurity risk management, featuring real-time simulations, scenario customization, and an intuitive user experience. Experimental evaluations demonstrate superior performance in detecting attack susceptibility and provide a replicable framework for integrating the human factor into modern cybersecurity risk strategies.

title: Lend a Hand: Semi Training-Free Cued Speech Recognition via MLLM-Driven Hand Modeling for Barrier-Free Communication
authors: Guanjie Huang, Danny H.K. Tsang, Xiao-Ping Zhang, Li Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Cued Speech (CS) is an innovative visual communication system that integrates lip reading with hand coding, designed to enhance effective communication for individuals with hearing impairments. Automatic CS Recognition (ACSR) refers to the AI-driven process of automatically recognizing hand gestures and lip movements in CS, converting them into text. Previous work was hindered by the scarcity of data, which prevented the full training of the hand-lip modeling and fusion modules, resulting in unsatisfactory performance that significantly limited ACSR’s effectiveness. To address this issue, we have innovatively explored the capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in recognizing hand codings in CS and propose a new Semi Training-Free paradigm for ACSR, named STF-ACSR. More precisely, we first design the CS Prompt Module (CSPM), which enables zero-shot recognition of hand movements through a training-free keyframe filtering mechanism and customized prompt engineering with MLLMs. Second, we introduce the Minimalist Fusion Module (MFM) to seamlessly integrate the recognition results into the lip reading model, effectively achieving superior recognition results. Extensive experiments have demonstrated that STF-ACSR significantly outperforms previous methods on both normal-hearing and hearing-impaired data. Implementations and more experimental results are available at: https://github.com/DennisHgj/STF_ACSR.

title: OpenHier: An Open-Vocabulary Hierarchical Image Classification Framework
authors: Pu Yang, Dongjing Miao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Open-vocabulary image classification can recognize arbitrary textual categories but often fails to capture hierarchical relationships, for example, a Ragdoll cat should also be recognized as both cat and animal. Therefore, we introduce OpenHier, a comprehensive framework for open-vocabulary hierarchical classification that encompasses a hierarchical structure, a dataset, a benchmark, and a model. To capture hierarchical relationships, we construct a systematic real-world hierarchical structure based on the linguistic lexical database WordNet and Vision-Language Large Model. Building upon this structure, we develop a large-scale dataset with 4M annotated images, as well as a benchmark with 50K annotated images. Meanwhile, we design an evaluation pipeline to assess open-vocabulary hierarchical classification performance using several curated metrics. Furthermore, we propose a hierarchical consistency constraint method and multimodal alignment strategy to build a hierarchical classification model. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that our model achieves superior performance on both multi-label image classification and hierarchical image classification tasks in the open-vocabulary setting.

title: XAI-Pruner: Explainability-Driven Pruning of CNN and Transformer
authors: Di Niu, Hengyue Pan, Jingfei Jiang, Jinwei Xu, Xin Niu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Pruning, a well-established model compression technique, has been extensively studied and widely adopted in practice. Previous studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of attribution methods in Explainable AI (XAI) for quantifying the contributions of individual network components. Motivated by this, we propose XAI-Pruner, an attribution-driven pruning framework built upon Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP). To mitigate the bias in relevance allocation when applying LRP to modern deep networks with residual connections, we introduce Relevance Gating, ensuring that only residual-path relevance is propagated. We further design Relevance Filtering to enforce sparsity and alleviate numerical instability across layers. Together, these two modules greatly simplify relevance propagation for pruning, reducing reliance on layer-specific ad-hoc treatments and improving stability. With relevance scores aligned on a unified scale, we also propose Global Structure-Aware Pruning to jointly optimize pruning rates across heterogeneous groups of prunable components. Extensive experiments on DeiT and ResNet demonstrate that XAI-Pruner achieves substantial compression while preserving accuracy. The code is available at https://github.com/Gyuuteki/XAI-Pruner.

title: Anisotropic Tensor Deconvolution of Hyperspectral Images
authors: Xinjue Wang, Xiuheng Wang, Esa Ollila, Sergiy A. Vorobyov
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) deconvolution is a challenging illposed inverse problem, made difficult by the data’s high dimensionality. We propose a parameter-parsimonious framework based on a low-rank Canonical Polyadic Decomposition (CPD) of the entire latent HSI ${\mathcal{X}} \in {{\mathbb{R}}^P}^{ \times Q \times N}$. This approach recasts the problem from recovering a large-scale image with PQN variables to estimating the CPD factors with (P +Q+N)R variables. This model also enables a structure-aware, anisotropic Total Variation (TV) regularization applied only to the spatial factors, preserving the smooth spectral signatures. An efficient algorithm based on the Proximal Alternating Linearized Minimization (PALM) framework is developed to solve the resulting non-convex optimization problem. Experiments confirm the model’s efficiency, showing a numerous parameter reduction of over two orders of magnitude and a compelling trade-off between model compactness and reconstruction accuracy.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (AI) Powered Deep Learning Model for Interpretable Retinal Fundus Diagnosis using Biomedical Images
authors: R Sabitha, M Arun Manicka Raja, K Manikandan, Bharani B R, K Shilpa, Pradha V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Image-based diagnosis of Retinal disease using fundus images with automated procedures is very important in early diagnosis of diseases like Diabetic Retinopathy (DR), Age-related Macular Degeneration (AMD), and Glaucoma. The majority of deep learning models, however, are not interpretable, restricting their credibility and clinical implementation. In this paper, a new Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) driven deep learning model, which combines ResNet50 and Vision Transformer (ViT) layers, is proposed to achieve and diagnose retinal diseases in an accurate and interpretable way. The model uses pixel-based explanation methods like Grad-CAM, SHAP and Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) to produce clinically significant visual explanations of the predictions. The images of the eye with the EyePACS and APTOS datasets were preprocessed and segmented into areas of interest in the anatomy and trained with a hybrid loss function that is enhanced with XAI. The metrics of evaluation have shown that the proposed model has a better classification accuracy of 96.8, F1-score of 96.2 and AUC of 98.1, which is better than a number of baseline CNN and transformer architectures. Also, the model achieves a high level of compliance between attention heatmaps and expert-labeled retinal lesions with a Trust Score of 84.9 %. This publication contributes to the creation of credible AI systems in ophthalmology through its ability to provide both predictive and visual interpretability, and thus be able to be deployed to real-world screening, telemedicine, and clinical decision support settings.

title: Districache: Distributed Parallelism for Accelerating Diffusion Models
authors: Huaqin Luo, Zijian Zhou, Laizhong Cui, Zhongxing Ming
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Diffusion models have achieved great success in the field of image synthesis due to their remarkable generative capabilities. However, enormous computational costs resulting in slow inference speed remains a major obstacle to generating images with diffusion models, especially when generating high-resolution images. To overcome this dilemma, we propose DistriCache, a training-free method for accelerating diffusion models. Extensive experiments show that our method effectively reduces denoising computations by at least 80% while preserving the competitive image quality. Our method achieves a 5.71× speedup on eight RTX 3090 GPUs for 1024×1024 image synthesis and demonstrates even greater scalability at higher resolutions, reaching a 20.50× speedup on eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs for 3840×3840 image generation. The code is available at https://github.com/LHQUer/DistriCache.

title: Direction-Aware Cross-Modal Fusion Network with Hierarchical Feature Reconstruction for RGB-T Salient Object Detection
authors: Tianyu Zang, Zikai Gao, Minne Li, Yunrui Guo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: RGB-Thermal (RGBT) salient object detection demonstrates superior robustness in complex environments by integrating visible and thermal infrared information. However, existing methods exhibit deficiencies in handling directional structural information, cross-modal edge preservation, and multi-granularity feature coordination. This paper proposes a Direction-aware Cross-modal Fusion Network (DCFNet) that addresses these challenges through hierarchical feature reconstruction. Specifically, we design three complementary modules. First, the Adaptive Information Enhancement (AIE) module leverages bidirectional spatial decomposition to capture horizontal and vertical structural features. Second, the Bi-directional Edge Adapter (BEA), which preserves cross-modal edge fidelity through differential encoding and bidirectional transfer mechanisms. Finally, we present the Channel-Spatial-Pixel Aggregation (CSPA) module that implements a hierarchical attention cascade from global semantic understanding to fine-grained details. Extensive experiments on VT5000, VT1000, and VT821 datasets demonstrate that DCFNet achieves state-of-the-art performance.

title: Quality Assessment of Noisy and Enhanced Speech with Limited Data: UWB-NTIS System for Voicemos 2024
authors: Marie Kunešová, Aleš Pražák, Jan Lehečka
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We present a system for non-intrusive prediction of speech quality in noisy and enhanced speech, developed for Track 3 of the Voice-MOS 2024 Challenge. The task required estimating the ITU-T P.835 metrics SIG, BAK, and OVRL without reference signals and with only 100 subjectively labeled utterances for training. Our approach uses wav2vec 2.0 with a two-stage transfer learning strategy: initial fine-tuning on automatically labeled noisy data, followed by adaptation to the challenge data. The system achieved the best performance on BAK prediction (LCC = 0.867) and a very close second place in OVRL (LCC = 0.711) in the official evaluation. Post-challenge experiments show that adding artificially degraded data to the first fine-tuning stage substantially improves SIG prediction, raising correlation with ground truth scores from 0.207 to 0.516. These results demonstrate that transfer learning with targeted data generation is effective for predicting P.835 scores under severe data constraints.

title: The Influence of Speed on the Generation of Airborne Wear Particles During Braking
authors: Wojciech Tarasiuk, Michał Kalinowski, Mariusz Liszewski, Tomasz Węgrzyn, Bozena Szczucka-Lasota, Marek Ożarowski
publish: 2026 25th International Symposium INFOTEH-JAHORINA (INFOTEH)
abstract: This study investigated the effect of braking speed on the intensity of wear particle generation in the braking system. The study used a TRW GDB1621DTE brake pad on a 60-millimeter-diameter steel disc to simulate a vehicle brake disc. Measurements were taken with an Rtec MFT-5000 tribometer in a chamber that isolated the friction zone from external conditions. The chemical composition of the sample was analyzed, and the roughness of the counter-sample (steel disc) was measured. The study examined how three speed levels (5, 7, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>) affected the coefficient of friction, temperature near the contact point, and number of wear particles generated under a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ N}$</tex> load, equivalent to moderately intense passenger car breaking. The results showed an increasing trend in particle emission with increasing speed. These results have significant implications for evaluating the durability and environmental impact of friction materials used in automotive braking systems, as well as for developing methods to minimize wear particle emissions into the atmosphere.

title: Dual-Granularity Intracranial Hemorrhage Detection Based on Multiple Instance Learning
authors: Haolin Li, Yiming Shi, Jiandong Gao, Ji Wu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multiple instance learning (MIL) is a weakly supervised paradigm widely used in medical image analysis such as intracranial hemorrhage (ICH) detection, where 3D CT scans and 2D slices are considered as bags and instances respectively. Despite excellent results, most MIL models focus primarily on patient-level objectives and neglect slice-level cues, producing suboptimal slice-level performance. In this work, we propose Dual-Granularity MIL (DG-MIL) for ICH detection, which builds upon the state-of-the-art Attention-based MIL architecture with two novel components: (i) Confident Set Selection (CSS) module that heuristically identifies high-confidence slices to provide slice-level supervision in a bootstrap fashion, and (ii) Salient Slices Masking (SSM) module, which randomly masks highly attended slices to mitigate attention over-concentration and promote more generalizable feature representations. Trained solely with patient-level labels, DG-MIL improves ICH detection performance at both granularity, achieving an AUC of 0.969 and 0.887 at patient and slice level respectively, outperforming the ABMIL baseline by 0.5% and 4.9%. Code is available at https://github.com/Alanthu/DG-MIL.

title: 3DPCNet: Pose Canonicalization for Robust Viewpoint-Invariant 3D Kinematic Analysis from Monocular RGB Cameras
authors: Tharindu Ekanayake, Constantino Álvarez Casado, Miguel Bordallo López
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Monocular 3D pose estimators produce camera-centered skeletons, creating view-dependent kinematic signals that complicate comparative analysis in applications such as health and sports science. We present 3DPCNet, a compact, estimator-agnostic module that operates directly on 3D joint coordinates to rectify any input pose into a consistent, body-centered canonical frame. Its hybrid encoder fuses local skeletal features from a graph convolutional network with global context from a transformer via a gated cross-attention mechanism. From this representation, the model predicts a continuous 6D rotation that is mapped to an SO(3) matrix to align the pose. We train the model in a self-supervised manner on the MM-Fi dataset using synthetically rotated poses, guided by a composite loss ensuring both accurate rotation and pose reconstruction. On MM-Fi, 3DPCNet reduces the mean rotation error from over 20° to 3.4° and the Mean Per Joint Position Error from ∼64 mm to 47 mm compared to a geometric baseline. On TotalCapture database, fine-tuned with calibrated multi-view viewpoint variation, 3DPCNet reaches 0.3–1.3° rotation error and 40–44 mm MPJPE on held-out viewpoints and motions. Qualitative cross-modal evaluation on TotalCapture further shows that accelerations derived from canonicalized video poses correspond well to wrist IMU signals, supporting physically plausible motion analysis under viewpoint changes.

title: Prototyping of a Customised Automated Water Sampling System for Quality Monitoring
authors: José Gonçalves, J. A. B. Coelho, Mariano Alvarez, Laiany Brancalião, Paulo Matos, João Paulo Coelho
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: This paper presents the development of an automated water sampling system designed to enhance quality control in water treatment facilities. The system is built around a mechanical structure that houses a watertight box containing all electronic components. A display inside the box allows users to program sampling schedules, including parameters such as the day, time, number of samples, sample volume, and intervals between samples. A balance integrated into the structure holds a bottle that collects the water samples, while a reservoir at the bottom accumulates water to ensure an adequate supply. A water pump connected to the structure enables controlled sample collection. The design ensures that all components are compactly integrated, while a non-invasive method is used to measure the volume of the sampled water, thereby avoiding direct contact between the sensors and the sample. A Project-Based Learning (PBL) approach, coupled with direct industry collaboration, has reinforced the effectiveness of active learning methodologies in engineering education.

title: Flexi-LoRA with Input-Adaptive Ranks: Efficient Finetuning for Speech and Reasoning Tasks
authors: Zongqian Li, Yixuan Su, Han Zhou, Zihao Fu, Nigel Collier
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods like Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) have become essential for deploying large language models, yet their static parameter allocation remains suboptimal for inputs of varying complexity. We present Flexi-LoRA, a novel framework that dynamically adjusts LoRA ranks based on input complexity during both training and inference. Through empirical analysis across question answering, mathematical reasoning, and speech tasks, we demonstrate that maintaining consistency between training and inference dynamics is important for effective adaptation, particularly for sequential reasoning tasks. Our findings reveal that input-dependent parameter allocation achieves higher performance with fewer parameters by optimally matching rank configurations to question complexity. Furthermore, task-specific dependency on rank dynamics varies, with mathematical reasoning tasks exhibiting higher dependency than QA tasks. Successful adaptation manifests not only in correctness but also in reasoning quality and instruction adherence. Flexi-LoRA consistently outperforms static LoRA while using fewer parameters, with performance gains more pronounced on tasks requiring strict reasoning chains. Our approach realizes key benefits of mixture-of-experts frameworks through a more streamlined implementation, reducing parameter redundancy while improving model capabilities. We provide comprehensive empirical studies across diverse tasks, establishing a basis for future work in input-adaptive and efficient fine-tuning approaches.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: A Dual-Path Approach to Optimizing LLMs: Entropy Constraint for Exploitation and Neural Perturbation For Exploration
authors: Dongxiang Zhang, Yiming Li, Erkun Zhang, Fangjiong Chen, Yijia Zhang, Shixun Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Optimizing Large Language Models (LLMs) requires managing the fundamental trade-off between knowledge exploitation (precision) and solution space exploration (diversity). This paper introduces a dual-path optimization framework to address this challenge by providing task-specific tuning methods. For tasks demanding high determinism, we propose Entropy-Constrained with Fine-Tuning, which incorporates an entropy regularization term into the loss function to explicitly reduce output uncertainty and strengthen the model’s precise reasoning capabilities. This method improved the accuracy of Llama3.2-1B on the MMLU bench-mark by 11.62%. Conversely, for scenarios requiring extensive exploration, we introduce Stochastic Neural Perturbation, a technique that applies transient perturbations to model weights during inference to stimulate the generation of diverse content. On the GSM8K benchmark, this approach achieved an accuracy of 50.68% on samples where the baseline model failed, significantly outperforming traditional sampling methods. Together, these complementary methods provide a targeted toolkit for optimizing LLMs, enhancing precision for exploitation-focused tasks and fostering novel discovery for exploration-driven applications.

title: A Modified Yolo with Dual-Branch Attention for High-Accuracy Detection of Corn Seedlings in UAV Images
authors: Haiqing Gou, Di Su, Jianjun Du, Xiaoze Jia, Yanyan Su, Yiyang Li, Shengze Yang, Shenglian Lu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: To address low detection accuracy and missed small targets of corn seedlings under complex seed-field conditions, we propose MS-YOLO, an improved model built upon YOLOv11. The model performance is enhanced through the integration of the PFC3k2 and DFC2PSA modules, together with an additional small-object detection head. Experimental results on the MS906 maize seedling dataset show that MS-YOLO achieves a 0.7 percentage-point improvement in mAP@50 compared with YOLOv11s. On the VisDrone2019 small object dataset, MS-YOLO outperforms YOLOv11s and YOLOv12s by 3.6 and 2.8 percentage points in mAP@50, respectively, demonstrating its effectiveness for small-object detection in UAV imagery.

title: Stage-Aware Event-Conditioned Transformers for Real-Time Odor Recognition with Electronic Noses
authors: Aayush Bhansali, Aditya Nigam
publish: 2026 National Conference on Communications (NCC)
abstract: Electronic noses (e-noses) that infer odor identity from gas-sensor arrays could enable low-cost monitoring in food safety, healthcare, and environmental sensing. Yet most existing systems ignore the strongly phased Baseline-ExposurePurge (BEP) dynamics and the baseline drift that dominate real deployments, making simple magnitude-based features brittle and limiting the gains from off-the-shelf sequence models. In this work, we develop a compact 13-channel MEMS electronic nose and introduce MEMS-Odor-32, a 32-odor dataset collected over five days (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6 0}$</tex> trials, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{2 5 M}$</tex> data points), capturing realistic inter-day variability in temperature, humidity, and sensor drift. Building on this hardware platform and open dataset, we present SmellFormer, a Stage-Aware Event-Conditioned Transformer that integrates dual-path baseline and delta encodings, event-relative temporal features, FiLM-style baseline conditioning, phasedependent gating, and kinetic summary tokens. On MEMS-Odor32, SmellFormer reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 0}$</tex> % Top-1 accuracy and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 6 9 8}$</tex> macroF1, outperforming MLP, CNN, LSTM, and vanilla transformer baselines. Ablation experiments show that phase-aware gating and kinetic tokens are particularly important for robustness. Beyond raw accuracy, SmellFormer yields chemically meaningful odor embeddings that cluster according to VOC families, suggesting a useful representation for downstream retrieval and few-shot learning.

title: Timbre-Based Pretraining with Pseudo-Labels for Multi-Instrument Automatic Music Transcription
authors: Rin Sato, Keitaro Tanaka, Shigeo Morishima
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper presents a timbre-based pretraining framework for multi-instrument automatic music transcription (AMT). While existing AMT methods achieve high accuracy for single-instrument settings, multi-instrument AMT remains challenging due to severe data imbalance and dependence on instrument-specific labels, which hinder balanced transcription across diverse instruments. To address this, we introduce a timbre-based pretraining framework that learns timbre representations independent of instrument labels. Specifically, a variational autoencoder learns a latent timbre space capturing fundamental harmonic characteristics of instruments. Pseudo-labeled audio is generated by rendering MIDI scores with timbre representations clustered in this space, allowing explicit control over which timbral similarities or differences the model should learn. Fine-tuning on instrument-labeled datasets enables the model to specialize to specific instruments while retaining generalized timbre representations. Experimental results show that our approach improves transcription performance for a wide range of instruments, particularly enhancing balance in multi-instrument settings. The code is available at https://github.com/stearicacid/timbre-based-pretraining.

title: Atomic Norm Minimization Revisited: Progressive Atom Identification And Refinement
authors: Xiaozhi Liu, Jinjiang Wei, Yong Xia
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Atomic norm minimization (ANM) is a key approach for line spectral estimation (LSE). Most related algorithms formulate ANM as a semidefinite programming (SDP), which incurs high computational cost. In this paper, we revisit the ANM problem and present a novel limit-based formulation, which dissects the essential components of the semidefinite characterization of ANM. Our new formulation does not depend on SDP and can be extended to handle more general atomic sets beyond mixture of complex sinusoids. Furthermore, we reveal the connection between ANM and Bayesian LSE approaches, bridging the gap between these two methodologies. Based on this new formulation, we propose a low-complexity algorithm called Progressive Atom Identification and Refinement (PAIR) for ANM. Simulation results demonstrate that PAIR achieves superior estimation accuracy and computational efficiency compared to other state-of-the-art methods.

title: Non-Bayesian Social Learning for Modeling Interacting Large Language Model Agents
authors: Zoya Firsova, Malek Khammassi, Mert Kayaalp, Ali H. Sayed
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Non-Bayesian social learning (NBSL) is a framework in distributed inference that describes how agents in a network combine local observations and their neighbors’ beliefs to infer a hidden state. While the framework has been extensively analyzed in theory, its role as a model of belief formation in realistic multi-agent systems remains under-explored. In this work, we investigate whether NBSL can capture the belief dynamics of interacting large language model (LLM) agents. We design controlled experiments in which LLM agents revise their beliefs sequentially based on local evidence and exchanges with their neighbors. Then, we compare their belief trajectories to those predicted by NBSL. Our results provide the first empirical evaluations of NBSL on modern AI collectives, and show that LLM networks can exhibit belief evolution patterns that closely follow NBSL dynamics.

title: Adversarial Fine-Tuning on Speech Foundation Model with Vulnerable Attention Consistency Regularization for Robust Speech Recognition
authors: Yanyun Wang, Baoyuan Wu, Li Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Speech foundation models (SFMs)-based automatic speech recognition (ASR) has been recently proven vulnerable to adversarial attacks. However, existing defenses designed for traditional ASR systems are either insufficient in effectiveness or unsuitable for SFMs. Specifically, detection-based methods fail to recover accurate transcriptions; preprocessing-based defenses introduce additional computational overhead and latency at inference time; and training-based defenses degrade the utility of pre-trained models, the core competitiveness of SFMs. In this work, for the first time, we study how to fine-tune Transformer-based SFMs for adversarially robust ASR, especially preserving utility while improving robustness. Specifically, we reveal that adversarial vulnerability concentrates in the cross-attention to encoder within early decoder layers, and suggest regularizing weights and outputs of such attentions under adversarial waveforms respectively as under clean and Gaussian inputs. By leveraging these two novel regularizations, namely attention divergence and feature similarity, we propose Vulnerable Attention consIstency Regularization (VAIR), offering an effective path to fine-tuning SFMs for ASR with state-of-the-art (SOTA) adversarial robustness and slight utility degradation.

title: ICPO: Illocution-Calibrated Policy Optimization for Multi-Turn Conversation
authors: Zhebo Wang, Xiaohu Mu, Zijie Zhou, Mohan Li, Wenpeng Xing, Dezhang Kong, Meng Han
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) in multi-turn conversations often suffer from a "lost-in-conversation" phenomenon, where they struggle to recover from early incorrect assumptions, particularly when users provide ambiguous initial instructions. We find that standard post-training techniques like Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) exacerbate this issue by rewarding confident, direct answers, thereby inducing overconfidence and discouraging the model from seeking clarification. To address this, we propose Illocution-Calibrated Policy Optimization (ICPO), a novel training framework that sensitizes the model to instruction ambiguity. ICPO augments the training corpus with underspecified prompts and conditions the reward signal on the user’s illocutionary intent, rewarding the model for expressing uncertainty or asking for clarification when faced with ambiguity. Experiments demonstrate that ICPO fosters appropriate humility, yielding a substantial average improvement of 75% in multi-turn conversation, while preserving robust performance on single-turn benchmarks. Our work presents a practical path toward more robust and collaborative conversational AI that can better navigate the nuances of human interaction.

title: A Lightweight Semantic Segmentation System for 3D Medical Image
authors: Xiaofeng Wu, Yongjiang Xue, Fei Qiao, Qingzeng Song
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: To tackle the slow speed and high energy consumption of semantic segmentation models for 3D medical images, we propose a lightweight system based on FPGA. We design a modular FPGA architecture to speed up the UNETR model’s inference process. For better hardware compatibility, we enhanced the Patch embedding layer with data reordering and linear layer mapping. We accelerated convolution calculations by integrating a spatial dimension decomposition strategy with a multiply-add tree structure, and used systolic array to speed up matrix multiplication in the encoder. The proposed accelerator achieves a high performance-to-power ratio on the Virtex UltraScale+ VU9P platform, boosting energy efficiency by 9 times over CPU and 2.5 times over GPU solutions. The system reduces model complexity by 67%, with a minimal 2.4% decrease in Dice coefficient, maintaining clinical accuracy. It offers a high-precision, low-latency intelligent segmentation solution for medical applications like surgical navigation.

title: Graph-Guided Contrastive Learning for Incomplete Multi-View Clustering with Consistent Global Graph
authors: Zhepeng Wang, Zhenghao Zhang, Tianyu Zong, Feng Chen, Jun Xie
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are widely used in Incomplete Multi-View Clustering (IMVC) for their exceptional graph learning and attribute aggregation capabilities. However, most GNN-based IMVC methods construct adjacency graphs directly from raw data, making them susceptible to view-specific noise which degrades views fusion quality. To overcome this, we propose Consistent Global Graph Learning (CGGL), designed to learn a purer, more consistent global adjacency graph by filtering out view-specific noise. Furthermore, we introduce Graph-guided Cross-view Contrastive Learning (GraphCC) to promote more compact and discriminative clustering structures in the fused features. We integrate CGGL and GraphCC into a unified framework, termed Graph-Guided Contrastive Learning for Incomplete Multi-view Clustering with Consistent Global Graph (GCIMCG). Extensive experiments on four mainstream datasets demonstrate GCIMCG’s superior performance against seven state-of-the-art methods.

title: SCFusion: Semantic and Contextual Fusion for Document-Level Event Argument Extraction
authors: Rong Wang, Zhicheng Wu, Fan Gao, Qingqiang Wu, Meihong Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Document-level event argument extraction (EAE) aims to identify arguments given a trigger across an entire document. Real-world texts often contain multiple interrelated events, yet most existing methods focus on single-event modeling or local trigger-role interactions, making them prone to role confusion and unable to capture cross-event dependencies. To overcome these limitations, we propose a unified framework with a multi-event prompt mechanism that jointly processes multiple events, enabling better inter-event correlation modeling. We further design two complementary modules: the Semantic Understanding Enhancement (SUE) module, which aligns argument slots with relevant tokens to reduce ambiguity, and the Context Information Enhancement (CIE) module, which aggregates cross-sentence and cross-event signals for document-level reasoning. A probability-based dynamic fusion mechanism adaptively balances SUE’s semantic precision with CIE’s contextual robustness. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate significant improvements over strong baselines, validating the effectiveness of our approach.

title: Enhancing Cross-View Geo-Localization Generalization Via Global-Local Consistency and Geometric Equivariance
authors: Xiaowei Wang, Ke Li, Di Wang, Yifeng Wang, Chengjian Wang, Libin Sun, Zhihong Wu, Yiming Zhang, Quan Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Cross-view geo-localization (CVGL) aims to match images of the same location captured from drastically different viewpoints. Despite recent progress, existing methods still face two key challenges: (1) achieving robustness under severe appearance variations induced by diverse UAV orientations and fields of view, which hinders cross-domain generalization, and (2) establishing reliable correspondences that capture both global scene-level semantics and fine-grained local details. In this paper, we propose EGS, a novel CVGL framework designed to enhance cross-domain generalization. Specifically, we introduce an E(2)-Steerable CNN encoder to extract stable and reliable features under rotation and viewpoint shifts. Furthermore, we construct a graph with a virtual super-node that connects to all local nodes, enabling global semantics to be aggregated and redistributed to local regions, thereby enforcing global–local consistency. Extensive experiments on the University-1652 and SUES-200 benchmarks demonstrate that EGS consistently achieves substantial performance gains and establishes a new state of the art in cross-domain CVGL.

title: Development of a Digital Twin for Spacecraft Proximity Operations
authors: Martina Ferrauto, Matteo Melchiorre, Laura Salamina, Erica Scantamburlo, Davide Sorli, Stefano Mauro
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: Autonomous rendezvous and capture are key capabilities for future on-orbit servicing (OOS) missions. Two main strategies are typically employed: berthing, where a robotic manipulator seizes the target, and docking, often implemented through probe-and-drogue mechanisms. While both approaches are well established in spaceflight operations, their detailed modeling and systematic comparison remain open research challenges. This paper introduces a dedicated multibody framework developed within the MUSAPOEM project to analyze and contrast berthing and docking operations. The berthing model comprises a servicer spacecraft, a non-cooperative target, and a 7-DOF robotic manipulator equipped with a gripper-type end effector. In the proposed approach, detailed CAD-based geometries are decomposed into convex subcomponents, enabling the accurate simulation of probe insertion, compliant latching, and nonlinear contact force exchanges within Simscape Multibody. A comparative analysis highlights the complementary nature of the two approaches. Berthing offers adaptability and robustness when dealing with unprepared or uncooperative targets, whereas docking provides a compact, autonomous solution optimized for cooperative missions with standardized interfaces. Although the docking results are currently limited to model development, the framework establishes a solid basis for extending the analysis to hard docking and for integrating control strategies. In this way, the work contributes to advancing high-fidelity simulation tools that support the design and validation of next-generation OOS missions.

title: QuChaTeR: A Hybrid Quantum-Chaotic Temporal Framework for Earthquake Prediction
authors: Emir Kaan Özdemir
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Seismic prediction remains challenging due to the highly nonlinear and chaotic dynamics of earthquake signals. While classical deep learning models such as LSTMs and CNNs capture local temporal features, and quantum models offer richer state representations, their integration with chaos-driven mechanisms remains underexplored. We introduce QuChaTeR, a hybrid architecture that combines wavelet-based preprocessing, chaotic maps, and variational quantum circuits with recurrent structures to enhance temporal feature extraction. Implemented in PyTorch and PennyLane, QuChaTeR is benchmarked against classical (LSTM, GRU, RNN, 1D-CNN, Reservoir Computing) and hybrid quantum-classical (Quantum LSTM) baselines. On a real-world seismic dataset, QuChaTeR demonstrates more stable convergence and superior performance across multiple evaluation criteria. Despite promising results, scalability and quantum hardware limitations remain challenges. Overall, this work demonstrates how quantum-chaotic hybridization provides a practical pathway toward more accurate and robust earthquake event prediction.

title: Regfuse: Registration Meets Infrared and Visible Image Fusion
authors: Chongke Bi, Chaoqun Sima, Lu Yang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The goal of infrared and visible image fusion is to generate fused images better suited for human perception and computer vision tasks. Existing registration-based fusion methods primarily focus on global alignment, while neglecting enhanced registration of critical regions and offering limited support for downstream vision tasks. To address these issues, we propose RegFuse, a unified framework that jointly optimizes image registration and fusion. On the one hand, a registration network is introduced to effectively handle misalignment; on the other hand, a detection network provides supervision during training, thereby significantly improving the alignment and fusion quality of key regions, while enhancing the adaptability and robustness of the fused images in downstream vision tasks. The experimental results show that RegFuse achieves excellent fusion, detection and segmentation performance on both MSRS and RoadScene dataset, and verifies its effectiveness and application potential in unregistered scenes. The source code is available at: https://github.com/poison-pig/RegFuse

title: Viability Analysis of Clean-in-Place (CIP) System Implementation in Softgel Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Facilities
authors: Karlo Boy Carillo, Ardvin Kester Ong
publish: 2026 25th International Symposium INFOTEH-JAHORINA (INFOTEH)
abstract: Clean-in-Place (CIP) is an automated, recipedriven cleaning process that eliminates equipment disassembly, ensuring repeatability, traceability, and compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) standards. This research investigates the feasibility of implementing CIP systems in softgel pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities, addressing both operational efficiency and economic sustainability. A single-case study design was employed, focusing on the compounding process of a softgel plant. Discrete-event simulation (DES) modeled two scenarios-manual cleaning and CIP-under identical production schedules to assess operational performance and economic outcomes. Results indicate that CIP implementation reduces production time by 39.56 %, water consumption by 77.14 %, detergent use by 72.85 %, and electricity usage by 92.30 %, while eliminating solvent use entirely. Economic analysis yielded a return on investment (ROI) of 11.12 % and a payback period of 8.99 years, suggesting CIP as a long-term strategic investment. These findings underscore CIP's potential to improve throughput, sustainability, and compliance in regulated environments. The study contributes to operational optimization literature by integrating cleaning policies into DES modeling and offers practical insights for pharmaceutical manufacturers seeking efficiency and environmental stewardship. Future research should validate these results through real-world trials and expand scope to include downstream processes and dynamic scheduling.

title: Bayesian Matrix Completion under Geometric Constraints
authors: Rohit Varma, Santosh Nannuru
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The completion of a Euclidean distance matrix (EDM) from sparse and noisy observations is a fundamental challenge in signal processing, with applications in sensor network localization, acoustic room reconstruction, molecular conformation, and manifold learning. Traditional approaches, such as rank-constrained optimization and semidefinite programming, enforce geometric constraints but often struggle under sparse or noisy conditions. This paper introduces a hierarchical Bayesian framework that places structured priors directly on the latent point set generating the EDM, naturally embedding geometric constraints. By incorporating a hierarchical prior on latent point set, the model enables automatic regularization and robust noise handling. Posterior inference is performed using a Metropolis-Hastings within Gibbs sampler to handle coupled latent point posterior. Experiments on synthetic data demonstrate improved reconstruction accuracy compared to deterministic baselines in sparse regimes.

title: Gait-Based Person Identification in the DAS System Using HOG-MAE
authors: Shang Shi, Kun Gui, Pengcheng Wang, Hongliang Ren, Jin Lu, Changqiu Yu, Quanjun Cao, Guomin Gu, Qi Xuan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This article proposes HOG-MAE, a novel self-supervised learning framework designed for efficient person identification using Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) spatiotemporal gait signals. To mitigate the high costs of manual annotation in DAS deployment, we develop a masked autoencoder that targets the reconstruction of Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) features. By reconstructing HOG features instead of raw pixels, the model prioritizes essential semantic attributes—specifically the spatial-structural geometry of strides and rhythmic frequency patterns—while maintaining inherent invariance to non-essential variations, such as background noise and signal intensity fluctuations caused by diverse footwear or walking speeds. Furthermore, leveraging the asymmetric distribution of spatial and temporal information in DAS signals, we further compress the gait semantic features, thereby achieving a lightweight model and reducing pre-training time. Experimental results demonstrate that HOG-MAE achieves a classification accuracy of 96.86% and reduces pretraining time by 22.08% compared to the standard MAE under the same epoch settings.

title: Training-Free Layered Framework for Geometry-Aware Multilingual Text Editing
authors: Zhenyu Yu, Haoran Jiang, Pei Wang, Rizwan Qureshi, Yong Xiang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We present a training-free framework for multilingual text editing in images, designed to handle complex geometric transformations while preserving layout and visual realism. The method employs a layered editing strategy that separates text from background content, enabling controllable rotation, translation, scaling, and warping in a modular manner. To enhance photometric and spatial consistency, we introduce a depth-aware composition module that adjusts brightness and contrast based on the background depth map, ensuring seamless integration with the scene. Unlike existing diffusion-based approaches, our pipeline is built entirely from pretrained components and requires no task-specific fine-tuning, making it lightweight and easily deployable. Extensive experiments on the AnyWord-3M benchmark demonstrate superior text fidelity, lower FID, and better layout preservation compared to state-of-the-art methods, particularly under large-scale and non-rigid transformations.

title: Decorrelation-Enhanced Multiband Subband Adaptive Filtering for RIR Tracking in Sound Field Control
authors: Junqing Zhang, Jingli Xie, Dongyuan Shi, Wen Zhang, Jingdong Chen, Jacob Benesty
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Room impulse responses (RIRs) play a central role in sound field control (SFC), yet the common assumption that RIRs remain time-invariant in SFC methods is often unrealistic in real-world scenarios. As a result, real-time RIR tracking becomes essential for providing timely feedback to the control algorithm and maintaining system performance under dynamic acoustic conditions. In this work, we propose a multiband structured subband adaptive filtering approach to RIR tracking, which effectively reduces the impact of input signal correlation due to colored excitation. Additionally, we introduce a decorrelation-enhanced variant to further improve convergence speed. Simulation results support the theoretical analysis, demonstrating that the proposed subband-based methods consistently achieve up to a 10 dB reduction in steady-state error compared to the time-domain NLMS algorithm, while maintaining robust performance in rapidly changing acoustic environments.

title: Enhancing Noise Robustness for Neural Speech Codecs Through Resource-Efficient Progressive Quantization Perturbation Simulation
authors: Rui-Chen Zheng, Yang Ai, Hui-Peng Du, Li-Rong Dai
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Noise robustness remains a critical challenge for deploying neural speech codecs in real-world acoustic scenarios where background noise is often inevitable. A key observation we make is that even slight input noise perturbations can cause unintended shifts in quantized codewords, thereby degrading the quality of reconstructed speech. Motivated by this finding, we propose a novel and resource- efficient training strategy to enhance the noise robustness of speech codecs by simulating such perturbations directly at the quantization level. Our approach introduces two core mechanisms: (1) a distance-weighted probabilistic top-K sampling strategy that replaces the conventional deterministic nearest-neighbor selection in residual vector quantization (RVQ); and (2) a progressive training scheme that introduces perturbations from the last to the first quantizer in a controlled manner. Crucially, our method is trained exclusively on clean speech, eliminating the need for any paired noisy-clean data.
